The Company hereby reminds investors to pay attention to the net profit attributable to owners of the parent company after deducting non-recurring gains and losses and the profitability it reflects. In 2022 and the period from January to September 2025, there were significant differences between the Company's net profit and net profit after deducting non-recurring gains and losses. This was primarily due to the fact that the two capital contributions by the Company's equity incentive platform Shanghai Yuyi to the Company in 2022 and 2025 resulted in the recognition of share-based payment expenses of RMB 18,722,600 and RMB 349,065,500, respectively, which were charged in full to management expenses in the current period as non-recurring gains and losses. The accounting treatment for these share-based payment expenses does not involve any cash outflow from the Company; while they affect net profit during the Reporting Period, they do not alter the net profit attributable to owners of the parent company after deducting non-recurring gains and losses, nor the cash flow position.

VII. Principal Financial Information and Operating Status After the Audit Cut-Off Date of the Financial Report

(I) Principal Operating Status After the Audit Cut-Off Date of the Financial Report

The audit cut-off date of the Company's financial report is September 30, 2025. Between the audit cut-off date of the financial report and the date of signing of this Prospectus, the Company's overall operations have been in good condition, and no material adverse changes have occurred in its operating environment or operating model; the Company's cooperation with customers and suppliers has remained good, with no material adverse changes; and no material changes have occurred with respect to the Company's directors, senior management and other key personnel, tax policies, and other material matters that may affect investors' judgment.

(II) Principal Financial Information After the Audit Cut-Off Date of the Financial Report

Rongcheng Certified Public Accountants conducted a review of the Company's consolidated balance sheet as of December 31, 2025, the consolidated income statement, consolidated cash flow statement, and notes to financial statements for the year 2025, and issued a Review Report (Rongcheng Yue Zi [2026] 230Z0011). Based on the reviewed financial data, the Company's principal financial position is as follows:

1. Principal Data from the Consolidated Balance Sheet

Unit: RMB 10,000

Item December 31, 2025 December 31, 2024 Change (%)
Total Assets 321,491.73 152,689.65 110.55%
Total Liabilities 60,187.97 24,732.04 143.36%
Total Owners' Equity 261,303.76 127,957.61 104.21%
Equity Attributable to Owners of the Parent Company 261,303.76 127,957.61 104.21%

As

Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (宇树科技股份有限公司)                                                                                         Prospectus

In the year 2025, the net amount of non-recurring profit and loss attributable to shareholders of the parent company was RMB -312,542,300, primarily due to the one-time recognition of share-based payment expenses of RMB 349,065,500 during the period.

VIII. Specific Listing Standard Selected by the Issuer

The Company has a special voting rights arrangement and has selected the following listing standard under Article 2.1.4, Paragraph (1) of the Shanghai Stock Exchange STAR Market Stock Listing Rules: estimated market capitalization of no less than RMB 10 billion. In June 2025, the pre-money valuation of the Company's most recent market-based equity financing round was RMB 12 billion and the post-money valuation was RMB 12.7 billion. Based on the above valuations, the Company's estimated market capitalization following this issuance satisfies the above listing standard.

IX. Important Matters Including Special Corporate Governance Arrangements of the Issuer

On May 16, 2025, the Company convened the Inaugural General Meeting and First Extraordinary General Meeting of Hangzhou Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (杭州宇树科技股份有限公司), at which a resolution was passed on the Proposal Regarding the Plan of the Joint Stock Company for Setting Up Special Voting Right Shares, establishing a special voting rights share structure.

As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, Wang Xingxing (王兴兴) directly holds 86,714,964 shares of the Company, representing 23.8216% of the Company's total share capital, making him the controlling shareholder of the Company. Under the special voting rights arrangement, the voting rights ratio of Wang Xingxing's directly held shares is 63.5457%; combined with control over the Shanghai Yuyi (上海宇翼) employee equity incentive holding platform through Hangzhou Tianze (杭州天则), the aggregate voting rights controlled by Wang Xingxing amount to 68.7816%. The specific details of the above differential voting rights arrangement are set out in Section 4 "Basic Information of the Issuer" — "VIII. Special Voting Right Shares or Similar Arrangements of the Issuer" of this Prospectus.

X. Principal Use of Proceeds and Future Development Plan

(I) Use of Proceeds

The Company intends to publicly issue no fewer than 40,446,434 new shares in this offering, representing no less than 10.00% of the total share capital after issuance. The fundraising investment projects for this offering have been reviewed and approved by the 14th Meeting of the First Board of Directors and the First Extraordinary General Meeting of 2026. After deducting issuance expenses, the proceeds will be invested in the following projects in order of priority:

Unit: RMB 10,000
No. Project Name Total Project Investment Proposed Use of Proceeds
1 Intelligent Robot Model Research and Development Project 202,245.93 202,245.93
2 Robot Body Research and Development Project 110,973.80 110,973.80
3 New Intelligent Robot Product Development Project 44,540.00 44,540.00
4 Intelligent Robot Manufacturing Base Construction Project 62,411.39 62,411.39
- Total 420,171.12 420,171.12

The above fundraising investment projects are consistent with the direction of the Company's core business development and will help the Company continue to deepen technological innovation and expand its production and operational scale.

The specific details of the use of proceeds in this offering are set out in Section 7 "Use of Proceeds and Future Development Plan" — "III. Specific Details of Fundraising Investment Projects" of this Prospectus.

(II) Future Development Plan

The Company is committed to becoming a continuously leading enterprise in the field of high-performance general-purpose robots globally. Currently, with independent research and development as its core, the Company has established a full-stack self-developed system encompassing robot bodies, core intelligent algorithms, embodied intelligence, and core components and assemblies through years of accumulation. This has resulted in a range of complete machine products represented by humanoid robots and quadruped robots, as well as core robot components and assemblies including body structures, joint modules, energy systems, computing platforms, motion control systems, perception systems, and dexterous hands. The Company also conducts product promotion and industry application around the above products. In the future, the Company will continue to invest in cutting-edge technology research and development, expand into international markets, and leverage adequate financial support and standardized corporate governance to help achieve the Company's development goals.

The specific details of the Company's future development plan are set out in Section 7 "Use of Proceeds and Future Development Plan" — "IV. Future Development Plan" of this Prospectus.

XI. Other Matters Having a Material Impact on the Issuer

Other matters having a material impact on the Issuer are set out in Section 10 "Other Important Matters" of this Prospectus.

1-1-31

Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (宇树科技股份有限公司)                                                                                         Prospectus

Section 3 Risk Factors

▶

I. Risks Related to the Issuer

(I) Risk That Technological Breakthroughs and Product Innovation Fall Short of Expectations

See "Section 2 Overview" — "I. (II) Special Risk Disclosures" of this Prospectus.

(II) Risk of Key Technology Leakage and Loss of Core Personnel

Through years of technological innovation and accumulated experience, the Company has developed a series of independently researched core technologies surrounding its high-performance general-purpose robot business and has rapidly risen to a leading position in the industry. The Company's independently developed core components and algorithm systems — including high-performance motors, reducers, motion control systems, and various sensors — have reached industry-leading levels, providing a high-performance and cost-effective body foundation for the research, development, and commercial application of general-purpose robots globally. At the same time, under the leadership of founder Wang Xingxing (王兴兴), the Company has assembled a core research and development team with a solid foundation in self-developed technologies, an entrepreneurial and innovative culture, extensive project experience, and leading R&D capabilities.

The above core technological achievements and core R&D team are key to the Company's ability to maintain its industry-leading competitiveness. As competition in the industry intensifies, the competition for talent among enterprises and regions is also becoming increasingly fierce. If the Company experiences leakage of key core technologies or loss of core R&D personnel, this will have an adverse impact on the Company's technological innovation capability, market competitiveness, and operating results.

(III) Corporate Governance Risk Arising from the Shareholders' Special Voting Rights Mechanism

See "Section 2 Overview" — "I. (II) Special Risk Disclosures" of this Prospectus.

(IV) Risk of High Revenue Growth Rate and Declining Gross Margin Level

See "Section 2 Overview" — "I. (II) Special Risk Disclosures" of this Prospectus.

(V) Risk of Rapid Growth in Inventory Scale and Inventory Write-Down Losses

At the end of each period during the reporting period, the book value of the Company's inventory was RMB 77,537,400, RMB 78,957,700, RMB 140,580,500, and RMB 278,036,600, respectively, accounting for 24.12%, 24.20%, 11.07%, and 11.14% of current assets, respectively. The amounts have been continuously increasing and the proportions are relatively high. The balance of inventory write-down provisions was RMB 6,525,000, RMB 9,710,700, RMB 16,793,700, and RMB 22,810,600, respectively, with provision ratios of 7.76%, 10.95%, 10.67%, and 7.58%, respectively. If changes occur in raw material prices, the supply chain, and the market environment, or if the unit price of the Company's principal products undergoes adverse changes due to product upgrades, supply-demand dynamics, or other factors, resulting in a significant decline in the net realizable value of relevant products in the Company's inventory, the Company will face the risk of increased inventory write-downs affecting its operating performance.

(VI) Risk of Rapid Growth in Accounts Receivable and Bad Debt Losses

At the end of each period during the reporting period, the balance of the Company's accounts receivable was RMB 7,846,700, RMB 11,067,100, RMB 21,594,800, and RMB 83,456,500, respectively, representing 6.38%, 6.95%, 5.50%, and 7.15% of revenue for the corresponding periods, respectively. During the reporting period, the Company's business grew rapidly, and the scale of accounts receivable grew accordingly. The Company's principal accounts receivable counterparties are domestic and foreign universities, well-known technology and industrial enterprises, or long-term cooperative customers, all of which are in good operating condition and have high credit standing. In the future, as the Company's business scale expands, accounts receivable may continue to increase. If the financial condition of downstream customers deteriorates, there is a risk that accounts receivable may become uncollectible, which could have an adverse impact on the Company's operating performance.

(VII) Risk of Significant New Capital Investment and Expenditures from Fundraising Investment Projects

During the implementation of the fundraising investment projects in this offering, significant amounts of depreciation, amortization, and other expenditures will be incurred, which will have a certain impact on the Company's future operating performance. Since the construction, completion, and generation of returns from the fundraising investment projects require a certain period of time and are subject to various uncertainties, if the future development trend of the industry in which the Company operates, market conditions, or other factors undergo materially adverse changes, resulting in the fundraising investment projects failing to achieve their projected returns, the additional depreciation, amortization, and period expenses arising from the fundraising investment projects will have an adverse impact on the Company's future operating performance.

(VIII) Risk of Foreign Exchange Rate Fluctuations Related to Foreign Currency Settlement of Overseas Revenue

During the reporting period, the Company's overseas principal business revenue amounted to RMB 69,352,800, RMB 87,642,400, RMB 215,707,300, and RMB 452,786,200, representing 57.21%, 55.63%, 55.70%, and 39.20% of principal business revenue, respectively. During the reporting period, the Company's exchange gains and losses amounted to RMB -5,678,200, RMB -2,981,200, RMB -4,437,700, and RMB 14,228,200, respectively. During January to September 2025, benefiting from the significant increase in domestic revenue, the proportion of overseas revenue declined somewhat; however, its sales amount and year-on-year growth rate still achieved strong growth, with overseas revenue still accounting for more than 35% of the Company's principal business revenue. The Company's overseas sales are primarily settled in US dollars. If the exchange rate of the US dollar against the Renminbi becomes more volatile in the future, or if exchange rate policies undergo significant changes, this will have a certain impact on the Company's performance.

(IX) Risk of Declining Operating Performance Under Multiple Influencing Factors

During the reporting period, the Company's operating performance achieved rapid growth. In 2024 and January to September 2025, operating revenue was RMB 392,370,600 and RMB 1,167,490,100, respectively, and the net profit attributable to shareholders of the parent company after deducting non-recurring profit and loss was RMB 77,503,600 and RMB 430,612,300, respectively. Benefiting from the accelerated development of the general-purpose robot industry, the continued breakthroughs in the performance of the Company's core products, the continuous enrichment of the product line, and the ongoing expansion of application scenarios, the Company achieved substantial growth in both operating revenue and profit levels during the reporting period. If in the future the Company is affected by factors such as slower-than-expected development of new technologies or promotion of new products, rising raw material costs, increased R&D investment intensity, and large share-based payment expenses arising from newly granted substantial equity incentives, and the Company fails to take effective measures to respond to such changes in a timely manner, the Company will face the risk of a significant decline in operating performance or even losses.

II. Industry-Related Risks

(I) Risk That Large-Scale Downstream Commercial Applications Fall Short of Expectations

See "Section 2 Overview" — "I. (II) Special Risk Disclosures" of this Prospectus.

(II) Risk of Escalating International Trade Friction and Control Policies

See "Section 2 Overview" — "I. (II) Special Risk Disclosures" of this Prospectus.

(III) Risk of Intensifying Industry Competition and Disorderly and Improper Competition

See "Section 2 Overview" — "I. (II) Special Risk Disclosures" of this Prospectus.

(IV) Risk of Social Attention Hot Topics and Market Demand Volatility

As a frontier technology sector, high-performance general-purpose robots have received extensive and intense attention from capital markets, the industry, and social media in recent years, with generally optimistic market expectations. However, the industry as a whole is still in its early stages of technological breakthroughs and commercial exploration. If progress is slow in areas such as key technology breakthroughs, cost control, or scenario applications, the current market enthusiasm and capital attention may decline significantly, causing the overall development pace of the industry to fall short of expectations and thereby having an adverse impact on the Company's market expansion and operating performance.

In the long run, the large-scale commercial application of humanoid robots needs to overcome multiple factors including technical reliability, production costs, market acceptance, and laws and regulations. If the cultivation of downstream application market demand lags, or if phased or cyclical fluctuations in demand occur, this will directly affect the industry's and the Company's capacity absorption and revenue realization. At the same time, changes in relevant global industrial policies and laws and regulations may also create uncertainty in market demand.

Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (宇树科技股份有限公司)                                                                                         Prospectus

(宇树有限, "Unitree Limited"), was established on August 26, 2016, and was converted in its entirety into Hangzhou Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (杭州宇树科技股份有限公司) as a joint stock limited company on May 28, 2025. The major changes in the Company's share capital and shareholders during the reporting period are shown in the diagram below:

June 2025: Capital reserve converted to registered capital; registered capital increased to RMB 364,017,906

(I) Establishment of the Limited Liability Company

On August 26, 2016, Unitree Limited (宇树有限) was established by Wang Xingxing with a registered capital of RMB 100,000. The shareholding structure of Unitree Limited at the time of establishment was as follows:

Unit: RMB 10,000
No. Shareholder Name Subscribed Registered Capital Shareholding Ratio
1 Wang Xingxing (王兴兴) 10.00 100.00%
Total 10.00 100.00%

After the establishment of the Company, Wang Xingxing successively paid in registered capital of RMB 30,000 in April 2018 and RMB 70,000 in December 2021. Pursuant to the then-effective Articles of Association of Unitree Limited, shareholder Wang Xingxing was required to pay in the subscribed registered capital of RMB 100,000 before August 1, 2025. The timing of Wang Xingxing's paid-in capital is consistent with the provisions of the Articles of Association.

1-1-37

(II) Establishment of the Joint Stock Company

On May 6, 2025, Unitree Limited convened a shareholders' meeting, which approved the audit and appraisal of the net assets of Unitree Limited with March 31, 2025 as the reference date for audit and appraisal, and the conversion of the entire Unitree Limited into a joint stock limited company through the conversion of audited net assets into shares.

On May 12, 2025, Rongcheng Certified Public Accountants (容诚会计师) issued Audit Report No. "Rongcheng Shen Zi [2025] 230Z3996" (容诚审字[2025]230Z3996号). Upon verification, as of the audit reference date of March 31, 2025, the net assets of Unitree Limited were RMB 1,371,618,138.76. On the same date, Zhongshui Zhiyuan (中水致远) issued Asset Appraisal Report No. "Zhongshui Zhiyuan Ping Bao Zi [2025] No. 020447" (中水致远评报字[2025]第020447号). Upon appraisal, as of the appraisal reference date of March 31, 2025, the appraised value of the net assets of Unitree Limited was RMB 1,432,959,000.

On May 15, 2025, Unitree Limited convened a shareholders' meeting, which approved the conversion of the entire Unitree Limited into Hangzhou Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (杭州宇树科技股份有限公司) as a joint stock limited company by all shareholders of Unitree Limited acting as promoters; the conversion used the audited net assets of Unitree Limited as of March 31, 2025 of RMB 1,371,618,138.76 as the basis, converted into 2,594,223 shares at a ratio of 1:0.0019, with a par value of RMB 1 per share (registered capital of RMB 2,594,223, with the registered capital remaining unchanged before and after the conversion), and the remaining RMB 1,369,023,915.76 was recorded as capital surplus of the joint stock company. On the same date, all shareholders of Unitree Limited, acting as promoters, jointly signed the Promoter Agreement.

On May 16, 2025, Unitree Technology (宇树科技) convened the Inaugural General Meeting and First Extraordinary General Meeting, at which resolutions were passed on matters including the Proposal Regarding the Conversion of the Company in Its Entirety into a Joint Stock Company, the Proposal Regarding the Work Report on the Preparation of the Joint Stock Company, and the Proposal Regarding the Formulation of the Articles of Association, and the non-employee directors and non-employee supervisors of the first Board of Directors were elected. On the same date, all promoters of the Company signed the Articles of Association.

On May 26, 2025, Rongcheng Certified Public Accountants (容诚会计师) issued Capital Verification Report No. "Rongcheng Yan Zi [2025] 230Z0052" (容诚验字[2025]230Z0052号). Upon verification, as of May 16, 2025, Hangzhou Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (in preparation) had received registered capital of RMB 2,594,223 in aggregate paid in by all shareholders, with the method of contribution being net assets, of which: RMB 2,594,223 was recorded as share capital and the remainder was recorded as capital surplus.

On May 28, 2025, Unitree Limited completed the business registration change procedures for the conversion of the entire company into a joint stock limited company, and obtained the Business License issued by the Hangzhou High-tech Industry Development Zone (Binjiang) Market Supervision and Administration Bureau with Unified Social Credit Code 91330108MA27YJ5H56.

The shareholding structure of the Company after the conversion was as follows:

The specific details of each change in share capital and shareholders during the reporting period are as follows:

1. 2022 First Capital Increase

On December 30, 2021, Unitree Limited, its original shareholders, and the new shareholders Jingqian No. 2 (经乾二号) and Hexagon (海克斯康) signed a Capital Increase Agreement, pursuant to which the registered capital of Unitree Limited was to be increased from RMB 182,635 to RMB 214,293, with the new registered capital of RMB 9,664 subscribed for by Shanghai Yuyi with cash consideration of RMB 9,664, the new registered capital of RMB 17,187 subscribed for by Jingqian No. 2 with cash consideration of RMB 71,500,000, and the new registered capital of RMB 4,807 subscribed for by Hexagon with cash consideration of RMB 20,000,000.

On December 30, 2021, Unitree Limited convened a shareholders' meeting, which reviewed and approved the above capital increase matters. On January 5, 2022, Unitree Limited completed the business registration change and filing procedures for the above capital increase.

After the completion of this capital increase, the shareholding structure of Unitree Limited was as follows:

8. Third Equity Transfer in 2024

On September 26, 2024, the equity transferor and equity transferees entered into a Share Transfer Agreement, pursuant to which Anchuang Technology (安创科技) agreed to transfer CNY 33,354 and CNY 3,706 of registered capital it held in the Company to Guanghe No. 2 (光合贰期) and Guangyue Investment (光越投资) respectively, for transfer considerations of CNY 45,000,000 and CNY 5,000,000 respectively.

On November 30, 2024, Unitree Limited convened a shareholders' meeting and approved the above equity transfer matters. On December 5, 2024, Unitree Limited completed the industrial and commercial change registration and filing in connection with the above equity transfer.

9. Establishment of the Joint Stock Company in 2025

For the conversion of Unitree Limited into a joint stock company in its entirety, please refer to "Section II, (II) Establishment of the Joint Stock Company" of this section.

11. Equity Financing and Equity Incentive Plan Expansion in June 2025

(1) Equity Financing

On June 6, 2025, Unitree Technology convened the Third Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders for 2025, which approved the proposal regarding the increase of the Company's registered capital. On the same date, Unitree Technology, its existing shareholders, and newly added shareholders — China Mobile Hecheng (中移和创), Tencent Technology (腾讯科技), Wuxi Jinqiu (无锡锦秋), Hangzhou Haoyue (杭州灏月), Shanghai Yunyang (上海云玚), and Hechuang Investment (合创投资) — entered into a Capital Increase Agreement. The basic details of this capital increase are as follows:

Also during this equity financing period, shareholder Source Code Capital (源码资本) and Wang Xingxing (王兴兴) entered into a Share Transfer Agreement on June 4, 2025, pursuant to which Source Code Capital transferred 11,378 shares of the Company to Wang Xingxing for a consideration of CNY 50,000,000.

(2) Equity Incentive Plan Expansion

On June 6, 2025, Unitree Technology convened the Third Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders for 2025, which approved the proposal regarding the capital increase by the Company's equity incentive platform Shanghai Yuyi (上海宇翼). On June 12, 2025, Unitree Technology and Shanghai Yuyi entered into a Capital Increase Agreement, pursuant to which Shanghai Yuyi subscribed for 144,668 newly issued shares of the Company at a total consideration of CNY 144,668, increasing its shareholding ratio in the Company to 10.9414%.

On June 12, 2025, Unitree Technology completed the industrial and commercial change registration and filing in connection with the above June 2025 equity financing and equity incentive plan expansion.

Upon completion of the above share changes, the equity structure of Unitree Technology was as follows:

12. Capitalization of Capital Reserves in June 2025

On June 18, 2025, Unitree Technology convened the Fourth Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders for 2025, which approved the proposal regarding the capitalization of capital reserves, agreeing to change the registered capital of Unitree Technology from CNY 2,889,031 to CNY 364,017,906 by transferring capital reserves of the joint stock company to share capital for the benefit of all shareholders.

On June 18, 2025, Unitree Technology completed the industrial and commercial change registration and filing in connection with the above capitalization of capital reserves.

The registered capital after this capitalization of capital reserves is 126 times the registered capital prior to such capitalization. Upon completion, the equity structure of Unitree Technology was as follows:

VIII. Special Voting Rights Shares or Similar Arrangements of the Issuer

As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, the Company has special voting rights shares in place. The details are as follows:

(I) Basic Information on the Special Voting Rights Arrangement

1. Shareholders' Resolution on the Special Voting Rights Arrangement

On May 16, 2025, the Company held the inaugural general meeting and the first extraordinary general meeting of the joint stock company, at which the resolution "Proposal Regarding the <Plan for the Establishment of Special Voting Rights Shares by the Joint Stock Company>" was voted upon and passed, approving the establishment of special voting rights shares. Pursuant to the special voting rights arrangement, 44,074,296 shares held by the Company's actual controller Wang Xingxing (王兴兴) are designated as Class A special voting rights shares, carrying special voting rights; all remaining shares of the Company are Class B ordinary shares, which do not carry special voting rights.

Except for matters stipulated in the Company's Articles of Association, when shareholders exercise voting rights on matters considered at the general meeting of shareholders, each special voting rights share carries 10 votes, and each ordinary share carries 1 vote.

2. Duration of the Special Voting Rights Arrangement

After the Company establishes the special voting rights arrangement, unless the general meeting of shareholders resolves to terminate the arrangement, it shall continue to subsist and operate in perpetuity in accordance with the relevant laws, regulations, and the Company's Articles of Association.

3. Holder Eligibility

Shareholders holding Class A shares shall be persons who have made significant contributions to the Company's development or business growth, and who continuously serve as directors of the Company, or holding entities actually controlled by such persons. The aggregate shareholding of shareholders holding Class A shares in the Company shall reach at least 10% of all issued shares with voting rights. The Company's controlling shareholder and actual controller Wang Xingxing (王兴兴) meets the foregoing requirements.

4. Ratio of Voting Rights of Special Voting Rights Shares to Ordinary Shares

Prior to this offering, the number of Class A shares with special voting rights is 44,074,296, held by the actual controller Wang Xingxing (王兴兴), and the remaining 319,943,610 shares are Class B ordinary shares, with details as follows:

No. Shareholder Name Share Class Number of Shares Shareholding Percentage Number of Votes Voting Rights Percentage
1 Wang Xingxing (王兴兴) Class A 44,074,296 12.1077% 440,742,960 57.9402%
Class B 42,640,668 11.7139% 42,640,668 5.6056%
Subtotal 86,714,964 23.8216% 483,383,628 63.5457%
2 Shanghai Yuyi (上海宇翼) Class B 39,828,600 10.9414% 39,828,600 5.2359%
3 Other shareholders (其他股东) Class B 237,474,342 65.2370% 237,474,342 31.2184%
Total 364,017,906 100.0000% 760,686,570 100.0000%

5. Scope of General Meeting Matters in Which the Special Voting Rights Held by the Holder May Participate in Voting

In accordance with the provisions of the Company's Articles of Association, when holders of Class A shares and Class B shares vote on all resolutions submitted to the general meeting of shareholders for voting, each Class A share carries 10 votes and each Class B share carries 1 vote. When shareholders exercise voting rights on the following matters, the number of votes per Class A share shall be equal to the number of votes per Class B share:

  1. Amendments to the Company's Articles of Association;
  2. Changes to the number of voting rights carried by special voting rights shares;
  3. Appointment or removal of independent directors;
  4. Appointment or removal of members of the audit committee;
  5. Appointment or removal of the accounting firm that issues audit opinions on the periodic reports of the listed company;
  6. Division, spin-off, merger, dissolution, and liquidation of the Company.

A resolution of the general meeting of shareholders on item (2) above shall be passed by no less than two-thirds of the voting rights held by the shareholders present at the meeting; provided, however, that this shall not apply to the conversion of a corresponding number of Class A shares into Class B shares in accordance with the Company's Articles of Association and the Shanghai Stock Exchange Science and Technology Innovation Board Stock Listing Rules.

6. Lock-up Arrangements and Transfer Restrictions for Special Voting Rights Shares

(1) No Issuance of Additional Class A Shares

After the Company's shares are listed on a domestic securities exchange, except in the case of pro-rata rights issues or capitalisation of reserves, no special voting rights shares shall be issued domestically or overseas, and the proportion of Class A shares shall not be increased. If, due to share repurchases or other reasons, the proportion of Class A shares increases, the Company shall simultaneously take measures such as converting a corresponding number of Class A shares into Class B shares to ensure that the proportion of Class A shares does not exceed the original level.

(2) Transfer Restrictions on Class A Shares

Class A shares of the Company may not be traded on the secondary market, but may be transferred in accordance with the relevant rules of the stock exchange.

(3) Conversion of Class A Shares

Upon the occurrence of any of the following circumstances, Class A shares shall be converted into Class B shares on a 1:1 basis:

  1. The shareholder holding Class A shares no longer meets the qualifications and minimum shareholding requirements stipulated in the Company's Articles of Association, or loses the relevant ability to perform their duties, resigns, or dies;
  2. The shareholder actually holding Class A shares loses actual control over the relevant holding entity;
  3. The shareholder holding Class A shares transfers the Class A shares held to another party, or entrusts another party to exercise the voting rights attached to the Class A shares;
  4. A change of control of the Company occurs.

Upon the occurrence of item (4) in the preceding paragraph, all Class A shares already issued by the Company shall be converted into Class B shares. Upon the occurrence of item (1) in the preceding paragraph, Class A shares shall be converted into Class B shares from the time the relevant circumstance occurs; the relevant shareholder shall immediately notify the Company, and the Company shall promptly disclose the specific circumstances, the time of occurrence, the number of Class A shares converted into Class B shares, the remaining number of Class A shares, and other relevant information.

(II) Relevant Risks Potentially Arising from Special Voting Rights and Their Impact on the Company

Under the special voting rights mechanism, the Company's actual controller is able to determine ordinary resolutions at the general meeting of shareholders and can also exert significant influence on special resolutions at the general meeting of shareholders, thereby further enhancing the actual controller's influence over the Company's major decisions through the general meeting of shareholders. If minority shareholders, including public investors, oppose the actual controller on significant corporate decisions and vote against such decisions at the general meeting of shareholders, they may be unable to materially affect the outcome of the vote due to the relative disparity in the number of votes per share. In special circumstances, the interests of the actual controller may not be consistent with those of the Company's other shareholders, particularly minority shareholders. The special voting rights arrangement established by the Company carries the possibility of affecting the interests of other shareholders, particularly minority shareholders.

(III) Investor Protection Measures

1. Measures to Prevent Abuse of the Special Voting Rights Mechanism

In order to prudently establish and operate the special voting rights mechanism and prevent its abuse, the Company has put in place effective constraints on special voting rights and on shareholders holding special voting rights through the following measures:

(1) Strictly Limiting the Scope of Special Voting Rights in Accordance with Law

The Company establishes special voting rights in order to enhance the stability and continuity of its own operational strategy as a science and technology innovation enterprise. The boundaries of special voting rights are clearly defined and do not apply to certain significant matters relating to fundamental rights of investors.

In accordance with the provisions of the Company's Articles of Association, when the general meeting of shareholders deliberates on specific significant matters, the number of votes per Class A share shall be equal to the number of votes per Class B share. For details, please refer to "Section VIII, (I), Item 5: Scope of General Meeting Matters in Which the Special Voting Rights Held by the Holder May Participate in Voting" of this section.

(2) Imposing Strict Reduction Restrictions on Special Voting Rights Shares

Class A shares with special voting rights are subject to stricter reduction restrictions than Class B shares, causing Class A shareholders to place greater emphasis on the long-term, stable development of the Company relative to other shareholders holding Class B shares and public investors, thereby constraining behavior that might abuse special voting rights to the detriment of the Company's interests.

In accordance with the Shanghai Stock Exchange Science and Technology Innovation Board Stock Listing Rules, Class A shares of the Company may be transferred in accordance with relevant exchange rules, but may not be traded on the secondary market. When a Class A shareholder transfers Class A shares to another party, the Class A shares shall be converted into Class B shares on a 1:1 basis.

(3) Broad, Prudent, and Independent Deliberation and Voting Procedures for Establishing Special Voting Rights

Prior to convening the extraordinary general meeting to deliberate on the proposal to establish special voting rights, all shareholders of the Company thoroughly consulted on the content and implications of the special voting rights mechanism. When the extraordinary general meeting deliberated on the proposal to establish special voting rights, the proposal was voted on by all shareholders with voting rights and received unanimous approval from all shareholders.

2. Measures and Institutional Arrangements for Protecting Investors' Rights and Interests

In addition to the above measures to prevent abuse of the special voting rights mechanism, the Company has also established the following specific systems and measures to fully protect investors' rights and interests:

(1) Emphasis on Shareholders' Right to Dividends

The Company has reviewed and passed the "Proposal Regarding the Formulation of the Company's Three-Year Shareholder Dividend Return Plan Following the Initial Public Offering and Listing on the Science and Technology Innovation Board" at the ninth meeting of the first Board of Directors and the sixth extraordinary general meeting of shareholders in 2025. When the general meeting of shareholders deliberates on specific cash dividend plans, it shall fully solicit the opinions and demands of minority shareholders, adhere to the basic principle of prioritising cash dividends, and, in the absence of significant investment plans or major cash expenditures, distribute annual cash dividends of no less than 10% of the distributable profits realised in the current period.

(2) Giving Full Play to the Supervisory Functions of Independent Directors

The Company has appointed 3 independent directors and established the System for the Work of Independent Directors, granting independent directors special powers including proposing the convening of board meetings, requesting the convening of extraordinary general meetings, and independently engaging external audit institutions and advisory institutions, and providing that they have the right to issue independent opinions on significant matters. When the general meeting of shareholders deliberates on the appointment or removal of independent directors, the number of votes per Class A share shall be equal to the number of votes per Class B share, which is conducive to strengthening the role of independent directors in representing the interests of minority shareholders and exercising independent supervision.

(3) Minority Shareholders' Right to Nominate Directors and to Convene Extraordinary Board Meetings

In accordance with Article 83 of the Articles of Association, shareholders holding or collectively holding 1% or more of the Company's shares with voting rights may propose candidates for directors to the Board of Directors, and the Board of Directors shall submit such candidates to the general meeting of shareholders for deliberation.

In accordance with Article 117 of the Articles of Association and Article 123 of the Articles of Association (Draft), shareholders representing more than 1/10 of voting rights, more than 1/3 of directors, the Chairman of the Board, the audit committee, or a majority of independent directors may propose the convening of an extraordinary board meeting. The Chairman of the Board shall convene and preside over an extraordinary board meeting within 10 days of receiving the proposal.

(4) Establishing a Series of Systems Including Standardized Related-Party Transactions

The Company has lawfully established a series of systems including the Rules of Procedure for General Meetings of Shareholders, Rules of Procedure for the Board of Directors, Related-Party Transaction Management System, Financing and External Guarantee Management System, External Investment Management System, and System for the Work of Independent Directors, so as to improve corporate governance. By placing the approval authority for larger transactions and important matters at the level of the general meeting of shareholders, the Company has formed a multi-tier decision-making mechanism comprising management decision-making, board of directors review and approval, audit committee supervision, and general meeting of shareholders review and approval, thereby preventing management from engaging in improper conduct that may harm the interests of the Company and public investors.

(5) Strengthening Information Disclosure and Investor Relations Management

The Company will strictly comply with the information disclosure regulatory requirements set forth in the Shanghai Stock Exchange Science and Technology Innovation Board Stock Listing Rules, the Measures for Continuous Supervision of Science and Technology Innovation Board Listed Companies (Trial), and the Shanghai Stock Exchange Science and Technology Innovation Board Listed Company Self-Regulatory Guidelines No. 1 — Standardized Operations. The ninth meeting of the first Board of Directors reviewed and passed the Information Disclosure Management System, which sets out clear provisions on the principles, procedures, authority, responsibilities, confidentiality measures, and contact information for information disclosure. In addition, the Company will disclose in its periodic reports the implementation and changes of the special voting rights arrangement during the reporting period, as well as the implementation of measures relating to the protection of investors' lawful rights and interests under the special voting rights arrangement.

IX. Variable Interest Entity (Contractual Control) Structure of the Issuer

The Company does not have a contractual control (VIE) structure.

(III) Top Ten Natural Person Shareholders Prior to This Offering and Their Positions at the Issuer

Prior to this offering, the Company

Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (宇树科技股份有限公司) — Prospectus

rights, priority liquidation rights, drag-along rights, and other special shareholder rights. Among these, the shareholder repurchase right constitutes a listing valuation adjustment mechanism (VAM) clause in which the Company itself is a party to the repurchase obligation.

2. Termination of Special Rights

In September 2025, the Company and its existing shareholders entered into the Termination Agreement Regarding Special Shareholder Rights Clauses of Hangzhou Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (hereinafter the "Termination Agreement"), which stipulated: with respect to the repurchase rights enjoyed by each shareholder, as well as other VAM-type clauses and/or arrangements meeting any of the following conditions: 1) the Company itself is a party to the VAM arrangement; 2) there exists an agreement that may result in a change of control of the Company; 3) the arrangement is linked to the Company's market capitalization; or 4) there exist circumstances that seriously affect the Company's ability to continue as a going concern or otherwise seriously impair investors' interests — the Termination Agreement provides that the repurchase right and similar clauses shall be thoroughly terminated and void ab initio as of the day immediately preceding the date on which the audit report for the Company's listing application is issued, and shall not be reinstated.

The Company's auditor, RSM China (容诚所), issued the audit report for this offering application on October 15, 2025. Accordingly, the shareholder repurchase right clauses were thoroughly terminated and void ab initio prior to the filing, and shall not be reinstated.

In order to treat all investors fairly and better protect the rights and interests of minority investors, the Company and its existing shareholders entered into the Supplemental Agreement to the Termination Agreement Regarding Special Shareholder Rights Clauses of Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (hereinafter the "Supplemental Agreement") in January 2026, making supplementary arrangements regarding the termination of other special shareholder rights clauses held by investor shareholders. The other special shareholder rights clauses were thoroughly terminated on the date of signing of the Supplemental Agreement and shall not be reinstated. The specific contents are as follows:

All special shareholder rights other than the repurchase right (including information and inspection rights, pre-emptive rights, rights of first refusal, co-sale rights, anti-dilution rights, dividend rights, priority liquidation rights, drag-along rights, priority investment rights, most-favored-nation clauses, special rights regarding material matters of the shareholders' meeting and board of directors, special rights of investor directors, share transfer restrictions, director appointment rights (including board observer rights), and supervisor appointment rights) were thoroughly terminated on the date of signing of the Supplemental Agreement. The termination of such clauses is permanent, unconditional, irrevocable, and non-reinstatable.

Upon review, the sponsor, the issuer's legal counsel, and the issuer's auditor are of the opinion that the repurchase rights enjoyed by the investor shareholders have been thoroughly terminated and void ab initio as of the day immediately preceding the date on which the audit report for the Company's listing application was issued (October 15, 2025), and shall not be reinstated; the other special rights clauses enjoyed by the investor shareholders were thoroughly terminated on the date of signing of the Supplemental Agreement in January 2026, and shall not be reinstated. Accordingly, the Company has effected a non-reinstatable, thorough termination of the special rights clauses of investor shareholders, which complies with the relevant regulatory requirements of the Guidelines for Application of Regulatory Rules — Issuance Category No. 4.

XI. Directors, Supervisors, Senior Management, and Core Personnel

(I) Overview of Directors, Senior Management, and Other Core Personnel

1. Members of the Board of Directors

No. Name Position Current Term of Office Nominator
1 Wang Xingxing (王兴兴) Chairman of the Board, General Manager, and Chief Technology Officer 2025.05 – 2028.05 Wang Xingxing

The specific profiles of the Company's directors are as follows:

Wang Xingxing (王兴兴): For his biography, please refer to "Section VII, (I) Basic Information of the Controlling Shareholder and Actual Controller" of this section.

Yang Zhiyu (杨知雨): Male, born in 1991, Chinese national, no permanent right of abode in a foreign country, holds a master's degree. He has been employed by the Company since October 2016 and currently serves as Director and Head of Mechanical Structure.

Chen Li (陈立): Male, born in 1990, Chinese national, no permanent right of abode in a foreign country, holds a master's degree. He has been employed by the Company since May 2018 and currently serves as Director and Head of Sales and Service System.

Zhang Yangguang (张阳光): Male, born in 1993, Chinese national, no permanent right of abode in a foreign country, holds a bachelor's degree. From July 2015 to March 2017, he served as an Algorithm Engineer at Qinu (Beijing) Technology Co., Ltd. (奇弩（北京）科技有限公司); from April 2017 to October 2017, he served as an Algorithm Engineer at Sinocare Medical Technology (Beijing) Co., Ltd. (赛诺微医疗科技（北京）有限公司); he has been employed by the Company since January 2018 and currently serves as Director and Head of Algorithms and Software.

Cui Wenhan (崔文瀚): Male, born in 1988, Chinese national, no permanent right of abode in a foreign country, holds a doctoral degree. From July 2016 to August 2019, he served as Head of Early-Stage Projects at Shanghai Ziyou Investment Management Co., Ltd. (上海自友投资管理有限公司); from August 2019 to May 2021, he served as Investment Director and President's Assistant at Ningbo Meihua Angel Investment Management Co., Ltd. (宁波梅花天使投资管理有限公司); since May 2021, he has served as Senior Vice President of CITIC Jinshi Investment Co., Ltd. (中信金石投资有限公司); he has served as a Director of the Company since January 2024.

Liang Wangnan (梁望南): Male, born in 1974, Chinese national, no permanent right of abode in a foreign country, holds a bachelor's degree. From August 1996 to October 2022, he successively served at Beijing Grain Group Co., Ltd. (北京粮食集团有限责任公司), the Beijing Municipal Party Committee's Commercial Work Committee (北京市委商贸工委), the Beijing Municipal Government State-owned Assets Supervision and Administration Commission (北京市人民政府国有资产监督管理委员会), and Beijing State-owned Capital Operation and Management Center (Beijing State-owned Capital Operation and Management Co., Ltd.) (北京国有资本经营管理中心（北京国有资本运营管理有限公司）); since October 2022, he has served as Director and General Manager of Beijing Jingguorui Equity Investment Fund Management Co., Ltd. (北京京国瑞股权投资基金管理有限公司); since February 2026, he has served as Deputy General Manager of Beijing State-owned Capital Operation and Management Co., Ltd. (北京国有资本运营管理有限公司); he has served as a Director of the Company since May 2025.

Li Zongyan (李宗彦): Male, born in 1979, Chinese national, no permanent right of abode in a foreign country, holds a doctoral degree. From August 2001 to December 2013, he served as a faculty member at Nanjing Audit University (南京审计大学); since January 2014, he has been employed at Zhejiang University of Finance and Economics (浙江财经大学), where he currently serves as Associate Dean and Professor of the School of Accounting; he has served as an Independent Director of the Company since September 2025.

Ni Chenkai (倪晨凯): Male, born in 1985, Chinese national, no permanent right of abode in a foreign country, holds a doctoral degree. From August 2014 to April 2016, he served as a Lecturer at Renmin University of China (中国人民大学); since May 2016, he has been employed at Fudan University (复旦大学), where he currently serves as a Professor at the School of Management; he has served as an Independent Director of the Company since September 2025.

Song Huasheng (宋华盛): Male, born in 1978, Chinese national, no permanent right of abode in a foreign country, holds a doctoral degree. Since April 2006, he has been employed at Zhejiang University (浙江大学), where he currently serves as a Professor at the School of Economics and Executive Director of the Zhejiang University Institute of International Economics; he has served as an Independent Director of the Company since September 2025.

2. Audit Committee

In September 2025, the Fifth Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders deliberated and approved the Articles of Association, abolishing the Board of Supervisors, with the Board of Directors' Audit Committee exercising the powers of the Board of Supervisors as stipulated in the Company Law.

The Audit Committee consists of 3 members: Li Zongyan, Ni Chenkai, and Zhang Yangguang, with Li Zongyan serving as the convener. For the biographies of the aforementioned directors, please refer to "Section XI, (I), 1, Members of the Board of Directors" of this section.

3. Senior Management

As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, the Company has 3 incumbent senior management personnel. Their basic information is as follows:

No. Name Position Current Term of Office
1 Wang Xingxing (王兴兴) Chairman of the Board, General Manager, and Chief Technology Officer 2025.05 – 2028.05
2 Wang Feng (王枫) Chief Financial Officer 2025.07 – 2028.05
3 Fu Fenghua (傅风华) Board Secretary 2025.07 – 2028.05

Wang Xingxing (王兴兴): For his biography, please refer to "Section XI, (I), 1, Members of the Board of Directors" of this section.

Wang Feng (王枫): Male, born in 1982, Chinese national, no permanent right of abode in a foreign country, holds a bachelor's degree. From July 2006 to May 2009, he served as Assistant Director at Chun'an Zhenye Tax Agency Co., Ltd. (淳安振业税务师事务所有限责任公司); from June 2009 to April 2014, he served as Finance Manager at Ningbo Xintai Machinery Co., Ltd. (宁波信泰机械有限公司); from April 2014 to May 2015, he served as Chief Financial Officer at Hangzhou Feizhu Technology Co., Ltd. (杭州菲助科技有限公司); from May 2015 to March 2021, he served as Chief Financial Officer at Zhejiang Shuanglin Environmental Co., Ltd. (浙江双林环境股份有限公司); he has been employed by the Company since March 2021 and currently serves as the Company's Chief Financial Officer.

Fu Fenghua (傅风华): Male, born in 1988, Chinese national, no permanent right of abode in a foreign country, holds a bachelor's degree. From September 2010 to May 2018, he successively served as Auditor, Senior Auditor, Project Manager, and Senior Project Manager at Tianjian Certified Public Accountants (Special General Partnership) (天健会计师事务所（特殊普通合伙）); from June 2018 to May 2025, he successively served as Deputy Financial Director, Chief Financial Officer and Board Secretary, and Deputy General Manager at Zhejiang Hangke Technology Co., Ltd. (浙江杭可科技股份有限公司); he has been employed by the Company since May 2025 and currently serves as the Company's Board Secretary.

(VIII) External Investment Activities of Directors, Supervisors, Senior Management, and Core Technical Personnel Related to the Issuer and Its Business

As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, apart from the Company's equity incentive platform, the directors, senior management, and other core personnel of the Company have no other external investment activities related to the issuer and its business.

(IX) Remuneration of Directors, Supervisors, Senior Management, and Core Technical Personnel

1. Composition and Basis for Determining Remuneration

The Remuneration and Appraisal Committee under the Company's Board of Directors is responsible, in accordance with the Articles of Association and applicable laws and regulations, for reviewing the overall remuneration plans for the Company's directors, senior management, and core technical personnel.

For directors, supervisors (prior to the abolition of the Board of Supervisors), senior management, and core technical personnel who hold specific operational positions within the Company, their remuneration consists of two components: salary and bonuses. Salary includes basic salary and performance-based salary determined according to factors such as the individual's position, seniority, and professional competence; bonuses are paid based on annual performance, performance assessments, and the Company's operating results.

The Company has appointed 3 independent directors since September 2025; the annual allowance for independent directors is RMB 100,000.

2. Procedures Followed

The remuneration of the Company's directors, supervisors (prior to the abolition of the Board of Supervisors), and senior management has been reviewed and approved in accordance with the relevant deliberation procedures stipulated in the Articles of Association, the Working Rules of the Remuneration and Appraisal Committee, and other corporate governance documents.

3. Remuneration as a Percentage of Total Profit

During the reporting period, the total remuneration (excluding share-based payments) of the Company's directors, supervisors (prior to the abolition of the Board of Supervisors), senior management, and core technical personnel, and its percentage of the Company's total profit, are as follows:

Unit: RMB 10,000

Item January–September 2025 Full Year 2024 Full Year 2023 Full Year 2022

Total Profit 17,103.59 9,947.32 -2,140.87 -2,772.14
Percentage 5.02% 8.68% -24.97% -17.47%

4. Remuneration Received from the Issuer and Related Enterprises in the Most Recent Year

During the reporting period, directors and supervisors who have not entered into employment contracts with the Company do not receive remuneration from the Company. Independent directors receive only independent director allowances from the Company and are not entitled to any other benefits.

Directors, supervisors (prior to the abolition of the Board of Supervisors), senior management, and core technical personnel who have entered into employment contracts with the Company receive remuneration from the Company. The total remuneration received from the Company in 2024 amounted to RMB 8,638,900, with details as follows:

XII. Equity Incentive Plans and Related Arrangements and Implementation

In order to further improve the Company's incentive system, motivate employees who have made significant contributions to the Company, attract outstanding talent, establish a medium- and long-term incentive and restraint mechanism, and effectively align the interests of shareholders, the Company, and individual incentive recipients so as to promote the Company's long-term development, the Company has, prior to the filing of this public offering application, formulated equity incentive-related arrangements in the form of an employee stock ownership plan. Shanghai Yuyi (上海宇翼) and its upper-tier partnership enterprises Hangzhou Yixin (杭州翌心) and Hangzhou Yiyi (杭州翌意) serve as the shareholding platforms for the Company's equity incentives. Incentive recipients indirectly hold the Company's shares by holding partnership interests in the incentive platforms as limited partners.

As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, Shanghai Yuyi holds 39,828,600 shares of the Company, representing a shareholding ratio of 10.9414%. For the basic information of Shanghai Yuyi, please refer to "Section VII, (II), 1, Shanghai Yuyi" of this section.

(I) Implementation of the Equity Incentive Plan

From September 2017 to December 2021, the Company successively entered into Option Agreements (hereinafter the "First Batch Subscription Agreements") with 17 employees including Yang Zhiyu, granting such employees the right to subscribe for equity interests in the Company at a price of RMB 1 per unit of registered capital (hereinafter the "First Equity Incentive").

In January 2024, the shareholders' meeting of Unitree Limited (宇树有限) deliberated and approved the Hangzhou Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. 2023 Option Incentive Plan (hereinafter the "Second Equity Incentive" and the "2023 Incentive Plan"), resolving to use Tianjin Unitree (天津宇树) (former name of Shanghai Yuyi) as the equity incentive implementation platform, and to incorporate the First Equity Incentive into the 2023 Incentive Plan for unified implementation and management. The Incentive Plan provides that the equity interests of the Company held by the incentive platform shall serve as incentive equity; incentive employees shall indirectly hold incentive equity by holding a certain quantity of property interests in the incentive platform; and the effective period of the incentive plan is 10 years.

From March 2024 to July 2025, pursuant to the 2023 Incentive Plan, the Company successively granted incentive interests to 6 employees and entered into Hangzhou Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. Option Subscription Agreements (hereinafter the "Second Batch Subscription Agreements").

In order to further improve the Company's equity incentive plan, in May 2025, the board of directors and shareholders' meeting of Unitree Limited resolved to confirm the exercise implementation status of the Company's First Equity Incentive and again clearly resolved to incorporate the First Equity Incentive and the Second Equity Incentive for unified implementation and management under the incentive platform Shanghai Yuyi.

In June 2025, the Company convened its Third Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders for 2025, which deliberated and approved the proposal regarding the capital increase by the Company's equity incentive platform Shanghai Yuyi. At this point, the proportion of the Company's shares corresponding to the partnership interests in Shanghai Yuyi available for granting equity incentives increased to 10.9414%.

In September 2025, the Eighth Session of the First Board of Directors and the Fifth Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders for 2025 deliberated and approved the Proposal on Amending the Employee Stock Ownership Plan Charter and the amended Employee Stock Ownership Plan Charter, revising the Company's 2023 Incentive Plan into the Employee Stock Ownership Plan Charter. Based on the above shareholders' meeting resolutions and the Employee Stock Ownership Plan Charter, the Company entered into supplemental agreements with incentive employees regarding the subscription agreements.

In March 2026, the Fourteenth Session of the First Board of Directors and the First Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders for 2026 deliberated and approved the Proposal on Amending the Employee Stock Ownership Plan Charter and the amended Employee Stock Ownership Plan Charter, adjusting the form of granting and exercising equity incentives from a method of one-time grant with staged exercise to restricted shares with one-time grant and exercise but staged release of restrictions. This amendment will take effect with respect to future incentive matters.

As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, among the incentive recipients who have been granted interests, 9 employees have subsequently resigned, and the corresponding granted incentive interests have been cancelled. Based on the total number of shares after the transfer of capital reserve to share capital in June 2022 and June 2025, the number of Company shares corresponding to the granted incentive interests of 14 employees totals 5,926,576 shares, of which the number of Company shares corresponding to the exercised incentive interests of 11 employees totals 5,113,372 shares. Among the incentive employees who have been granted interests, 12 are from the R&D department, and 2 are from the sales and production departments, respectively.

(II) Arrangements Related to the Equity Incentive Plan

Pursuant to the Company's Employee Stock Ownership Plan Charter, the main arrangements of the employee stock ownership plan established prior to this offering are as follows:

1. Incentive Interests

Under the above employee stock ownership plan, the equity incentive interests are the partnership interests in the incentive platform Shanghai Yuyi and its upper-tier partnership enterprises Hangzhou Yixin (杭州翌心) and Hangzhou Yiyi (杭州翌意).

2. Unlock Period Arrangements

Incentive recipients shall unlock the granted incentive interests in four tranches. If an incentive recipient meets the performance targets within the assessment year, they may unlock 25% of the total granted incentive interests upon the first anniversary of the grant date, a further 25% upon the second anniversary, a further 25% upon the third anniversary, and a further 25% upon the fourth anniversary. If an incentive recipient fails to meet the performance assessment targets for a given year, the unvested interests for that year shall not be carried forward to subsequent years for unlocking.

3. Service Period Arrangements

Prior to a qualifying listing, incentive recipients may not transfer, encumber, or otherwise dispose of all or any part of their incentive interests in the incentive platform; after a qualifying listing, if at the relevant time the Company has been listed for 36 months following the qualifying listing and the unlocked incentive interests for each tranche have been held for 5 years from the date of that tranche's unlock, the incentive recipient may apply to dispose of the incentive interests held. That is, the service period for each tranche of unlocked incentive interests is the later of: 36 months after the qualifying listing and 5 years after the exercise of each respective tranche.

4. Repurchase of Incentive Interests

Pursuant to the relevant provisions of the Company's employee stock ownership plan charter, the handling of incentive interest repurchases under different circumstances is as follows:

(1) Blameless Departure During the Service Period

If an incentive recipient's employment is terminated during the service period due to mutual agreement with the Company, incapacity for work, death, disability, retirement, or the Company's economic layoffs, the repurchase price for the exercised incentive interests held by such recipient shall be calculated according to the following formula:

Repurchase Price = Exercise Unit Price × Number of Incentive Interests × (1 + Applicable Benchmark Interest Rate for Time Deposits of Equivalent Term at the Time × 150% × N), where N is the coefficient equal to the actual number of days from the incentive recipient's unlock date to the repurchase date divided by 365.

(2) Unauthorized Departure or Termination of Service by an Employee Who Has Voluntarily Proposed to Dissolve the Employment Relationship During the Service Period

If an incentive recipient who has voluntarily proposed to dissolve the employment relationship during the service period abandons their post or terminates service prior to reaching a mutual agreement with the Company, the administrator has the right to designate a specific purchaser to repurchase the incentive interests held by such recipient, at a repurchase price equal to the actual consideration paid by the recipient.

(3) Misconduct by an Incentive Recipient or Participation in Other Incentive Plans

If an incentive recipient engages in misconduct or participates in the incentive plan of another company, the administrator has the right to designate a specific purchaser to repurchase the incentive interests held by such recipient, at a repurchase price equal to the actual consideration paid by the recipient.

(III) Impact of Equity Incentives on the Company's Operations, Financial Condition, and Control, and Exercise Arrangements After Listing

1. Accounting Treatment of Share-Based Payments Related to Equity Incentives

The Company's equity incentives involve share-based payment accounting treatment. Share-based payment expenses recognized on an allocated basis over the employees' vesting periods are recorded as recurring profit or loss. The relevant accounting treatment complies with the requirements of the Accounting Standards for Business Enterprises and other applicable standards.

Grant Date: The specific grant date agreed upon in the relevant incentive agreements entered into between the Company and the incentive recipients.

Vesting Period: The service period for each tranche of exercised incentive interests is the later of 36 months after the qualifying listing and 5 years after the exercise of each respective tranche, with share-based payment expenses recognized by monthly amortization.

Fair Value: The Company's equity incentives are valued by reference to the second category of restricted shares, using the Black-Scholes model to calculate the fair value of the incentive interests based on the investment price of institutional investors at a comparable point in time. Zhongshui Zhiyuan Asset Appraisal Co., Ltd. (中水致远资产评估有限公司) assessed the fair value of the equity instruments and issued the Value Analysis Report on the Fair Value of Stock Options Involved in the Implementation of Option Incentives by Hangzhou Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (Reference No. 中水致远评咨字[2025]第020114号) and the Value Analysis Report on the Fair Value of Stock Options Involved in the Implementation of Option Incentives by Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (Reference No. 中水致远咨报字[2025]第020019号).

Treatment of Departed Employees: During the vesting period, when an incentive recipient departs, unexercised incentive interests are cancelled, and the share-based payment expenses previously recognized for the departed employee are reversed in the period of departure.

2. Share-Based Payment Situation During the Reporting Period and Future Impact of Share-Based Payments

During the reporting period, share-based payment expenses arising from grants to incentive recipients and recorded as recurring profit or loss were RMB 293,800, RMB 498,000, RMB 979,400, and RMB 1,519,000, respectively, representing a relatively small proportion of the non-recurring items-adjusted net profit for each period. The implementation of the above equity incentives is lawful and compliant. The controlling shareholder and actual controller of the Company have not changed before or after the implementation of the equity incentives, and there has been no material adverse impact on the Company's operations, financial condition, or control, nor is there any situation that damages the interests of the Company or its shareholders.

In addition to the share-based payment expenses arising from grants to incentive recipients recorded as recurring profit or loss, the Company recognized share-based payment expenses of RMB 18,722,600 and RMB 349,065,500 in 2022 and January–September 2025, respectively, in connection with Shanghai Yuyi's two capital increases at registered capital price in January 2022 and June 2025, both of which were recognized in full as non-recurring profit or loss for the respective periods. This caused the Company's accounting net profit to decrease to RMB -22,100,500 and RMB 105,331,400 for 2022 and January–September 2025, respectively. These share-based payment expenses do not involve any cash outflow from the Company. Although they affect the net profit during the reporting period, they do not change the net profit attributable to the owners of the parent company after deducting non-recurring profit or loss, nor the cash flow situation. The Company's net profit attributable to the owners of the parent company after deducting non-recurring profit or loss for 2022 and January–September 2025 was RMB -8,070,800 and RMB 430,612,300, respectively.

As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, the partnership interests in the upper-tier partnerships of Shanghai Yuyi held by the Company's actual controller Wang Xingxing correspond in aggregate to a 9.54% equity interest in the Company. These partnership interests will be used in the future to grant equity incentives to the Company's employees. At that time, the Company will calculate and recognize relevant share-based payment expenses for the applicable periods based on the fair value of the shares at the time of granting incentive interests, the employees' grant price, and the agreed service periods. The relevant share-based payment expenses may have a significant impact on the Company's future operating results.

(IV) Nominee Shareholding and Restoration Arrangements for Equity Incentives

1. Nominee Shareholding in the Company's Historical Development

(1) Establishment of the Nominee Shareholding Arrangement

From September 2017 to December 2021, the Company entered into the First Batch Subscription Agreements with the incentive employees under the First Equity Incentive. In view of confidentiality considerations regarding the identity of those incentive recipients, the agreements provided that the Company's equity interests corresponding to the exercise of rights by such incentive recipients would be held in nominee capacity by the Company's actual controller, Wang Xingxing. From March 2018 to April 2025, pursuant to the First Batch Subscription Agreements, the Company's equity interests corresponding to the exercises by 4 First Equity Incentive recipients were successively held in nominee capacity by Wang Xingxing; by April 2025, the proportion of the Company's equity held in nominee capacity for exercised interests represented 0.56% of the Company's total share capital at that time.

(2) Termination of the Nominee Shareholding Arrangement

In order to further improve the Company's equity incentive plan, in May 2025, the board of directors and shareholders' meeting of Unitree Limited resolved to confirm the exercise implementation status of the Company's First Equity Incentive and clearly resolved to incorporate the First Equity Incentive and the Second Equity Incentive for unified implementation and management under the incentive platform Shanghai Yuyi. In the same month, the Company entered into Supplemental Agreements to the First Batch Subscription Agreements with each of the First Equity Incentive recipients respectively, stipulating that the incentive equity corresponding to the recipients' exercises would all be adjusted to the corresponding property interests in the incentive platform, and Wang Xingxing would no longer hold the Company's equity in nominee capacity for the incentive recipients; the matters relating to the incentive recipients' equity incentives would be incorporated into the 2023 Incentive Plan and Shanghai Yuyi for unified implementation and management.

As a result, the nominee shareholding arrangement between the exercised incentive employees and Wang Xingxing in respect of the Company's equity corresponding to the exercised incentive equity was terminated. The First Equity Incentive recipients, upon exercise, will have the corresponding exercised property interests in the incentive platform held in nominee capacity by Wang Xingxing, and will no longer directly or by nominee hold Company equity.

2. Nominee Holding of Property Interests in Shanghai Yuyi's Historical Development

(1) Establishment of the Nominee Holding Arrangement

In 2024, the Company successively entered into Second Batch Subscription Agreements with 3 employees pursuant to the 2023 Incentive Plan. Pursuant to the Second Batch Subscription Agreements, the incentive recipients would not be registered as named partners of the incentive platform with the registration authority upon acquiring incentive interests; the exercised incentive interests would be held in nominee capacity by Wang Xingxing, a partner of the shareholding platform.

At the same time, after the First Equity Incentive recipients signed the Supplemental Agreements to the First Batch Subscription Agreements in May 2025, the exercised incentive interests of the First Equity Incentive recipients were held in nominee capacity by Wang Xingxing, a partner of the incentive platform. As of September 2025, the equity ratio of the Company's shares corresponding to the partnership interests held in nominee capacity by Wang Xingxing in the incentive platform Shanghai Yuyi on behalf of the aforementioned 11 exercising incentive employees was 1.40%.

(2) Termination of the Nominee Holding Arrangement

In September 2025, the aforementioned 11 employees and the Company entered into Second Supplemental Agreements to the First Batch Subscription Agreements and Supplemental Agreements to the Second Batch Subscription Agreements, providing that from the effective date of such supplemental agreements and upon the completion of the industrial and commercial change registration/filing for the named registration of the exercised incentive interests, the nominee holding arrangement for the exercised incentive interests between the incentive recipients and the actual controller would be terminated. In November 2025, the exercising employees completed the industrial and commercial change registration, and the nominee holding arrangement for the partnership interests corresponding to 1.40% of the Company's equity in the Shanghai Yuyi shareholding platform was thereby fully terminated.

Based on interviews with the aforementioned persons and written confirmation letters issued by them, the nominee shareholding arrangements in the Company's historical development and the nominee holding of property interests in the historical development of the equity incentive platform have been fully terminated, and the parties to the nominee arrangements have confirmed that no disputes or potential disputes exist during the nominee holding period or in the course of terminating the nominee holding arrangements.

As at the end of each period during the reporting period, the Company's employee headcount was 209, 264, 328, and 480, respectively.
