Driven by the multiple demands of population aging, rising labor costs, and improving production efficiency and quality of life, high-performance general-purpose robots capable of replacing or assisting humans in performing various tasks have become an important breakthrough for addressing labor shortages and efficiency bottlenecks, harboring enormous market demand. On the one hand, humanoid robots, leveraging their terrain adaptation advantages, have gradually been applied in various industrial scenarios such as electric power systems, emergency fire protection, construction sites, and smart factories, and their commercialization process is entering a stage of rapid development. On the other hand, humanoid robots are at a critical juncture of breaking through a technological inflection point and building applications spanning both consumer-grade and industry-grade, and also possess the potential for future application across multiple industries, including but not limited to household services, education and entertainment, commercial services, healthcare, and industrial production. The deployment of applications in the aforementioned fields implies enormous market prospects and is expected to drive a broad and vast consumer-grade and industry-grade market.

  3. Policy support and social investment reinforce development momentum. In recent years, the central government and localities across the country have successively issued relevant policies for the robotics industry, standardizing and encouraging rapid industry growth, and further increasing acceptance of robots across various industries. China's industrial policies will have a positive impact on the industry from both the supply side and the demand side: on the supply side, enterprises engaged in the R&D and product development of various forms of intelligent robot technologies receive policy support, which will be conducive to their rapid development; on the demand side, industries across the board will increase their acceptance of robots and attempt to explore robot applications, which will be conducive to mature commercial applications. This plays a key role in the promotion, application, and iterative upgrading of robot products.

    The powerful social influence and potential economic benefits of general-purpose robots have driven large-scale investment from social capital and industrial funds, bringing enormous development opportunities to the robotics industry and its upstream and downstream industrial chains, accelerating the productization and industrialization of the general-purpose robot industry, and are expected to help facilitate its rapid commercial deployment.

(2) Challenges Faced

  1. Core technologies still face bottlenecks and urgently require further breakthroughs and innovation. As the most complex, highest-level, and most cutting-edge sub-sector of the robotics field, high-performance general-purpose robots still face numerous technological challenges in their development, including but not limited to motion control and dynamic balance, perception and environmental interaction, artificial intelligence and learning capability, energy efficiency and battery life, more powerful human-robot interaction, and actuator precision and response speed. The development of the aforementioned core technologies involves multi-disciplinary integration; from hardware to software, and from low-level control to high-level cognition, continuous technological breakthroughs and innovations are still required, which further affects the process of high-performance general-purpose robots advancing toward generalized, intelligent, and large-scale applications.

  2. Persistently high R&D and production costs constrain large-scale applications. The persistently high costs of high-performance general-purpose robots are an important factor constraining their development. On the one hand, their production costs are relatively high, with substantial investments in hardware, software, and artificial intelligence during the production process; as high-performance general-purpose robots have not yet achieved large-scale applications, the economies of scale on the production side have not yet been realized. On the other hand, high-performance general-purpose robots require relatively high upfront R&D investment in areas such as complex mechanical structures, high-performance perception and interaction systems, and artificial intelligence, and the relevant technology R&D is fraught with uncertainty—the difficulty of achieving breakthroughs in core technologies and the time required are difficult to accurately predict. In many industrial scenarios, natural human labor, industrial robots, and automation equipment offer higher cost-effectiveness and can achieve automation functions at lower cost. Therefore, reducing the comprehensive costs of high-performance general-purpose robots to improve cost-effectiveness is crucial for their large-scale applications.

  3. Application scenarios at the current stage are highly limited, and future market demand is subject to uncertainty. There are numerous and complex application scenarios in the real world, and many edge or unconventional scenarios exist. When dealing with these special situations, high-performance general-purpose robots need to possess extremely high autonomy and adaptability, which is currently difficult to achieve at the present technology level. Consequently, the mass-application market for high-performance general-purpose robots at the current stage is not yet mature; application scenarios are relatively limited and highly fragmented, with current commercial applications primarily concentrated in scenarios such as quadruped robot inspection, quadruped/humanoid robot scientific research, and consumer entertainment. In broader industry-level applications, high-performance general-purpose robots have not yet formed complete, commercially mature applications; in consumer-level applications, the high price and limited functions present enormous challenges to large-scale commercial use.

6. Cyclical Characteristics of the Industry

The Company is primarily engaged in the R&D, production, and sales of high-performance general-purpose humanoid robots, quadruped robots, robot components, and embodied intelligence models. Downstream industry demand is continuously growing and market space is continuously expanding; there are no significant cyclical characteristics in the industry.

During the reporting period, the industry in which the Company operates is in a stage of rapid development, with downstream market demand growing rapidly. The market scale is expected to continue to grow in the future, and the industry's technology level, development trend, barriers to entry, and other characteristics are expected to remain basically stable.

(IV) Competitive Landscape of the Industry

1. Competition in Sub-Sectors

Empowered by technologies such as artificial intelligence, large models, and cloud computing, general-purpose robots will gradually develop powerful

1-1-119

宇树科技股份有限公司                                                        招股说明书

的智能、思维和类人的语言能力，行业发展正处于商业化爆发的前期阶段。市场普遍预期，随着通用机器人商业化落地逐渐实现，到 2030 年市场规模可达到数百亿美元，未来将会带动万亿级别的产业链发展。

基于上述背景，近年来越来越多的厂商开始布局行业级、消费级四足/人形机器人，导致竞争日益激烈。海外企业中，波士顿动力、特斯拉、Figure 等在技术研发和商业应用上的先行探索，为行业发展提供了重要参考；国内企业中，以宇树科技为代表的专业化创业企业专注于产品自研、迭代及商业化场景布局，部分大型科技企业借助自身在 AI、智能硬件生态的优势探索生态赋能，国内整车企业利用汽车制造中积累的零部件优势和生产经验为机器人研发及工业应用场景探索提供潜在可行性。各大厂商为争夺市场份额，不断推出性能更优异、价格更低廉的产品。市场竞争压力推动机器人价格不断下降，也推动了技术的进步与成本的优化。

对于四足机器人，其竞争格局已呈现出较为清晰的分层特征，参与者大体可分为国内与海外两类。在国内市场，以宇树科技为代表的头部企业具备全栈自研能力与产品先发优势，宇树科技和云深处在商业化应用、规模化交付方面形成领先优势；除两家头部企业外，其他参与企业多为初创企业，产品多处于新品推广、市场拓展阶段。国内厂商的快速发展与持续创新，使得四足机器人已能够较快进入科研教育、商业消费及行业应用等下游领域。相较而言，海外厂商虽起步较早，但当前出货与应用规模整体不及国内企业，下游应用更多集中于科研和工业场景。以 Boston Dynamics、ANYbotics 等为代表的欧美厂商，四足机器人业务以高单价设备销售、定制化项目交付为主，市场扩张进展相对较慢，在规模化普及、商业化应用已与国内厂商存在差距。

对于人形机器人，国内厂商已呈现较为明显的规模分化。在国内市场，宇树科技作为较早实现商业化交付、规模化量产的头部厂商，产品广泛覆盖了科研教育、商业消费与行业应用等众多现实场景，宇树科技 2025 年度不含双臂轮式的类人形态纯人形机器人的发货量已超 5,500 台，出货量居全球第一。除宇树科技等少数企业 2025 年出货量突破千台级以上规模外，其余行业主要企业的出货量在千台以下区间，量产规模出现阶梯式分化，但较上年度均实现了较快增长。相较而言，海外人形机器人企业目前整体以原型机开发迭代、试点部署及小规模交

1-1-120

宇树科技股份有限公司                                                        招股说明书

付为主，量产出货规模显著低于国内企业。2025 年，海外主要厂商 Figure、Agility Robotics 的人形机器人出货量均在 150 台左右，上述数据均未经各公司公开确认。Tesla 目前尚未公开对外发售人形机器人，其人形机器人目前主要用于公司内部开发测试。

现阶段行业竞争的关键已从原型机开发展示转向产品交付与持续迭代，国内厂商在产业化节奏、出货规模与场景覆盖方面，已呈现更为明显的优势；其中，公司凭借"科研—行业—消费"三类场景的同步覆盖与平台化生态建设，产业化效果最为突出，保持了领先的市场地位。同时，部分国内企业更为侧重围绕工业场景推进人形机器人的验证与部署，目前该场景整体处于探索验证阶段。

高性能通用机器人产业目前正处于产业化发展初期，面临着核心技术有待突破、生产成本高昂、商业化落地场景有限等诸多制约因素。目前行业内涌入的企业都将面临上述问题的制约，能够率先突破核心技术瓶颈、探索成熟的商业化应用场景并形成成熟应用、生产端实现规模效应并降低成本的企业将脱颖而出，成为高性能通用机器人行业的领导者并获得更大的市场份额。

2. Major Enterprises in the Industry

(1) Tesla (TSLA.O)

Tesla was founded in 2003 and is a leading global enterprise in the electric vehicle sector. Tesla's robotics project is the Tesla Bot, also known as Optimus — a general-purpose bipedal humanoid robot developed under the leadership of CEO Elon Musk. In August 2021, Tesla unveiled the humanoid robot concept machine, the Tesla Bot; in February 2022, Tesla introduced a humanoid robot prototype; in December 2023, Tesla released the Optimus-Gen2, achieving certain improvements in stability, agility, speed, and lightweighting.

(2) Figure

Figure is a technology company focused on developing humanoid robots, founded in 2022. It is dedicated to building general-purpose humanoid robots capable of autonomously completing complex tasks, with the aim of deploying them across manufacturing, logistics, warehousing, and retail industries. In August 2024, the company launched its robot product Figure 02 and piloted its application in automotive factory production.

1-1-121

宇树科技股份有限公司                                                        招股说明书

(3) Boston Dynamics

Boston Dynamics was founded in 1992 and is a well-known American high-performance general-purpose robotics company. In 2013, Boston Dynamics released the humanoid robot Atlas and has continuously improved its capabilities; the robot is now capable of climbing over obstacles, performing aerial rotations, back flips, and autonomous navigation in narrow terrain.

(4) UBTECH Robotics Corp Ltd (优必选科技股份有限公司, 9880.HK)

UBTECH was founded in March 2012 and is primarily engaged in the intelligent service robot and intelligent robot solution business. Its main products include educational intelligent robots, logistics intelligent robots, humanoid and commercial service robots, as well as consumer-grade robots such as intelligent lawn mowers, pool-cleaning robots, and sweeping robots, along with other hardware products. Since 2018, UBTECH has successively launched multiple Walker-series humanoid robot products.

(5) Dobot (越疆科技股份有限公司, 2432.HK)

Dobot was founded in July 2015 and is one of the enterprises specializing in the development, manufacturing, and commercialization of collaborative robots, and is also a participant in the embodied intelligent robot sector. Dobot's collaborative robot products have been applied in industrial manufacturing, commercial retail, medical surgery, healthcare, scientific research, and education.

(6) Leju Robotics (乐聚智能（深圳）股份有限公司)

Leju Robotics (Shenzhen) Co., Ltd. was founded in March 2016 and is an enterprise engaged in the research, development, and production of intelligent robot products. The company has successively launched multiple intelligent robot products and derivatives.

(7) Hangzhou Yushu Technology (杭州云深处科技股份有限公司)

Hangzhou Yushu Technology Co., Ltd. was founded in November 2017. The "Jueying" (绝影) series quadruped robots developed by the company have been deployed in scenarios including power station, factory, and pipeline inspection, as well as emergency rescue, fire reconnaissance, and scientific research and development.

(8) Agibot (智元创新（上海）科技股份有限公司)

Agibot (Shanghai) Technology Co., Ltd. was founded in February 2023. Its main products include humanoid robot series such as Yuanzheng (远征), Jingling (精灵), and Lingxi (灵犀). It is reported to have applications in scenarios including education and research, industrial manufacturing, cultural performance, interactive services, and commercial logistics.

3. Issuer's Market Position

The Company insists on full-stack in-house development of core technologies for general-purpose robots, continuously enriches its product matrix, and has led innovation and large-scale commercialization in the high-performance general-purpose robotics industry. The Company has captured a major share of the global market for both quadruped robots and humanoid robots, and in particular enjoys a high level of brand recognition and market share in overseas markets. During the reporting period, the cumulative sales volume of the Company's quadruped robots exceeded 30,000 units, establishing the Company's dominant position in the global quadruped robot market. Meanwhile, since the Company independently developed and introduced its first humanoid robot H1 in 2023 and the mid-sized humanoid robot G1 in 2024, the sales volume of the Company's humanoid robot products has achieved rapid growth. In 2025, the Company's humanoid robot shipment volume exceeded 5,500 units (pure humanoid form, excluding wheeled dual-arm robots), ranking first globally in shipment volume — demonstrating that the Company has leveraged its core in-house development to achieve first-mover commercialization and has attained a leading market advantage in the general-purpose robotics sector.

Since its establishment, the Company has continuously conducted technology R&D and product innovation in the high-performance general-purpose robotics field. In particular, in the area of motion control capability — which reflects the overall performance of high-performance general-purpose robots — the Company has continuously achieved numerous industry-wide technical performance breakthroughs. Examples are as follows:

Category Event Date Product Model Event Significance
High-Difficulty Maneuvers 2024 H1 Humanoid Full-size electric-drive humanoid robot performs standing back flip World's first
2025 G1 Humanoid Electric-drive humanoid robot performs standing side flip World's first
Locomotion Speed 2020 A1 Quadruped Maximum running speed reaches 3.3 m/s Fastest small-to-medium quadruped robot of similar specifications domestically at the time
2021 Go1 Quadruped Maximum running speed reaches 4.7 m/s Broke the world record for running speed among quadruped robots of similar specifications
2023 B2 Quadruped Maximum running speed reaches 6.0 m/s Broke the world record for running speed among quadruped robots of similar specifications
2024 H1 Humanoid Achieved running speed of 3.3 m/s Set the world record for running speed among full-size humanoid robots
2025 H1 Humanoid Achieved running speed exceeding 5 m/s Broke the world record for running speed among full-size humanoid robots
2025 H1 Humanoid Completed 1,500 meters in 6 minutes 34 seconds Broke the long-distance running speed record for humanoid robots of the same specifications
Load Capacity 2025 A2 Quadruped Continuous walking of over 12 km in 3 hours with a 25 kg payload Highest published payload-to-endurance ratio among quadruped robots of the same specifications at the time
Innovative Applications 2026 G1 Humanoid / H2 Humanoid CCTV Year of the Horse Spring Festival Gala group dance "Wu BOT" (《武BOT》) Consecutive acrobatic parkour over tables, launch back flips, and multiple other world records
2025 H1 Humanoid CCTV Year of the Snake Spring Festival Gala group dance "Yang BOT" (《秧BOT》) Fully AI-driven autonomous group humanoid robot performance
2025 G1 Humanoid Became the sole participating model in the "CMG World Robot Competition • Series" mecha combat arena Pioneered global humanoid robot combat competition
Robot Athletics Competition 2025 H1 Humanoid Won first place in the 1,500-meter race in 6 minutes 34 seconds At the inaugural World Robot Athletics Games, the Company won a total of 11 medals and was the enterprise with the most gold medals and total medals, fully validating the outstanding athletic performance and reliability of the Company's humanoid robots (H1, G1) in high-dynamic tasks such as competitive running and obstacle racing
2025 H1 Humanoid Won first place in the 400-meter race in 1 minute 28 seconds
2025 H1 Humanoid Won first place in the 4×100-meter relay race in 1 minute 48 seconds
2025 G1 Humanoid Won first place in the 100-meter obstacle race in 33.71 seconds

Note: The performance records above were compiled by the Company based on publicly available industry information at the time.

The Company has continuously expanded the application of its robots across multiple industry sectors. The Company's high-performance general-purpose robots, by virtue of their outstanding product performance, have provided critical support for frontier algorithm validation, system integration research, and embodied intelligence exploration in robotics, and are widely recognized by the academic community and technology enterprises. Representative customers include numerous well-known domestic and international universities, research institutions, and industry-leading technology enterprises.

In terms of industry applications, the Company has promoted industry-level applications of general-purpose robots globally in areas such as inspection and survey, fire rescue, and public services, bringing more efficient, safe, and intelligent new solutions to industries such as energy and chemicals, intelligent firefighting, and smart cities. These applications have been validated and deployed at numerous domestic and international industry-leading enterprises including State Grid Corporation, China Southern Power Grid, CNPC (PetroChina), Sinopec, and Baowu Group.

In the consumer sector, the Company pioneered the "high performance + high value-for-money" strategy to open up the critical pathway from technological R&D to large-scale application of general-purpose robots, significantly lowering the barrier to use for consumer-level users. The Company broadly covers consumer-level market demand for general-purpose robot products, promotes the application of general-purpose robot products in consumer-level markets such as educational instruction, cultural performance, and intelligent services, and has won widespread user favor through outstanding product experience and scenario adaptability, thereby gaining a leading market share and brand influence in the industry.

Furthermore, the Company's products have successively appeared at multiple major international exhibitions and performances, including the 2021 CCTV Spring Festival Gala, the 2023 American Super Bowl, the 2023 19th Asian Games, the 4th Asian Para Games, the 2025 CCTV Spring Festival Gala, and the 2026 CCTV Spring Festival Gala, and have been reported multiple times by authoritative media such as CCTV News. The Company's product capabilities have received widespread recognition from domestic and international industry professionals and users, demonstrating the technical stability and environmental adaptability of the Company's products.

4. Competitive Advantages and Disadvantages

(1) Competitive Advantages

1) Innovative In-House Algorithm Development and Intelligent Motion Control Advantages

The Company, relying on its full-stack in-house development capabilities spanning from embedded low-level drivers to advanced motion control algorithms, has built a deep technical barrier in the field of robot software and algorithms. The Company's core algorithms are closely oriented around the high-dynamic motion requirements of robots in unknown, unstructured environments, covering multi-level capabilities including "cerebellum" motion control and "cerebrum" understanding and prediction. Through a continuous OTA upgrade mechanism and an open secondary development ecosystem, the Company continuously improves the adaptability and commercial efficiency of its various algorithms.

At the "cerebellum" level, the Company has introduced deep reinforcement learning technology to address environmental uncertainty and complexity. Through large-scale parallel simulation and training, it enhances the robot's mobility and generalization capabilities in unknown environments. Through dynamics modeling and feedforward of the physical machine, it improves consistency from simulation to physical machine while ensuring precise and reliable control. At the same time, the Company's motion control algorithms deeply integrate multi-modal perception information from LiDAR, vision, inertial measurement units (IMU), and force sensors, achieving a closed loop between multi-modal perception and motion control. In the ongoing development and evolution of "cerebellum"-related embodied proprioceptive intelligence model technology, the Company has demonstrated leading technical foresight and engineering execution capabilities, forming a clear, stable, and high-frequency iteration cadence, reflecting continuously leading R&D efficiency and systematic innovation capabilities in the field of motion control.

At the "cerebrum" level, the Company collects large volumes of data from physical machines for training, enabling high-performance general-purpose robots to better understand natural language instructions and environmental perception interaction, and to generate corresponding action strategies, advancing toward true "embodied intelligence." At the same time, the Company is actively exploring and developing embodied intelligence models for robots, aiming to build embodied large models capable of predicting the state of interactions between the robot itself and its environment. By predicting future interactions with the physical world to dynamically optimize decision-making performance, and combining the advantages of imitation learning and reinforcement learning, the Company aims to significantly compress algorithm iteration and task adaptation cycles, accelerating migration from simulation to physical deployment. Regarding progress in R&D of "cerebrum"-related embodied large model technology, the Company currently has deployments and investments across two major technical pathways — the WMA model and the VLA model — and has successively open-sourced and released the general WMA model "UnifoLM-WMA-0" in September 2025 and the general VLA model "UnifoLM-VLA-0" in January 2026, achieving notably significant R&D results. Overall R&D capability and technical maturity have reached a leading tier within the industry.

At the system-level support layer, the Company supports an OTA upgrade mechanism, which enables faster and more continuous iteration of algorithm functions and improvements to user experience compared to offline upgrades. The on-board system algorithms independently developed by the Company possess the ability to monitor and manage on-board resources, sensing and computing load and resource utilization for effective scheduling. At the same time, the Company provides a user-friendly visual interface, flexible invocation interfaces, and easy-to-use secondary development toolkits, allowing users to conveniently modify configuration parameters. This lowers the barrier to product use, attracts a large number of developers for R&D and creation, and promotes algorithm optimization for deployment in specific scenarios through community feedback.

2) Full-Stack In-House Mechanical Design System and Hardware Advantages

Leveraging its hardware accumulation and deep vertical integration capabilities in the high-performance general-purpose robotics field, the Company has built an in-house hardware development system covering mechanical structures, power systems, and embedded control. In the face of the comprehensive requirements for hardware design of high-performance general-purpose robots, the Company focuses on the coordinated optimization of core elements including joint actuation (motors, reducers, drivers), energy management (high-power batteries, thermal control), and highly integrated lightweight structures, ensuring the comprehensive performance of the complete machine. Targeting the unstructured, high-dynamic complex operating conditions faced by high-performance general-purpose robots, the Company has broken through the fixed design paradigms of traditional industrial equipment and independently developed a set of specialized design theories and methodologies for high-performance general-purpose robots, covering transmission design, configuration optimization, strength verification, electromagnetic compatibility, and thermal management. This provides a theoretical foundation and engineering practice support for the R&D of reliability, durability, and impact resistance of high-performance general-purpose robots in variable environments. At the same time, the Company has significantly reduced the weight of key components through innovative application of new materials and advanced design methods such as topology optimization, and has endowed the complete machine with an excellent balance of lightweighting and structural rigidity.

At the joint actuation technology level, the Company pioneered and maintains the electric motor drive path for general-purpose robots. Through independently developed high power density joint motors and matching servo drives, it achieves precise control and efficient conversion of power output. The driver hardware selection and solution design balances high load capacity and instantaneous response characteristics, supporting millisecond-level torque adjustment and overheating protection, ensuring stability of high-performance general-purpose robots during high-dynamic actions.

At the hardware system design level, the Company deeply integrates embedded software and hardware with motion control algorithms. Through a customized control architecture, it has built a highly real-time closed-loop system that not only ensures precise execution of complex actions but also achieves intelligent management of motor heat and battery status. In addition, the Company implements a rigorous testing and verification system and simulation-driven design, ensuring the reliability and adaptability of the hardware system under extreme operating conditions, providing a solid foundation for rapid product iteration and large-scale application.

3) Cost Control and Large-Scale Delivery Advantages

Since its establishment, the Company has adhered to an in-house R&D approach for core components, pioneering the adoption of electric motor drive technology in the high-performance general-purpose robotics field. Relying on the deep technical accumulation of its core technology team, the Company has achieved independent R&D of motor drives, the complete machine mechanical structure, and whole-body control systems, significantly improving overall machine performance and product reliability. This approach provides advantages including high control precision, fast response speed, low operating noise, and ease of maintenance, while significantly reducing hardware costs.

By virtue of its flexible organizational structure and forward-looking market judgment, the Company has achieved technology reuse and collaborative development of core modules — including joint actuation, mechanical structures, battery management, and software algorithms — across humanoid robots and quadruped robots. This strategy has not only significantly reduced redundant R&D investment and effectively amortized per-product development costs, but has also materially accelerated the process from prototype development to mass delivery. Furthermore, multi-product, multi-scenario technology validation and data feedback continuously drive iterative optimization of key technologies, forming a virtuous technology feedback loop. In response to the industry characteristics of rapidly iterating high-performance general-purpose robot products and continuously evolving hardware body requirements in the AI era, the Company emphasizes stability and reliability in production processes and component selection, prioritizing solutions with high maturity and stable supply chains, and then conducting in-depth optimization and customized development for the performance requirements of high-performance general-purpose robots. By building a highly flexible production process and supply chain system, the Company can rapidly respond to technological changes and market demands, possessing shorter development cycles and faster mass delivery capabilities compared to competitors.

In terms of cost control, the Company has gradually established its own production lines through in-house development and production of core components and assemblies, achieving deep control over the supply chain. The vertical integration capability not only ensures rapid product and technology iteration, but also significantly reduces material procurement and manufacturing costs. Large-scale production further strengthens the Company's bargaining power with upstream suppliers, creating a sustained cost advantage. For example, the starting price of the Company's Go2 Air quadruped robot has fallen below RMB 10,000; the starting price of the basic version of the G1 humanoid robot is RMB 85,000; and the R1 Air humanoid robot further continues the high-value-for-money positioning with a starting price of RMB 29,900, once again lowering the market price threshold for high-performance general-purpose humanoid robots, reflecting the Company's industry-leading capabilities in cost control and large-scale delivery.

4) Rapid Product Iteration and Continuous Innovation Advantages

The Company has established a highly flat organizational structure that breaks down collaboration barriers between departments, significantly improving decision-making and response efficiency. Under this mechanism, the pace of product iteration significantly leads the industry, enabling rapid responses to technological changes and market demands, and continuously developing innovative products with strong competitiveness.

The Company insists on in-house R&D as its core, achieving breakthroughs in core components such as high-performance motors, reducers, and dexterous hands, with performance reaching industry-leading levels, laying a solid foundation for the high performance and high value-for-money of the overall machine products. Relying on rapid prototyping and iterative upgrades, as well as advanced reinforcement learning-based algorithms, the Company's products have achieved continuous and efficient technology accumulation and innovation breakthroughs. For example, in March 2024, the Company was the first in the world to achieve a back flip with a full-size electric-drive humanoid robot; around the same time, the humanoid robot H1 broke the world record with a running speed of 3.3 m/s; in Q1 2025, the humanoid robot G1 successively completed five iterative upgrades of high-dynamic maneuvers; and in August of the same year, the Company's humanoid robot products won 4 championship titles at the inaugural 2025 World Robot Athletics Games. These achievements not only demonstrate the Company's strong technological capabilities but also reflect the rapid response capability of the Company's technology system.

The Company's core technology team possesses the technical foundation and innovation DNA for persistent in-house R&D, comprising primarily master's and doctoral talent from multiple renowned domestic and international universities, with rich project experience and solid scientific research capabilities. Under the leadership of founder Wang Xingxing (王兴兴), the Company's core technology team upholds the corporate culture of "extreme insight, self-achievement, collaborative win-win," with the mission of "creating the world's technology tree," and continues to innovate and break through in pursuit of the corporate vision of "advancing the world through technology."

5) Product First-Mover Advantage and Rich Product Matrix Advantages

The Company demonstrated keen market foresight in its early days, rapidly capturing market share through technically advanced quadruped robots, and gradually building an open and collaborative industrial chain cooperation network leveraging its first-mover advantage and cost control capabilities, establishing close cooperative relationships with a variety of high-quality partners to continuously improve product richness and business flexibility. Building on the Company's deep technical accumulation and in-house production experience in the quadruped robot sector, the Company independently developed and mass-produced its first humanoid robot product, H1, in 2023, formally entering the humanoid robot field and leading the development of the humanoid robot industry through its rapid mass production capabilities. Currently, the Company has formed a product portfolio covering quadruped robots (Go Series, A Series, B Series) and humanoid robots (H Series, G Series, R Series), and has expanded into key components such as dexterous hands, collaborative robotic arms, and LiDAR sensors, building a robot product matrix centered on "mobility + manipulation + interaction." Leveraging its full-chain in-house development and production capabilities and multi-product synergy advantages, the Company not only continuously leads in performance but also sets an industry benchmark in high value-for-money, continuously driving innovation in robot technology R&D and application breakthroughs.

6) Open and Leading Industrial Chain Cooperation Advantages

The Company attaches great importance to deepening the scientific research and education market, establishing deep collaborations with world-renowned universities and technology enterprises to jointly explore frontier robot technologies. Against the backdrop of rapidly evolving AI technology, the Company maintains keen awareness of the latest technology trends, actively builds an open and collaborative robotics ecosystem, establishes a first-mover advantage in the high-performance general-purpose robotics industry, and provides continuous empowerment in robot intelligence.

The Company, by building an embodied intelligence open-source community, has open-sourced full-process algorithms based on its products covering data collection, model training, physical machine deployment, and motion control; its products possess a high degree of extensibility in structure, interfaces, and functionality, supporting developers in customizing hardware configurations, adding peripherals, or upgrading modules according to their needs, thereby providing full-chain secondary development support to developers. The Company's open-source reinforcement learning platform has attracted extensive collaboration from numerous renowned domestic and international universities and research institutions, mutually promoting theoretical and algorithm innovation iteration. Under the open and innovative collaboration model, the iteration speed of modules such as world models and motion control is several times faster than traditional closed R&D models, forming a virtuous cycle of technology feedback and product iteration.

In terms of industrial commercialization, the Company not only provides software development and mechanical modification support, but also offers customized solutions for different industry requirements, configuring specialized peripheral equipment for scenarios such as intelligent inspection, exploration and detection, and emergency firefighting. Currently, the Company's quadruped robot and humanoid robot products and technical services have been applied in multiple sectors including petrochemical, power, and education. By continuously expanding its ecosystem and strategic partnerships, the Company is steadily advancing the innovation and application of robot technology on a global scale.

(2) Competitive Disadvantages

1) Relatively Concentrated Financing Channels

The high-performance general-purpose robotics industry is at the initial stage of a commercial explosion. In order to maintain the Company's technological competitiveness, continuously enhance the core competitiveness of its products, and ensure timely responses to changes in market demand, the Company must engage in large-scale capital and R&D investment on a long-term and sustained basis, requiring substantial funding support in areas such as factory expansion, equipment procurement, market development, R&D investment, and talent acquisition. Currently, the Company's funding primarily comes from primary market equity financing and its own accumulation, with relatively concentrated financing channels and limited overall financial strength, which to a certain extent constrains the expansion of the Company's scale and business development.

2) Industry Applications Yet to Be Deepened

With technological progress, the market generally expects that humanoid robots will experience substantial productivity improvements in the future, playing an increasingly important role in industrial production, medical services, warehousing and logistics, family companionship, and scientific research and education. As a product of emerging technology, despite the large number of enterprises entering the global robot industry chain for production and R&D, most of the humanoid robots currently on the market remain at the application scenario validation and testing stage, and large-scale commercial deployment in daily-life domains has not yet been achieved. As an industry leader, the Company continuously explores practical applications of humanoid robots through technological collaborations with top domestic and international universities and commercial validation with leading downstream customers. There is a degree of uncertainty as to whether the enormous potential of the relevant markets can be realized in practice.

(V) Comparison with Comparable Companies in the Same Industry

1. Principal Business Conditions

The Company is primarily engaged in the R&D, production, and sales of high-performance general-purpose humanoid robots, quadruped robots, robot components, and embodied intelligence models. Currently, there are no listed general-purpose robotics companies on the A-share market. The Company has selected humanoid robot-related listed companies on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange as comparable companies. The relevant details are as follows:

Comparable Company Principal Business Description
UBTECH (优必选) Primarily engaged in intelligent service robots and intelligent robot solution business. Main products include educational intelligent robots, logistics intelligent robots, humanoid and commercial service robots, as well as consumer-grade robots such as intelligent lawn mowers, pool-cleaning robots, and sweeping robots, along with other hardware products.
Dobot (越疆) Primarily engaged in the design, development, manufacturing, and commercialization of collaborative robots. In March 2025, Dobot officially released the embodied intelligent humanoid robot Atom. Main products include six-axis collaborative robots, four-axis collaborative robots, and composite robots.

3. Market Position

For a comparison of the market positions of the Company and comparable companies in the same industry, please refer to Section II, (IV), 3, "Issuer's Market Position" of this section.

4. Issuer's Technological Strength and Key Business Data and Indicators Measuring Core Competitiveness

As a complex system integrating software and hardware algorithms, artificial intelligence technology, production engineering technology, and R&D of various components and parts across multiple disciplines and fields, the technological strength and core competitiveness of enterprises in the high-performance general-purpose robotics industry are difficult to reflect through specific technical indicators. They are typically reflected comprehensively in areas such as engineering mass production capability, product portfolio and iteration speed, motion control capability, and open-source ecosystem.

(1) Engineering Mass Production Conditions

Based on publicly available information, the engineering mass production conditions of the Company and comparable listed companies in the same industry are as follows:

Company Name Main Robot Products Cumulative Sales Volume of General-Purpose Robots
UBTECH (优必选) Educational intelligent robots, logistics intelligent robots, humanoid and commercial service robots, as well as consumer-grade products such as intelligent lawn mowers, pool-cleaning robots, and intelligent sweeping robots According to the company's public disclosures, the Walker S2 robot delivered more than 500 units in 2025. According to third-party reports, UBTECH's 2025 shipment volume was 1,000 units or 600 units.
Dobot (越疆) Collaborative robots, embodied intelligent humanoid robots According to the company's public disclosures, Dobot launched its first humanoid robot, Atom, in June 2025, and achieved small-batch delivery of embodied intelligent robots in the first half of 2025.
Unitree Technology (宇树科技) High-performance general-purpose humanoid robots, quadruped robots, robot components During the reporting period from 2022 to September 2025, the Company's cumulative humanoid robot sales volume was nearly 4,000 units, and quadruped robot sales volume totaled more than 30,000 units. In 2025, humanoid robot shipments exceeded 5,500 units (pure humanoid form, excluding wheeled dual-arm robots). Based on third-party data on the humanoid robot shipment volumes of other industry enterprises globally in 2025, the Company's 2025 humanoid robot shipment volume ranked first in the global industry.

Data source: Periodic reports, official websites, and publicly disclosed documents of each listed company. UBTECH's 2025 humanoid robot shipment volume is compiled from the Omdia Market Radar: General-purpose Embodied Intelligent Robots, 2026 and the 2025 Humanoid Robot Market Research Report.

As can be seen from the table above, the Company has achieved large-scale mass production and stable delivery of both its quadruped robots and humanoid robots, forming a highly mature engineering manufacturing system. For the robotics industry, engineering mass production is not only a reflection of production capability, but also a core validation indicator of technology maturity and commercial viability.

(2) Product Portfolio and Iteration Speed

Since the Company launched its first commercial product, Laikago, in 2017, it has maintained a relatively high frequency of new product launches and iterations, reflecting the Company's rapid innovation and R&D strength in the robotics field. Taking humanoid robots as an example, the Company's first independently developed full-size humanoid robot, H1, came to market in August 2023. Just nine months later, in May 2024, the Company released the mid-sized humanoid robot G1, which garnered widespread market enthusiasm for its precise motion control capabilities and highly anthropomorphic interactive performance. Entering 2025, the Company once again unveiled multiple major new products, including R1, which places greater emphasis on practicality and interactive experience, and H2, which achieves major breakthroughs in motion capability and flexibility.

In terms of product portfolio, the Company's product matrix is quite comprehensive, broadly covering both full-size (H1, H2) and mid-size to small-mid-size (G1, R1) humanoid robot segments, as well as industry-grade (B1, B2, and A2) and consumer-grade (Go1, Go2) quadruped robot segments. The Company's product launch timelines are relatively early, giving it a notably significant first-mover advantage, full-category coverage, and rapid iteration advantage across each segment.

(3) Robot Motion Control Capability

A general-purpose robot's ability to complete high-difficulty maneuvers is also regarded as a comprehensive reflection of its level of technical maturity. This not only tests the hardware performance and collaborative design of various body structure elements, but also places high demands on motion control algorithms; any technical weakness in any link will cause problems such as instability of action, response lag, or insufficient robustness for the overall system. At the same time, motion capability with high stability and high complexity is an important prerequisite for general-purpose robots to execute complex tasks in more application scenarios in the future.

In recent years, the completion of high-difficulty maneuvers by the Company's humanoid robots is as follows:

No. High-Difficulty Maneuver Date Event
1 Standing Back Flip March 2024 The Company's full-size electric-drive humanoid robot H1 performed a standing back flip — the world's first instance.
2 Group Dance January 2025 16 H1 humanoid robots from the Company participated in the CCTV Year of the Snake Spring Festival Gala group dance "Yang BOT" (《秧BOT》). Actions were choreographed to dance requirements, executed through AI training, and the robots were capable of fully automatic position changes and formation transitions.
3 Standing Side Flip March 2025 G1 completed the world's first standing side flip by an electric-drive humanoid robot.
4 Combat May 2025 The Company's humanoid robot G1 became the sole participating model in the "CMG World Robot Competition • Series" mecha combat arena, pioneering global humanoid robot combat competition.
5 Competitive Running and Obstacle Racing August 2025 The Company won a total of 11 medals at the inaugural World Humanoid Robot Athletics Games, taking first place in the 1,500-meter race, first place in the 400-meter race, first place in the 4×100-meter relay, and first place in the 100-meter obstacle race, fully validating the outstanding athletic performance and reliability of the Company's humanoid robots (H1, G1) in high-dynamic tasks such as competitive running and obstacle racing. The Company was the one with the highest total gold medal count and total medal count.
6 Accompanying Dance and Webster Flip December 2025 Six G1 humanoid robots from the Company performed alongside the singer in Wang Leehom's (王力宏) Chengdu concert, performing "Full Throttle" (《火力全开》), repeatedly and stably completing high-difficulty maneuvers including accompanying dance and Webster flips.
7 Group Martial Arts Performance February 2026 25 humanoid robots from the Company participated in the CCTV 2026 Spring Festival Gala program "Wu BOT" (《武BOT》), breaking multiple world records, including: (1) World's first consecutive acrobatic parkour over tables; (2) World's first launch back flip, with maximum flip height exceeding 3 meters; (3) World's first consecutive single-foot back flips, including two-step wall-run back flips; (4) World's first Airflare grand circle 7.5 rotations; (5) World's first group rapid repositioning (maximum arbitrary repositioning speed can reach 4 m/s).

Taking the technical capabilities demonstrated by the Company in the "Wu BOT" (《武BOT》) program at the 2026 Spring Festival Gala as an example, the Company comprehensively upgraded its cluster planning and control system and stunt motion control technology, achieving rapid choreography, simulation migration, and stable coordination of large-scale robot cluster movements. Through its self-developed high-dynamic repositioning control system and large-scale pre-trained motion control model, it ensured precise realization of high-speed mobile repositioning, smooth splicing of multiple complex actions, and good generalization capability. The Company also innovatively developed an AI full-body state model and elastic energy management system, effectively solving the challenges of localization in intense motion and the impact of transient high current surges on robots. Extensive and in-depth multi-domain technological innovations helped the Company successfully present, in a live setting, the world's first fully autonomous humanoid robot group martial arts performance (including complex rapid repositioning), breaking multiple industry records.

1-1-132

Among these, the cluster motion control technology for high-difficulty maneuvers of humanoid robots — as a concentrated breakthrough for technical challenges such as multi-machine coordination, tool interaction, and environmental interaction — is highly congruent with the environmental characteristics of real-world scenarios such as industrial manufacturing, warehousing and logistics, and home services, and has a clear technology transfer pathway. For example, automated cluster control and action synchronization achieved through multi-machine coordination systems can be applied to industrial inspection, warehouse sorting, and assembly lines; tool interaction and external force disturbance handling capabilities can be transferred to factory assembly, heavy object handling, and domestic service applications; and the relative positioning and environmental interaction technology demonstrated by parkour and table flipping can help improve robot operation efficiency and environmental adaptability in scenarios such as traversing narrow spaces and climbing stairs.

(4) Product Technology Open-Source Ecosystem

As of the end of February 2026, the open-source technologies released by the Company cover multiple general-purpose robotics technology chains including simulation modeling, motion control, embodied large models, and supporting software technology stacks, with relatively early development timelines and high industry recognition. First, in terms of open-source timing and community influence, the Company has been continuously releasing open-source projects on GitHub — the world's largest code hosting platform and open-source community — since 2018, and has now accumulated a total of 43 published projects. This represents a number of open-source projects and a level of attention far exceeding that of peers in the same industry, forming an active and large developer base.

Second, in terms of technical breadth and system completeness, the Company's open-source content covers the complete chain from frontier embodied large models and low-level motion control to software and hardware interfaces. Representative open-source achievements include: reinforcement learning training for motion control, simulation environment construction, teleoperation and physical machine data collection, robot SDKs and Python interfaces, ROS/ROS2 middleware integration, SLAM and multi-modal perception, as well as the UnifoLM-WMA-0 model and UnifoLM-VLA-0 model and other key technologies.

In summary, compared with companies in the same industry, the Company is at an industry-leading level in engineering mass production, product iteration speed, product matrix coverage, motion control capability, and open-source ecosystem, comprehensively reflecting the Company's technological strength and core competitiveness.

(VI) The Issuer's Position and Role in the Industrial Chain, and Its Relevance to Upstream and Downstream Industries

High-performance general-purpose robots are intelligent robots with environmental perception capabilities, flexible mobility, autonomous learning and collaborative capabilities, and the ability to adapt to different tasks. High-performance general-purpose robots can perform a variety of tasks ranging from physical operations to intelligent decision-making, and are designed to serve as multi-functional assistants in human production and daily life.

The industrial chain in which high-performance general-purpose robots operate primarily consists of upstream components, assemblies, and perception and motion control systems; midstream robot bodies; and downstream terminal applications. It can be further divided into two major directions: quadruped robots and humanoid robots. For specific details, please refer to Section II, (III), 1, "Position and Role of High-Performance General-Purpose Robots in the Industrial Chain, and Their Relevance to Upstream and Downstream Industries" of this section.

III. Sales Conditions and Major Customers

(I) Production Conditions of Main Products

The Company's main production processes include the production, assembly, and testing of various components and assemblies — including motors, reducers and joint modules, battery modules, circuit modules, and structural components — as well as complete machine products, following a flexible production model. During the reporting period, the Company could flexibly deploy production personnel among various production lines, and there was no relatively fixed designed production capacity.

The Company's production volume, sales volume, and production-to-sales ratio for each type of product are shown in the following table:

Unit: Units

Product Category Item January–September 2025 2024 2023 2022
Quadruped Robots Production Volume 21,122 7,240 3,149 2,520
Sales Volume 17,946 7,136 3,121 2,403
Production-to-Sales Ratio 84.96% 98.56% 99.11% 95.36%
Humanoid Robots Production Volume 3,701 545 9 —
Sales Volume 3,551 410 5 —
Production-to-Sales Ratio 95.95% 75.23% 55.56% —

With respect to quadruped robots, the production-to-sales ratio was generally at a high level in the first three years of the reporting period. The production-to-sales ratio for quadruped robots declined in January–September 2025, primarily because the Company began its cooperation with JD.com's self-operated platform (京东自营) at the end of 2024, selling mainly consumer-grade quadruped robots to it under a transaction model of shipping goods first and settling accounts later, with revenue recognized after settlement. As of the end of September 2025, the volume of goods dispatched and located in JD.com's self-operated warehouses was relatively large, leading to a temporary decline in the production-to-sales ratio.

With respect to humanoid robots, both production and sales volumes of the Company's products have increased year over year. In 2023 and 2024, the Company's H1 and G1 humanoid robots were successively brought to market and entered batch production and sales, and the production-to-sales ratio rose accordingly. By January–September 2025, the production-to-sales ratio of the Company's humanoid robots rose to a relatively high level.

(II) Revenue Conditions of Main Products

1. Composition of Principal Business Revenue

During the reporting period, the specific breakdown of the Company's principal business revenue by product category is as follows:

Unit: RMB 10,000

Category January–September 2025 Full Year 2024 Full Year 2023 Full Year 2022

1-1-134

Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (宇树科技股份有限公司) — Prospectus

Amount Proportion Amount Proportion Amount Proportion Amount Proportion
Quadruped Robots 48,798.55 42.25% 23,054.37 59.53% 11,938.09 75.78% 9,282.38 76.57%
Humanoid Robots 59,518.79 51.53% 10,689.76 27.60% 296.71 1.88% — —
Robot Components 6,653.89 5.76% 4,453.82 11.50% 2,692.34 17.09% 2,141.42 17.66%

Total 115,494.95 100% 38,727.28 100% 15,753.65 100% 12,122.77 100%

During each period of the Reporting Period, the Company achieved primary business revenue of RMB 121.2277 million, RMB 157.5365 million, RMB 387.2728 million, and RMB 1,154.9495 million, respectively, primarily comprising revenue from quadruped robots and humanoid robots.

2. Changes in Prices of Major Products

During the Reporting Period, changes in the prices of the Company's quadruped robots and humanoid robots were as follows:

Product Type Item January–September 2025 FY 2024 FY 2023 FY 2022
Quadruped Robots Sales Volume (units) 17,946 7,136 3,121 2,403
Unit Price (RMB 10,000/unit) 2.72 3.23 3.83 3.86
Sales Revenue (RMB 10,000) 48,798.55 23,054.37 11,938.09 9,282.38
Humanoid Robots Sales Volume (units) 3,551 410 5 —
Unit Price (RMB 10,000/unit) 16.76 26.07 59.34 —
Sales Revenue (RMB 10,000) 59,518.79 10,689.76 296.71 —

During the Reporting Period, revenue growth from quadruped robots was primarily driven by the rapid increase in sales volume. Although the average selling price of the Company's quadruped robot products declined year by year due to improvements in process technology, reductions in manufacturing costs, and changes in product mix, sales revenue still achieved relatively strong growth owing to the rapid increase in sales volumes.

In 2023, following the market launch of the Company's first full-size general-purpose humanoid robot H1, the Company completed the sale of 5 humanoid robots in that year, with relatively high unit prices. In 2024, following the market launch of the Company's first mid-size general-purpose humanoid robot G1, humanoid robot product sales entered a phase of rapid growth, with volume growth becoming the primary driver of humanoid robot revenue growth.

3. Primary Sales Model

During the Reporting Period, the Company's products were sold through a combination of offline and online channels, as well as direct sales and distribution channels. The specific revenue composition is as follows:

Item January–September 2025 FY 2024 FY 2023 FY 2022

As shown in the table above, during the Reporting Period, offline sales channels accounted for a relatively high proportion of revenue, consistently exceeding 85%. Within the offline channel, the Company adopted a sales model primarily based on direct sales, supplemented by distribution.

During the Reporting Period, the products sold by the Company to the above customers included humanoid robots, quadruped robots, and robot components. There were no instances in which the proportion of sales to any single customer exceeded 50% of operating revenue. The Company had no related-party relationship with any of the top five customers during the Reporting Period.

IV. Procurement and Major Suppliers

(I) Raw Material Procurement During the Reporting Period

1. Major Raw Material Procurement

The Company's major raw material procurement includes mechanical components, electronic components, electrical materials, packaging materials, and other materials. The primary contents of each category are as follows:

Category Contents
Mechanical Components Machined parts, die-cast parts, fasteners, plastic parts, etc.
Electronic Components Capacitors, resistors, inductors, PCBs, transistors, crystal oscillators, chips, antennas, etc.
Electrical Materials Wires, LEDs, gimbals, batteries, etc.
Basic Materials Metal materials (steel, aluminum, copper, etc.) and non-metallic materials
Packaging Materials Packaging boxes, packaging bags, wooden cases, cartons, etc.
Auxiliary Materials Thermal pads, silicone cloth, insulation sheets, sealing rings, etc.

During the Reporting Period, the specific procurement amounts and their respective proportions of total raw material procurement were as follows:

Unit: RMB 10,000

Item January–September 2025 FY 2024 FY 2023 FY 2022
Amount Proportion Amount Proportion Amount Proportion Amount Proportion
Mechanical Components 24,877.31 47.88% 9,384.04 48.45% 2,815.78 39.91% 3,010.73 40.13%
Electronic Components 13,041.86 25.10% 4,626.81 23.89% 2,249.28 31.88% 2,999.33 39.98%
Electrical Materials 11,444.22 22.03% 4,285.12 22.12% 1,580.10 22.39% 1,240.65 16.54%
Basic Materials 512.19 0.99% 433.27 2.24% 147.06 2.08% 15.56 0.21%
Packaging Materials 1,193.77 2.30% 382.83 1.98% 142.62 2.02% 149.39 1.99%
Auxiliary Materials 888.16 1.71% 256.04 1.32% 120.81 1.71% 86.54 1.15%
Total 51,957.51 100% 19,368.12 100% 7,055.65 100% 7,502.19 100%

During the Reporting Period, as the scale of the Company's business grew rapidly, procurement amounts showed an increasing trend in line with the rapidly growing scale of sales. In each period of the Reporting Period, raw material procurement was primarily comprised of mechanical components, electronic components, and electrical materials. From 2023 to 2024, the proportion of electronic component procurement declined slightly, primarily due to: (1) the Company having procured a moderate level of safety stock in 2022 to address market volatility, and subsequently reducing such safety stock procurement as the market stabilized; and (2) product iteration resulting in reduced demand for certain electronic components.

2. Changes in Prices of Major Raw Materials During the Reporting Period

In the Company's day-to-day procurement activities, the sub-categories and specifications of raw materials comprising the products vary considerably. For example, mechanical components, which account for the largest proportion of procurement, differ significantly depending on the robot product; electronic components include multiple sub-types and models such as PCBs, resistors, capacitors, inductors, transistors, crystal oscillators, and chips. Overall, given the large number of raw material sub-categories and specifications, the changes in the average procurement unit price of major raw material categories cannot adequately reflect changes in raw material prices.

During the Reporting Period, market supply of the Company's major raw materials was sufficient, and all procurement was conducted with reference to market prices, which remained generally stable. The Company's product profit margin levels are aligned with changes in procurement costs and market demand, and changes in procurement prices will not have a material adverse impact on the Company's production and operations.

During the Reporting Period, the proportion of procurement from the top five suppliers as a percentage of total raw material procurement for each period was 18.77%, 22.07%, 26.44%, and 21.72%, respectively. The concentration ratio was relatively low, and there were no instances in which procurement from any single supplier exceeded 50.00% of total procurement. The Company had no related-party relationship with any of its major suppliers during the Reporting Period.

(III) Major Energy Procurement

The primary energy required in the Company's production process is electricity. During the Reporting Period, the Company's electricity procurement in each period was as follows:

Item January–September 2025 FY 2024 FY 2023 FY 2022
Electricity Consumption (10,000 kWh) 272.30 145.43 90.29 66.74
Amount (RMB 10,000) 196.47 171.48 106.38 78.80
Unit Procurement Price (RMB/kWh) 0.72 1.18 1.18 1.18

During the Reporting Period, the Company's electricity consumption increased rapidly, primarily due to increased production requirements resulting from higher order volumes.

In the first three years of the Reporting Period, the Company's average electricity tariff was relatively stable. During January–September 2025, the Company's average electricity tariff declined, primarily because the Company, for operational reasons, newly leased premises at Guanshan Shuzhi Industrial Park during the current period, where electricity tariffs are relatively low. Additionally, the electricity tariff at the Fengda Industrial Park premises leased by the Company also decreased during the current period, resulting in a lower average electricity tariff for the Company.

(IV) Outsourced Processing Procurement

The Company's major products are characterized by long process flows and numerous production stages. Based on its mastery of full-process technology, the Company adopts a strategy of outsourced processing for process stages with high technological maturity and sufficient market supply, in order to optimize resource allocation and improve cost efficiency. During the Reporting Period, the Company's production outsourcing activities were as follows:

No. Process Primary Content of Outsourced Processing
1 SMT (Surface Mount Technology) A series of process steps for processing on PCB substrates, including material placement, programming, and inspection
2 Injection Molding Processing of various components using injection molding machines and molds
3 Surface Treatment Surface treatment of materials through acid washing, oxidation, and other methods
4 Custom Wiring Harness Customization of various wire harnesses used in products, including wire cutting, soldering, glue application, heat shrink tubing, and other processes
5 Winding Winding of wire around motor rotors
6 Machining Processing of hardware component parts using cutting, grinding, and other equipment
7 Others Miscellaneous processes including assembly, welding, rubber coating, cutting, etc.

During the Reporting Period, the procurement amounts for outsourced production processing were RMB 1.6623 million, RMB 2.3881 million, RMB 7.0118 million, and RMB 18.0338 million, respectively, representing 2.46%, 2.72%, 4.15%, and 3.84% of the cost of operating revenue, respectively. Outsourced processes have increased in line with the expansion of the Company's operational scale and product categories.

(II) Intangible Assets

1. Land Use Rights

As of the end of the Reporting Period, the Company and its subsidiaries do not hold any land use rights.

2. Trademarks

As of January 31, 2026, the Company holds 386 registered trademarks in China and 112 registered trademarks in Hong Kong, Macao, Taiwan, and overseas. Details of the domestic trademarks and the primary trademarks registered in countries and regions including the United States, the European Union, the United Kingdom, South Korea, and Japan are set out in "Section XII Appendices" of this Prospectus under "Appendix V: List of Trademarks Held by the Issuer."

3. Patents

As of January 31, 2026, the Company holds 262 patents. The total number of granted and publicly disclosed domestic patents is 169, and overseas patents total 93, comprising 20 domestic invention patents, 76 domestic utility model patents, and 73 domestic design patents. As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, none of the patent rights are subject to pledge, seizure, freezing, or any other restriction on rights. Details are set out in "Section XII Appendices" of this Prospectus under "Appendix VI: List of Patents Held by the Issuer."

Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (宇树科技股份有限公司) — Prospectus

As stipulated in the Framework Strategic Cooperation Agreement, the scope of cooperation includes high-level talent introduction and cultivation, innovative talent training, joint scientific research, and application demonstration. Intellectual property rights shall be determined through negotiation based on the principle of fairness and mutual benefit; ownership of foreground intellectual property will be negotiated based on the specific project funding allocation and task assignment; each party retains ownership of any background intellectual property it contributes to the research institute; and all parties shall observe confidentiality obligations. As of the date of issuance of this Prospectus, no jointly-owned intellectual property has been created among the parties pursuant to the Strategic Cooperation Agreement.

(III) Mechanisms for Sustaining Technological Innovation, Technology Reserves, and Technology Innovation Arrangements

Since its founding, the Company has consistently adhered to the research and development philosophy of full-chain in-house development, guided by market demand in the high-performance general-purpose robotics industry, to lead product innovation and large-scale commercialization. The Company places great emphasis on independent research and development and technological innovation. Through iterative R&D of robot hardware, intelligent systems, and core components, it has accumulated rich technical experience and established industry-leading technology reserves. To date, 12 core technologies have been developed. The Company ensures the continuous output of technological innovation results through multiple means and has made reasonable arrangements for innovation incentive mechanisms and future technology reserves, as follows:

1. Sound Technology R&D System

The Company has established a comprehensive R&D management system and R&D framework. The R&D department, in accordance with the Company's development strategy and plans, and in combination with market demands and industry technology trends, organizes and carries out R&D activities including new technology and new product development, process improvement, and product iteration. During the R&D process, the R&D department, pursuant to the Company's R&D management system, implements full-process management of R&D activities to ensure the efficiency and reliability of technological innovation, laying a solid foundation for the continuous output of the Company's innovative results.

2. Comprehensive Intellectual Property Protection Mechanism

The Company attaches great importance to intellectual property management and protection. It has established dedicated intellectual property management positions specifically responsible for the application and management of the Company's intellectual property rights and for tracking and searching cutting-edge industry technologies, ensuring the efficient operation of the Company's intellectual property and protecting its core technology assets. At the same time, the Company continuously reinforces employees' awareness of confidentiality in its daily management, signs Confidentiality Agreements with all R&D personnel, strictly handles any incidents of information leakage, and eliminates the occurrence of technology leaks to safeguard the Company's core technology security.

3. Effective Talent Team Building and Incentives

The Company focuses on the growth and development of its employees and continuously attracts outstanding external talent. It has established a comprehensive and effective talent cultivation system, dedicated to building a talent team with industry-leading R&D capabilities and an international perspective. The Company adopts an innovation-led incentive model, encouraging employees to research and explore new technologies, new products,

and new applications, so as to ensure the Company's capacity for technological innovation and the continuous output of innovative results.

4. Forward-Looking Technology Reserves

As one of the pioneers in the field of high-performance general-purpose robotics, the Company is committed to building a robotics product ecosystem through technological innovation and driving the technological iteration and industrialization of the global robotics industry. The Company makes technology reserves and R&D layout around the aforementioned vision, aiming to further optimize product performance, expand product varieties, and enhance the Company's core competitiveness. For details on specific ongoing R&D projects and the Company's technology reserves, please refer to "Section VII, (II), 1, Major R&D Projects Currently Underway and Their Progress" of this section.

(IV) Core Technical Personnel and R&D Team

1. Overview of the R&D Team

The Company classifies personnel whose current-period R&D working hours account for no less than 50% of their total working hours in the R&D department, based on the nature of their department, job responsibilities, and participation in R&D projects, as R&D personnel. At the end of each reporting period, the R&D team headcount was as follows:

Unit: Persons
Item End of September 2025 — Headcount End of September 2025 — Percentage End of 2024 — Headcount End of 2024 — Percentage End of 2023 — Headcount End of 2023 — Percentage End of 2022 — Headcount End of 2022 — Percentage
R&D Personnel 175 36.46% 124 37.80% 104 39.39% 72 34.45%
Total Employees 480 100% 328 100% 264 100% 209 100%

The Company attaches importance to technology research and development, and the number of R&D personnel has grown year by year. As of the end of the reporting period, the Company had 175 R&D personnel, accounting for more than 30% of total employees.

At the end of each reporting period, the educational background distribution of the Company's R&D personnel was as follows:

2. Core Technical Personnel

The Company has 3 core technical personnel in total. Their biographical information is set out in "Section IV, Basic Information of the Issuer, XI, (I), 4, Core Technical Personnel" of this Prospectus. The contributions of the Company's core technical personnel to the Company's R&D activities, their professional qualifications, major research achievements, and awards received are as follows:

Wang Xingxing (王兴兴), as the Company's Chief Technology Officer, has made the following major contributions to R&D activities: (1) formulating the Company's technology development strategy and direction, and leading its implementation; (2) presiding over the R&D work of the R&D department; and (3) promoting and advancing key R&D projects and overseeing the R&D process. Wang Xingxing has more than 15 years of experience in robotics R&D. His relevant major achievements and awards include: (1) during his master's studies, he pioneered the development of the XDog quadruped robot — the world's first scheme driven by low-cost outer-rotor brushless motors — which laid the core technological foundation for the Company's product development; (2) he was selected for TIME magazine's 2025 list of the 100 Most Influential People in AI, and was awarded the title of Outstanding Builder of Socialism with Chinese Characteristics among Non-Public Economic Figures at the 6th National Conference, the 2025 Chinese Youth May Fourth Medal, and other honors; he has led over 130 of the Company's patents.

Yang Zhiyu (杨知雨), as the Company's Head of Mechanical Structure, has made the following major contributions to R&D activities: presiding over the design and R&D of robot-related parts, components, and complete machines. Yang Zhiyu has nearly 10 years of experience in robotics mechanical system R&D. His relevant major achievements and awards include: (1) completing the mechanical structure design, core component breakthroughs, and complete machine design for multiple generations of the Company's landmark robot products, achieving a technological breakthrough from zero to one; (2) as the hardware lead, developing multiple robot models that have been successfully deployed at major domestic and international events, including the China Media Group (CMG) Spring Festival Gala, the Hangzhou Asian Games, and the U.S. Super Bowl; he has led over 30 of the Company's patents.

Zhang Yangguang (张阳光), as the Company's Head of Algorithms and Software, has made the following major contributions to R&D activities: presiding over the algorithm and software R&D for robot-related products, including the development and optimization of robot control algorithms, system integration and deployment, and software system development and implementation. Zhang Yangguang has nearly 10 years of experience in motion control algorithm R&D for legged and humanoid robots. His relevant major achievements and awards include: (1) completing, participating in, and managing the motion control algorithm R&D and iterative upgrades for the Company's full range of core products, and leading key technology breakthroughs and performance improvements in areas such as "stable walking on complex and rugged terrain," "high-speed running," "complex dance movements," "aerial somersaults," and "full-body arbitrary stable movements" for humanoid robots; (2) as the Head of Algorithms and Software, successfully completing multiple major events including the CMG Spring Festival Gala and the U.S. Super Bowl, with related control algorithms successfully applied in numerous significant domestic and international settings; he has led over 10 of the Company's patents and software copyrights.

3. Binding and Incentive Measures

The Company has implemented effective incentive measures for core technical personnel; core technical personnel hold shares in the Company directly or indirectly. At the same time, the Company has established a sound R&D process and intellectual property protection system, and has signed confidentiality agreements with relevant technical personnel to guard against the risks of technology leakage and talent attrition.

4. Major Changes

The Company has 3 core technical personnel in total. No material changes occurred during the reporting period.

(V) Scientific Research Strength and Achievements

The Company has actively promoted the R&D of core technologies and product iteration, and has received a number of important domestic and international awards and technology platform accreditations. Details are as follows:

VIII. Work Safety and Environmental Protection

(I) Environmental Protection

The Company is focused on the R&D, production, and sales of high-performance general-purpose humanoid robots, quadruped robots, robot components, and embodied intelligence models. Pursuant to the National Economic Industry Classification (GB/T4754-2017), the Company's business falls under "C34 General Equipment Manufacturing" — "C349 Other General Equipment Manufacturing" — "C3499 Other General Equipment Manufacturing Not Elsewhere Classified." The Company's industry does not belong to the heavily polluting industries specified in the Notice on Issuing the Measures for Environmental Credit Assessment of Enterprises (Trial) (Huan Fa [2013] No. 150).

As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, the environmental permits and qualifications obtained by the Company are as follows:

No. Certificate Name Certificate Number
1 Fixed Pollution Source Discharge Registration Receipt 91330108MA27YJ5H56001X
2 Fixed Pollution Source Discharge Registration Receipt 91330108MA27YJ5H56003W

The Company's production model is primarily assembly-based production; the production process flow is detailed in "Section I, (V), Flow Chart of Main Products or Services" of this section. The main pollutants generated during the Company's daily business operations are waste gas, wastewater, and solid waste. Waste gas is primarily generated by welding, coating, and adhesive bonding processes in the assembly workshop; wastewater is primarily domestic sewage and cleaning wastewater; and solid waste is primarily general waste packaging materials. The relevant environmental protection and treatment measures are effective. According to the enterprise credit report provided by the Hangzhou Public Credit Information Platform, as of the end of the reporting period, the Company had no violations of laws or regulations in the field of ecological environment.

(II) Work Safety

The Company is focused on the R&D, production, and sales of high-performance general-purpose humanoid robots, quadruped robots, robot components, and embodied intelligence models, and does not belong to a high-risk industry.

During the reporting period, the Company did not engage in any illegal or non-compliant acts in work safety during production and operations, did not experience any major safety accidents, and did not receive any administrative penalties related to work safety. The Company's principal business and production operations comply with the requirements of work safety regulations.

IX. Overseas Operations of the Issuer

During the reporting period, the Company's overseas sales involved customers in the United States, Europe, Japan, and other regions. As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, the Company's Hong Kong subsidiary, UNITREE LIMITED, has not yet commenced operations.
