During the reporting period, the net non-recurring gains and losses attributable to shareholders of the parent company were RMB -14,029.70 thousand, RMB 6,873.90 thousand, RMB 16,998.20 thousand, and RMB -325,280.80 thousand, respectively. The negative net non-recurring gains and losses attributable to shareholders of the parent company in 2022 and January to September 2025 were primarily attributable to the recognition of share-based payment expenses of RMB 18,722.60 thousand and RMB 349,065.50 thousand, respectively, arising from the two capital contributions made by Shanghai Yuyi (上海宇翼) into the Company in 2022 and 2025. The accounting treatment of such share-based payment expenses does not involve any cash outflow from the Company and, although it has an impact on net profit during the reporting period, it does not alter the net profit attributable to owners of the parent company after deduction of non-recurring gains and losses, nor the cash flow position.

The Company's operating results have grown rapidly, and the Company is not materially dependent on non-recurring gains and losses.

VI. Taxation

(I) Major Types of Taxes and Tax Rates

Type of Tax Tax Base Tax Rate
Value-Added Tax (VAT) Taxable sales revenue 6%, 13%
Enterprise Income Tax Taxable income 8.25%, 15%, 16.50%, 20%
Urban Maintenance and Construction Tax Turnover taxes payable 5%, 7%
Education Surcharge Turnover taxes payable 3%
Local Education Surcharge Turnover taxes payable 2%

During the reporting period, the enterprise income tax rates applicable to each taxpaying entity within the Company's consolidation scope are as follows:

Name of Taxpaying Entity Enterprise Income Tax Rate
Issuer 15%
Hong Kong Unitree (香港宇树)* 8.25%, 16.50%
Shanghai Gaoyi (上海高羿) 20%
Beijing Lingyi (北京灵翌) 20%
Shenzhen Tianyi (深圳天羿) 20%
Unitree Robot (宇树机器人) 20%

*Note: Pursuant to the Inland Revenue (Amendment) (No. 3) Ordinance 2018 issued by the Hong Kong Inland Revenue Department, companies registered in Hong Kong have been subject to a two-tiered profits tax rate system for years of assessment commencing on or after April 1, 2018, under which the first HKD 2,000,000 of assessable profits is taxed at 8.25% and assessable profits exceeding HKD 2,000,000 are taxed at 16.50%.

(II) Tax Incentives

1. Tax Incentive Policies

(1) Value-Added Tax

Pursuant to Several Policies to Further Encourage the Development of the Software Industry and the Integrated Circuit Industry (Guo Fa [2011] No. 4) issued by the State Council and the Notice of the Ministry of Finance and the State Administration of Taxation on VAT Policies for Software Products (Cai Shui [2011] No. 100), for self-developed and self-produced software products sold by the Company, the portion of the actual VAT burden exceeding 3% after VAT is levied at the statutory rate is subject to an immediate rebate upon collection policy.

Pursuant to the Announcement on the VAT Additional Credit Deduction Policy for Advanced Manufacturing Enterprises (Announcement No. 43 of 2023 of the Ministry of Finance and the State Taxation Administration), from January 1, 2023 to December 31, 2027, advanced manufacturing enterprises are permitted to deduct an additional 5% of their current period's creditable input VAT against their VAT payable. The Company has enjoyed the preferential policy of an additional 5% credit deduction against creditable input VAT since 2023.

(2) Enterprise Income Tax

1) High-and-New-Technology Enterprise Preferential Treatment

The Company obtained the High-and-New-Technology Enterprise Certificate No. GR202233005178 on December 24, 2022, valid for three years. The Company obtained the High-and-New-Technology Enterprise Certificate No. GR202533001609 on December 19, 2025, valid for three years. Pursuant to the Enterprise Income Tax Law of the People's Republic of China and the Administrative Measures for the Recognition of High-and-New-Technology Enterprises, "high-and-new-technology enterprises that the State needs to give key support shall pay enterprise income tax at a reduced rate of 15%."

2) Small and Low-Profit Enterprise Preferential Treatment

Pursuant to the Announcement on Tax and Fee Policies to Further Support the Development of Small and Micro Enterprises and Individual Industrial and Commercial Households (No. 12 of 2023) issued by the Ministry of Finance and the State Taxation Administration, the policy of reducing the taxable income of small and low-profit enterprises to 25% of their actual taxable income, taxed at a rate of 20%, has been extended to December 31, 2027. Pursuant to the Announcement on the Implementation of Income Tax Preferential Policies for Small and Micro Enterprises and Individual Industrial and Commercial Households (No. 12 of 2021), from January 1, 2022 to December 31, 2022, for the portion of the annual taxable income of small and low-profit enterprises not exceeding RMB 1,000,000, the taxable income is reduced to 12.5% and taxed at a rate of 20%. Pursuant to the Announcement on Further Implementing Preferential Income Tax Policies for Small and Micro Enterprises (No. 13 of 2022) issued by the Ministry of Finance and the State Taxation Administration, from January 1, 2022 to December 31, 2024, for the portion of the annual taxable income of small and low-profit enterprises exceeding RMB 1,000,000 but not exceeding RMB 3,000,000, the taxable income is reduced to 25% and taxed at a rate of 20%. During the years 2022, 2023, 2024, and the period January to September 2025, the Company's subsidiary Hangzhou Unitree Robotics Co., Ltd. (杭州宇树机器人有限公司) enjoyed the above preferential policies; during January to September 2025, the Company's subsidiaries Shanghai Gaoyi Technology Co., Ltd. (上海高羿科技有限公司), Beijing Lingyi Technology Co., Ltd. (北京灵翌科技有限公司), and Shenzhen Tianyi Technology Co., Ltd. (深圳天羿科技有限公司) enjoyed the above preferential policies.

(3) Other Tax Incentive Policies

In addition to the tax incentive policies described above, the Company also enjoys preferential tax policies for the additional deduction of research and development expenses incurred in developing new technologies, new products, and new processes, as well as export tax rebates and excess input tax credit refund policies.

2. Impact of Tax Incentive Policies on the Company's Operating Results

During the reporting period, the proportion of tax incentives to the Company's total profit is as follows:

Unit: RMB 10,000

Item January–September 2025 Year 2024 Year 2023 Year 2022
Impact of high-and-new-technology enterprise income tax incentive 5,219.73 991.49 - -
Impact of additional deduction for R&D expenses 1,284.74 1,009.08 715.26 425.59
Impact of VAT immediate rebate upon collection 1,065.01 595.96 233.90 220.20
Impact of small and micro enterprise income tax incentive 22.13 - - -
Total tax incentives 7,591.61 2,596.53 949.16 645.78
Total profit 17,103.59 9,947.32 -2,140.87 -2,772.14
Tax incentives as a percentage of total profit 44.39% 26.10% -44.34% -23.30%

During the reporting period, the tax incentives enjoyed by the Company primarily consisted of the high-and-new-technology enterprise income tax incentive, the additional deduction for R&D expenses, and the VAT immediate rebate upon collection for software products. The high-and-new-technology enterprise tax incentive policy is a long-term policy implemented by the State to encourage the development of high-and-new-technology enterprises. The Company has a stable R&D team and a sufficient number of R&D personnel with strong technological research and development capabilities, and has considerable certainty in continuously satisfying the requirements for high-and-new-technology enterprise recognition. The policies for the additional deduction of R&D expenses and the VAT immediate rebate upon collection for software products are long-term preferential tax policies implemented by the State to support the healthy development of the real economy; these policies have relatively certain expectations and are continuous in nature. Accordingly, the Company's tax incentives are stable and sustainable and do not have a material adverse impact on the Company's operations.

(I) Basic Financial Indicators

Financial Indicator 30 September 2025 31 December 2024 31 December 2023 31 December 2022
Current ratio (times) 6.36 5.34 3.85 7.17
Quick ratio (times) 5.65 4.75 2.92 5.44
Asset-to-liability ratio (parent company) 14.94% 16.20% 23.57% 15.43%
Asset-to-liability ratio (consolidated) 15.17% 16.20% 23.57% 15.43%
Net assets per share attributable to shareholders of the issuer (RMB/share) 6.68 495.08 155.36 160.89
Financial Indicator January–September 2025 Year 2024 Year 2023 Year 2022
Accounts receivable turnover rate (times/year) 22.23 24.03 16.83 17.88
Inventory turnover rate (times/year) 2.24 1.54 1.12 1.08
EBITDA (RMB 10,000) 16,945.55 9,445.20 -1,874.65 -2,391.11
Interest coverage ratio (times) 212.71 222.66 -26.75 -41.95
Net profit attributable to shareholders of the parent company (RMB 10,000) 10,533.14 9,450.18 -1,114.51 -2,210.05
Net profit attributable to shareholders of the issuer after deduction of non-recurring gains and losses (RMB 10,000) 43,061.23 7,750.36 -1,801.91 -807.08
R&D expenditure as a percentage of revenue 7.73% 17.84% 31.39% 24.39%
Net cash flows from operating activities per share (RMB/share) 1.17 74.44 2.57 -15.69
Net cash flows per share (RMB/share) 3.38 132.87 45.96 42.86

Note: The calculation formulas for the above financial indicators are as follows, and the same calculation method applies in the subsequent text:

  • (1) Current ratio = Current assets / Current liabilities
  • (2) Quick ratio = (Current assets − Inventories) / Current liabilities
  • (3) Asset-to-liability ratio = (Total liabilities / Total assets) × 100%
  • (4) Net assets per share attributable to shareholders of the parent company = Net assets attributable to shareholders of the parent company / Total shares outstanding at end of period
  • (5) Accounts receivable turnover rate = Revenue / ((Beginning accounts receivable balance + Ending accounts receivable balance) / 2); this indicator for January–September 2025 has not been annualized
  • (6) Inventory turnover rate = Operating costs / ((Beginning inventory book value + Ending inventory book value) / 2); this indicator for January–September 2025 has not been annualized
  • (7) EBITDA = Net profit + Income tax expense + (Interest expenses − Interest income) + Depreciation of fixed assets + Depreciation of right-of-use assets + Amortization of intangible assets + Amortization of long-term deferred expenses
  • (8) Interest coverage ratio = (Total profit + Interest expenses) / Interest expenses
  • (9) R&D expenditure as a percentage of revenue = R&D expenses / Revenue
  • (10) Net cash flows from operating activities per share = Net cash flows from operating activities / Total shares outstanding at end of period
  • (11) Net cash flows per share = Net increase in cash and cash equivalents / Total shares outstanding at end of period

Net profit attributable to shareholders of the issuer after deduction of non-recurring gains and losses 43,061.23 7,750.36 -1,801.91 -807.08

With independent R&D as its core, the Company has, through years of accumulation, established a self-developed and self-manufactured operating system covering robot bodies, core intelligent algorithms, embodied intelligence, and core components, forming robot body products represented by humanoid robots and quadruped robots, as well as robot component products including dexterous hands, collaborative robotic arms, lidar, joint motors, and other products, and has carried out market promotion and industry applications around such products. During the reporting period, the commercialization of robots has continued to accelerate and downstream market demand has grown rapidly. The Company has actively promoted core technology R&D and product iteration, leading innovation and large-scale deployment in the high-performance general-purpose robotics industry, and has captured major market share globally in quadruped robots and humanoid robots. During the reporting period, the Company's revenue from principal business operations achieved rapid growth, with a compound annual growth rate of as high as 78.73% from 2022 to 2024. During January to September 2025, the Company's revenue from principal business operations and net profit attributable to shareholders of the parent company after deduction of non-recurring gains and losses had both exceeded the full-year levels for 2024.

Details of the sales growth of the Company's major products are set out in Section VIII, (I), 1, "Analysis of the Composition and Changes in Revenue" of this section.

(I) Analysis of Revenue

1. Analysis of the Composition and Changes in Revenue

During the reporting period, the composition of the Company's revenue is as follows:

Unit: RMB 10,000

Item January–September 2025 Year 2024 Year 2023 Year 2022
Amount Proportion Amount Proportion Amount Proportion Amount Proportion
Revenue from principal business operations 115,494.95 98.93% 38,727.28 98.70% 15,753.65 99.00% 12,122.77 98.62%

During the reporting period, the Company's revenue was primarily derived from principal business operations including humanoid robots, quadruped robots, and robot components. The Company's principal business is prominent, with the proportion exceeding 98% in each period. Other operating revenue primarily comprises rental income, technical service income, and the like, which were of small amounts and low proportions in each period.

2. Analysis of the Composition of Revenue from Principal Business Operations

(1) Analysis of Revenue from Principal Business Operations by Product Category

During the reporting period, the composition of the Company's revenue from principal business operations by product category is as follows:

Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (宇树科技股份有限公司) — Prospectus

continuously iterated and updated products within the reporting period, and persistently optimized product performance. New products such as B1, Go2, B2, and B2-W were successively launched, further driving growth in sales revenue.

During the reporting period, the sales of the Company's quadruped robot products were as follows:

The contribution of the above factor changes to the growth in sales revenue of the Company's quadruped robots is as follows:

Unit: RMB 10,000

Item January–September 2025 FY2024 FY2023
Amount Contribution Amount Contribution Amount Contribution
Impact of volume change 34,924.01 135.66% 15,357.71 138.16% 2,773.51 104.44%

Total impact 25,744.18 100% 11,116.28 100% 2,655.71 100%

Note: Impact of unit price change = (current period average unit price – prior period average unit price) × current period sales volume; Impact of volume change = (current period sales volume – prior period sales volume) × prior period average unit price. The same applies below.

As shown in the table above, the revenue growth of the Company's quadruped robots during the reporting period was primarily driven by the rapid increase in sales volume. Although the average selling price of the Company's quadruped robots has declined year-by-year as a result of improved manufacturing processes, reduced production costs, and optimized product mix, sales revenue still achieved relatively rapid growth due to the rapid increase in sales volume.

2) Humanoid Robots

Since the Company independently developed and mass-produced its first full-size general-purpose humanoid robot H1 in 2023, humanoid robot sales revenue grew rapidly from 2023 through January–September 2025, reaching RMB 2.9671 million, RMB 106.8976 million, and RMB 595.1879 million, respectively, with the share of the Company's main business rising from 1.88% in 2023 to 51.53% in January–September 2025.

The rapid growth of the Company's humanoid robots was likewise driven by both internal and external factors. On the external side, the rapid development of artificial intelligence has promoted technological progress in general-purpose humanoid robots, driving R&D investment, application exploration, and market attention in humanoid robotics, and stimulating product demand from scientific research and education, commercial and consumer, and industry application sectors. On the internal side, the Company has continuously increased R&D investment and taken the lead in developing and mass-producing multiple humanoid robot products, continuously enriching its product portfolio, and establishing first-mover advantages and core competitive strengths. The products have gained wide recognition and adoption from both domestic and overseas customers, driving rapid growth in product sales.

During the reporting period, the sales of the Company's humanoid robot products were as follows:

Item January–September 2025 FY2024 FY2023
Amount Change Amount Change Amount
Sales Volume (units) 3,551 1,054.80% 410 8,100.00% 5
Average Unit Price (RMB 10,000/unit) 16.76 -35.71% 26.07 -56.06% 59.34
Sales Revenue (RMB 10,000) 59,518.79 642.38% 10,689.76 3,502.76% 296.71

Note: The changes in sales volume and sales revenue for January–September 2025 are calculated based on annualized data.

The contribution of the above factors to the growth in sales revenue of the Company's humanoid robots is as follows:

Unit: RMB 10,000

Item January–September 2025 FY2024
Amount Contribution Amount Contribution
Impact of volume change 81,893.99 167.72% 24,033.49 231.25%
Impact of unit price change -33,064.96 -67.72% -13,640.44 -131.25%
Total impact 48,829.03 100% 10,393.05 100%

In 2023, following the launch of the Company's first full-size general-purpose humanoid robot H1, the Company completed sales of 5 humanoid robots during the year at a relatively high unit selling price. In 2024, following the launch of the Company's first mid-size general-purpose humanoid robot G1, humanoid robot product sales entered a phase of rapid growth, and sales volume growth became the primary driver of the Company's humanoid robot sales revenue growth.

Meanwhile, the decline in the average selling price of the Company's humanoid robots since 2024 is primarily attributable to two factors: product mix and technological advancement. In terms of product mix, the Company's second humanoid robot G1, mass-produced in 2024, is a mid-size humanoid robot with a lower cost and selling price than the full-size model H1; the growth in G1 sales volume has pulled down the overall average selling price of the Company's humanoid robots. In terms of technological advancement, with continuous improvements in manufacturing processes and ongoing optimization of cost structures, the unit cost of the Company's humanoid robots has also achieved steady declines. On this basis, in order to further consolidate the Company's industry position and build long-term competitive advantages, the Company appropriately lowered product pricing in 2025 after comprehensively evaluating the market development of humanoid robots, cost optimization trends, and profit margins.

3) Robot Components

During each period of the reporting period, the Company's sales revenue from robot components was RMB 21.4142 million, RMB 26.9234 million, RMB 44.5382 million, and RMB 66.5389 million, respectively, comprising primarily robotic arms, 4D LiDAR, and various other robot components. During the reporting period, the sales revenue from the aforementioned robot components increased correspondingly with the growth in robot product revenue.

4) Others

During each period of the reporting period, revenue from other products and services such as fitness pumps was RMB 6.9897 million, RMB 8.2651 million, RMB 5.2933 million, and RMB 5.2372 million, respectively, with relatively small amounts and proportions.

(2) Analysis of the Geographic Composition of Main Business Revenue

During the reporting period, the geographic composition of the Company's main business revenue was as follows:

Unit: RMB 10,000

Item January–September 2025 FY2024 FY2023 FY2022
Amount % of Total Amount % of Total Amount % of Total Amount % of Total

From 2022 to 2024, the Company's overseas revenue accounted for a slightly higher proportion than domestic sales, reflecting a high degree of recognition for the Company's products in overseas markets. In January–September 2025, the proportion of domestic revenue increased significantly to 60.80%, primarily because: benefiting from the Chinese New Year Spring Festival Gala performance at the beginning of 2025, the Company's brand recognition and visibility continued to grow nationwide; at the same time, since 2025, the rapid development of the domestic artificial intelligence and robotics industry, along with policy support, has promoted procurement demand and application development for general-purpose robots among customers in scientific research and education, commercial and consumer, and industry application sectors, driving a significant increase in the Company's domestic sales revenue.

During the reporting period, with the continued growth of the Company's business scale and the successive introduction of new products, quarterly revenue has shown an overall upward trend. At the same time, since most new products begin generating revenue in the second half of the year and start generating revenue at scale in Q4 of the current year, and certain customers implement centralized procurement and budget management systems whereby procurement activities or project acceptances tend to occur in Q4, the proportion of Q4 revenue in each year has been relatively high.

3. Analysis of Main Business Costs by Cost Element

During the reporting period, the composition of the Company's main business costs by cost element was as follows:

Unit: RMB 10,000

Item January–September 2025 FY2024 FY2023 FY2022
Amount % of Total Amount % of Total Amount % of Total Amount % of Total
Direct Materials 37,518.66 80.12% 12,258.78 72.62% 6,403.99 72.87% 5,005.33 73.97%
Direct Labor 3,763.71 8.04% 2,143.02 12.70% 1,227.74 13.97% 697.94 10.31%
Manufacturing Overhead 5,547.18 11.85% 2,477.88 14.68% 1,156.18 13.16% 1,063.60 15.72%
Total 46,829.55 100% 16,879.68 100.00% 8,787.90 100.00% 6,766.87 100.00%

The Company's main business costs consist of direct materials, direct labor, and manufacturing overhead. During the reporting period, each cost category showed an overall growth trend consistent with the year-on-year growth in business scale.

From 2022 to 2024, the composition of the Company's main business costs by cost element remained relatively stable. In January–September 2025, the proportion of direct materials costs increased significantly compared to 2024, while the proportions of direct labor and manufacturing overhead declined. The primary reasons are: (1) as the Company's business scale grew rapidly, economies of scale became increasingly apparent; at the same time, the Company continuously improved production efficiency by strengthening the standardization of production processes and worker proficiency; (2) the Company's humanoid robot business expanded rapidly, with its share of revenue rising from 27.60% in 2024 to 51.53% in January–September 2025. Since humanoid robot products have a relatively high proportion of material costs, the increase in their revenue share drove up the overall proportion of direct materials in the Company's main business costs.

In summary, the changes in the composition of the Company's main business costs by cost element during the reporting period are consistent with the Company's actual business conditions and are reasonable.

During the reporting period, the Company's gross profit was primarily derived from main business activities and showed a growth trend. The Company has a focused main business and good profitability.

Category January–September 2025 FY2024 FY2023 FY2022

During the reporting period, the Company's main business gross profit grew rapidly, consistent with the trend in operating revenue growth. The Company's main business gross profit is primarily derived from humanoid robots and quadruped robots. Quadruped robot gross profit was RMB 39.3161 million, RMB 52.1821 million, RMB 118.7323 million, and RMB 270.8020 million, respectively, accounting for 73.41%, 74.91%, 54.35%, and 39.44%, respectively; humanoid robot gross profit was RMB 0, RMB 2.6012 million, RMB 73.1563 million, and RMB 374.4299 million, respectively, accounting for 0%, 3.73%, 33.48%, and 54.53%, respectively. Since the Company's first general-purpose humanoid robot H1 was launched in 2023, humanoid robots rapidly became one of the Company's main products, with their share of sales revenue increasing year by year, and their share of gross profit growing correspondingly and substantially.

2. Analysis of Main Business Gross Margin

During the reporting period, the Company's main business gross margin was as follows:

Item January–September 2025 FY2024 FY2023 FY2022
Quadruped Robots 55.49% 51.50% 43.71% 42.36%
Humanoid Robots 62.91% 68.44% 87.67% —
Robot Components 60.43% 56.94% 48.76% 58.94%
Others 23.19% 23.23% 21.11% 23.20%
Total 59.45% 56.41% 44.22% 44.18%

During the reporting period, the Company's main business gross margin showed an overall upward trend. Specific analysis is provided below.

(1) Quadruped Robots

During each period of the reporting period, the gross margin of the Company's quadruped robots was 42.36%, 43.71%, 51.50%, and 55.49%, respectively, showing an overall upward trend. The primary reasons are: on the one hand, the Company achieved significant production cost reductions through continuous improvements in manufacturing processes, and also realized certain procurement cost reductions as production and sales volumes increased, resulting in a declining trend in unit costs for major products during the reporting period; on the other hand, differences in product mix across reporting periods caused differences in gross margin. As industry-grade quadruped robots such as B2 and B2-W were mass-produced and their share of revenue increased, the overall gross margin of quadruped robots also increased accordingly.

(2) Humanoid Robots

From 2023 to January–September 2025, the gross margin of the Company's humanoid robots was 87.67%, 68.44%, and 62.91%, respectively. Because certain components of the Company's humanoid robots are interchangeable with those of quadruped robots, the humanoid robots have a certain cost advantage, resulting in relatively high overall gross margins.

In 2023, the Company sold 5 units of the initial version of the H1 product; at that time, commercial humanoid robots were relatively scarce, so the selling price and gross margin of that batch of products were both high and are not comparable with subsequent years. In January–September 2025, the gross margin of humanoid robots declined somewhat. The primary reasons are: on the one hand, the Company independently developed and launched the G1 mid-size humanoid robot in May 2024, which quickly became the primary sales model. Since G1, as a mid-size humanoid robot, has a lower unit price, unit cost, and gross margin than the full-size H1, it pulled down the overall gross margin of the Company's humanoid robots. On the other hand, the Company continued to optimize product unit costs through production and procurement cost reductions; on this basis, in order to further consolidate the Company's industry position and build long-term competitive advantages, the Company appropriately lowered product selling prices in 2025 after comprehensively evaluating the market development of humanoid robots, cost optimization trends, and profit margins.

(3) Robot Components

During each period of the reporting period, the gross margin of the Company's robot components was 58.94%, 48.76%, 56.94%, and 60.43%, respectively. The Company's robot components include key core components such as robotic arms, 4D LiDAR, and high-computing-power modules for both quadruped robots and humanoid robots. Different components have different gross margins, and changes in the product mix across reporting periods have resulted in fluctuations in the gross margin of robot components.

3. Comparative Analysis of Gross Margin with Listed Peers

The Company is primarily engaged in the R&D, manufacturing, and sales of high-performance general-purpose humanoid robots, quadruped robots, robot components, and embodied intelligence models. Currently, there are no listed companies on the A-share market in the general-purpose robotics sector. The Company has selected comparable companies from among humanoid robot-related listed companies on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. The main products and business activities of the relevant companies are as follows:

Company Abbreviation Main Business Main Products Application Sectors
UBTECH Robotics (优必选) Primarily engaged in intelligent service robots and intelligent robot solution businesses, including humanoid robot products Educational intelligent robots, logistics intelligent robots, humanoid and commercial service robots, as well as intelligent lawn mowers, pool cleaning robots, floor-sweeping robots, and other consumer-grade robots and other hardware products Education, logistics, household consumer use, etc.
Dobot (越疆) Primarily engaged in the design, development, manufacturing, and commercialization of collaborative robots Six-axis collaborative robots, four-axis collaborative robots, composite robots, etc. Manufacturing, retail, medical and healthcare, educational research, etc.

During the reporting period, the comparison of the Company's overall gross margin with those of listed peers was as follows:

Listed Company January–September 2025 FY2024 FY2023 FY2022
UBTECH Robotics (优必选) — 28.65% 31.53% 29.16%
Dobot (越疆) — 46.56% 43.54% 40.75%
Average — 37.61% 37.54% 34.96%
The Company 59.83% 56.98% 44.75% 44.94%

Note: Listed peers UBTECH Robotics (优必选) and Dobot (越疆) have not yet announced their Q3 2025 financial data. The same applies below.

During the reporting period, the Company's overall gross margin was higher than that of listed peers.

On the one hand, there are differences in product composition and application scenarios between the Company and its listed peers. Among the listed peers, UBTECH's robots are primarily educational intelligent robots, logistics intelligent robots, and household consumer-grade products, mainly applied in education, logistics, and household consumer use; Dobot's robots are primarily collaborative robots, mainly applied in manufacturing, retail, medical and healthcare, STEAM education, and scientific research scenarios. Both the types of robots and their application sectors differ from the Company's, resulting in differences in gross margins.

On the other hand, the Company's cost control capabilities are at the leading edge of the industry. Since its founding, the Company has adhered to a full-stack, self-developed technology approach covering both complete robot systems and core components, and was the first to adopt motor-driven technology in the field of high-performance general-purpose robots. Drawing on the deep expertise of its core technology team, the Company independently developed motor drives, the overall mechanical structure of the robot, and whole-body control systems, significantly improving overall performance and product reliability, resulting in advantages such as high control precision, fast response speed, low operating noise, and easy maintenance, while significantly reducing hardware costs. In terms of cost control, the Company's self-development and self-manufacture of core components has created vertical integration capability over the supply chain, which not only ensures rapid product and technology iteration, but also significantly reduces material procurement and manufacturing costs. At the same time, large-scale mass production has further strengthened the Company's bargaining power with upstream suppliers, creating a sustained cost advantage.

(IV) Analysis of Period Expenses

During each period of the reporting period, the Company's period expenses were as follows:

During each period of the reporting period, the Company's total period expenses were RMB 83.3067 million, RMB 93.6733 million, RMB 137.0059 million, and RMB 545.5908 million, respectively, accounting for 67.77%, 58.86%, 34.92%, and 46.73% of operating revenue for the same period, respectively. From 2022 to 2024, as the Company's business scale and operating revenue grew rapidly, the period expense ratio showed a declining trend, and the economies of scale of the Company's operations began to emerge. In January–September 2025, the Company's period expense ratio increased, primarily because the share-based payment expenses recognized in the current period were relatively high.

1. Selling Expenses

(1) Composition of Selling Expenses

During the reporting period, the specific composition of the Company's selling expenses was as follows:

Unit: RMB 10,000

Item January–September 2025 FY2024 FY2023 FY2022
Amount % of Total Amount % of Total Amount % of Total Amount % of Total
Staff Remuneration 3,617.32 47.59% 2,880.40 48.69% 1,675.89 44.43% 1,086.22 42.04%
Advertising and Business Promotion Expenses 2,257.28 29.70% 1,729.97 29.24% 1,331.17 35.29% 1,085.67 42.02%
Platform and Intermediary Service Fees 879.99 11.58% 697.91 11.80% 197.50 5.24% 119.31 4.62%
Travel Expenses 520.12 6.84% 421.91 7.13% 351.95 9.33% 101.87 3.94%
Depreciation and Amortization 79.55 1.05% 71.83 1.21% 65.00 1.72% 44.82 1.73%

Total 7,600.85 100.00% 5,915.85 100.00% 3,771.83 100.00% 2,583.68 100.00%

During each period of the reporting period, the Company's selling expenses were RMB 25.8368 million, RMB 37.7183 million, RMB 59.1585 million, and RMB 76.0085 million, respectively, accounting for 21.02%, 23.70%, 15.08%, and 6.51% of operating revenue, respectively. The Company's selling expenses consist primarily of staff remuneration, advertising and business promotion expenses, and platform and intermediary service fees. Advertising and business promotion expenses primarily comprise costs incurred from placing advertisements on online platforms and digital media. Platform and intermediary service fees primarily comprise operation and maintenance service fees charged by online platforms and sales commissions.

During the reporting period, the Company's selling expenses showed an overall growth trend, primarily because: 1) the Company actively developed and maintained markets, expanding its sales headcount by recruiting personnel with industry experience; 2) the Company continued to increase investment in advertising and business promotion expenses to strengthen market and customer coverage and promote sales conversion; 3) as the Company's revenue scale grew rapidly, platform and intermediary service fees increased accordingly; 4) the Company's sales activities continued to increase, and related travel expenses continued to grow. In 2024 and January–September 2025, as the Company's operating revenue grew rapidly, the Company's selling expense ratio declined sharply, with economies of scale becoming increasingly apparent.

(2) Comparison of Selling Expense Ratio with Listed Peers

During the reporting period, the comparison of the Company's selling expense ratio with those of listed peers was as follows:

Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (宇树科技股份有限公司) — Prospectus

Item January–September 2025 FY 2024 FY 2023 FY 2022
Investment income from long-term equity investments accounted for under the equity method -0.91 — — —
Gains from changes in fair value -24.28 95.42 -5.66 5.66
   Of which: Trading financial assets -24.28 95.42 -5.66 5.66

During the reporting period, the Company's investment income primarily comprised: (1) investment income recognized upon the disposal of wealth management products; and (2) interest income earned during the holding period of debt investments, as the Company purchased large-denomination bank certificates of deposit with temporarily idle funds, which were classified as debt investments.

During the reporting period, gains from changes in fair value represent the floating returns on wealth management products held. The decrease in FY 2023 was mainly attributable to the Company's disposal of wealth management products during the year, upon which the cumulative gains from changes in fair value were transferred to investment income.

Item January–September 2025 FY 2024 FY 2023 FY 2022
Credit impairment losses -350.34 -113.53 -61.82 -17.18
   Of which: Bad debt losses on accounts receivable -283.76 -74.09 -26.18 -11.80
   Bad debt losses on other receivables -66.58 -39.44 -35.64 -5.38
Asset impairment losses -1,304.16 -1,037.85 -840.56 -653.64
   Of which: Inventory write-down losses and impairment losses on costs to fulfill contracts -1,300.89 -1,037.65 -839.87 -652.37
   Impairment losses on contract assets -3.27 -0.20 -0.69 -1.27

During the reporting period, the Company's credit impairment losses increased, primarily because the growth in revenue scale led to an increase in accounts receivable, resulting in a corresponding increase in bad debt provisions. Asset impairment losses showed an overall upward trend, primarily because the scale of inventories continued to rise alongside revenue growth, resulting in a corresponding increase in inventory write-down losses and impairment losses on costs to fulfill contracts.

4. Non-Operating Income

During the reporting period, the Company's non-operating income amounted to RMB 496,200, RMB 60,800, RMB 70,200, and RMB 733,700, respectively, with a relatively minor impact on total profit. Among these, non-operating income in FY 2022 was primarily prize money of RMB 470,000 received by the Company from participating in robotics-related competitions; non-operating income for the period from January to September 2025 was primarily gains from forfeitures and confiscations.

5. Non-Operating Expenses

During the reporting period, the Company's non-operating expenses amounted to RMB 57,900, RMB 102,600, RMB 154,900, and RMB 291,100, respectively, with a relatively minor impact on total profit, primarily comprising sponsorship expenditures.

Note: Data for UBTECH Robotics covers the period from 2022 to January–June 2025; Dobot has not disclosed provision rates for accounts receivable for January–September 2025, and the range in the table above covers 2022 to 2024.

During the reporting period, the Company and its peer listed companies classified their accounts receivable portfolios using different methods. UBTECH Robotics classifies accounts receivable portfolios based on whether they are overdue and the overdue period; Dobot classifies accounts receivable portfolios based on whether they are overdue and the aging of overdue accounts receivable; while the Company classifies accounts receivable portfolios based on aging and determines the corresponding provision rates accordingly. The Company's accounts receivable are predominantly within 1 year of aging, and the bad debt provision rate for this aging bracket is close to that of Dobot.

A comparison of the expected credit loss provision rates on accounts receivable between the Company and its peer listed companies is as follows:

(5) Prepayments

At the end of each period during the reporting period, the carrying amounts of the Company's prepayments were RMB 2,797,100, RMB 3,583,500, RMB 8,307,800, and RMB 13,713,200, respectively, each accounting for less than 1% of total assets in the respective period, representing a relatively small proportion. The Company's prepayments primarily consist of advance payments for raw material purchases. During the most recent year and period, as the business scale grew rapidly, the Company's procurement amounts showed a rapid upward trend, and accordingly, the balance of prepayments grew rapidly.
