Section 7: Use of Proceeds and Future Development Plans

▶

I. Basic Overview of the Use of Proceeds

(I) Overview of Investment Projects Funded by the Proceeds

The Company proposes to publicly issue no fewer than 40,446,434 shares of RMB-denominated ordinary shares (A shares). After the issuance, shares held by the public shall account for no less than 10.00% of the total share capital post-issuance. The proceeds from this issuance, net of issuance expenses, are intended to be used for the following fundraising investment projects:

Unit: RMB 10,000

No. Project Name Total Project Investment Proceeds Intended for Use
1 Intelligent Robot Model Research and Development Project 202,245.93 202,245.93
2 Robot Hardware Body Research and Development Project 110,973.80 110,973.80
3 New Intelligent Robot Product Development Project 44,540.00 44,540.00
4 Intelligent Robot Manufacturing Base Construction Project 62,411.39 62,411.39
- Total 420,171.12 420,171.12

Prior to the receipt of the proceeds from this issuance, the Company may, in accordance with the actual payment schedule of each fundraising investment project, advance relevant project investment payments using self-raised funds. After the receipt of the proceeds from this issuance, a portion of the proceeds will be used to replace self-raised funds advanced prior to the receipt of such proceeds.

If the actual proceeds raised from this issuance fall short of the amount of proceeds intended for each project, the Board of Directors shall arrange the specific use of the proceeds based on the importance and urgency of the above-mentioned fundraising investment projects. The Company will make up any shortfall in project investment through self-raised funds.

(II) Proceeds Management System

The Company has formulated the Proceeds Management System applicable after the listing. This system was reviewed and approved at the Company's Sixth Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders in 2025, and sets out clear provisions regarding the dedicated account storage, use, change of use, management, and supervision of the proceeds.

(III) Specific Arrangements for the Key Direction of Proceeds Toward Science and Technology Innovation, and Its Contributions to the Issuer's Principal Business Development and Impact on Future Operating Strategy

1. Specific Arrangements for the Key Direction of Proceeds Toward Science and Technology Innovation

The fundraising investment projects will help enhance the Company's sustained R&D capabilities and engineering manufacturing capabilities in robot-related technologies and products, further consolidating the Company's industry-leading position and technological advantages in the field of general-purpose robotics. By implementing the "Intelligent Robot Model Research and Development Project," the "Robot Hardware Body Research and Development Project," and the "New Intelligent Robot Product Development Project," the Company will continuously develop and iterate core products such as new AI-based humanoid robots and quadruped robots, while also continuously researching and optimizing core intelligent algorithms, mechanical control systems, and other underlying robot-related technologies, accelerating multi-scenario application deployment of general-purpose robots.

2. Contribution of Proceeds to the Issuer's Principal Business Development and Impact on Future Strategy

The Company has conducted thorough research on its current situation and formulated detailed future development strategies, based on which the fundraising investment projects were developed. These projects are primarily directed toward science and technology innovation and intelligent manufacturing, and will support the Company's future strategic development in two respects: strengthening R&D capabilities, and enhancing industrialization and automation capabilities.

First, the fundraising investment projects will help improve the level of software and hardware equipment required for the Company's R&D activities, expand the R&D team, optimize the team structure, and further strengthen the Company's R&D capabilities in areas such as robot embodied intelligence models, hardware bodies, and new robot products. Second, the Company can expand production capacity through the fundraising investment projects, improve the degree of automation and production efficiency, and meet the rapidly growing product demand from downstream markets. Third, by implementing the fundraising investment projects, the Company will benefit from reduced operational instability arising from its dependence on leased premises for long-term production operations.

(IV) Basis for Determining the Fundraising Investment Projects

1. Basis for the Alignment of Fundraising Investment Projects with the Company's Principal Business, Operating Scale, and Development Objectives

As of the current date, the Company has good operating performance and financial condition; however, existing production capacity can no longer meet the rapidly growing market demand. Against the backdrop of rapid development in the high-performance general-purpose robot industry, capacity bottlenecks will constrain the Company's continued business growth and long-term development. The implementation of the fundraising investment projects will help the Company further expand its operating scale, improve its market positioning, comprehensively enhance its business development capabilities, expand its competitive advantages, and is consistent with the Company's overall development strategy, aligning with the Company's existing principal business, operating scale, and future development objectives.

2. Basis for the Alignment of Fundraising Investment Projects with the Company's Financial Condition

The Company has sound financial condition and sustainable profitability, which can provide support for the implementation of the fundraising investment projects and their subsequent operations. At the same time, after the receipt of the proceeds, the Company's financial strength will be significantly enhanced, its asset structure will be further optimized, and its ability to withstand risks will improve accordingly, in alignment with the Company's financial condition.

3. Basis for the Alignment of Fundraising Investment Projects with the Company's Technical Conditions

Through years of continuous R&D investment and accumulation of technical know-how, the Company currently possesses significant technological advantages. It has independently developed, across the full technology stack, core robot model algorithms in embodied intelligence, reinforcement learning, and motion control; intelligent systems for thermal management, energy management, and motor drive; as well as core robot components and sub-assemblies including high-performance motors, reducers, dexterous hands, LiDAR, and various sensors, forming a number of core technologies with commercial applications.

As of January 31, 2026, the Company holds 262 patents, comprising 169 domestically granted patents and 93 overseas patents, including 20 domestic invention patents, 76 domestic utility model patents, and 73 domestic design patents.

The technology innovation R&D and new product development covered by the fundraising investment projects are based on the Company's existing R&D capabilities and technologies. The implementation of the fundraising investment projects will further enhance the Company's technical capabilities, in alignment with the Company's technical conditions.

4. Basis for the Alignment of Fundraising Investment Projects with the Company's Management Capabilities

The Company has established a sound corporate governance structure and an internal control system that meets the needs of its production and operations, ensuring effective implementation of all systems. The Company's senior management and heads of major departments all have extensive industry experience and are capable of effectively managing the Company's various business functions including R&D, procurement, production, and sales. The fundraising investment projects are aligned with the Company's existing management capabilities.

(V) Impact of Fundraising Investment Projects on Horizontal Competition and Independence

After the implementation of the fundraising investment projects, no horizontal competition will arise, and the Company's independence will not be adversely affected.

II. Analysis of the Necessity and Feasibility of the Fundraising Investment Projects

(I) Necessity Analysis of the Fundraising Investment Projects

1. Continuously Accumulating Advanced Robot Embodied Intelligence Models and Hardware Body-Related Technologies to Lay the Foundation for the Company's Long-Term Development

"Brain, Cerebellum, and Hardware Body" are analogies for the three core modules of high-performance general-purpose robots. The "Brain" refers to the cognitive intelligence of general-purpose robots, analogous to the robot's cognitive decision-making system, task planning capabilities, and embodied large models; the "Cerebellum" refers to the embodied intelligence of general-purpose robots, analogous to the robot's motion control system, whole-body dexterous movement capabilities, and embodied body intelligence models; the "Hardware Body" refers to the physical structure of general-purpose robots, including structure, actuators, communication buses, and other physical structural components of the general-purpose robot.

Currently, the industry is in the early stage of rapid development, with continuous technological innovation in the three core modules and continuous production iteration of core components and sub-assemblies. As a leading enterprise in the high-performance general-purpose robot industry, the Company has always treated the accumulation of core technologies across the three modules as the core support for long-term development, continuously tackling challenges around the three core modules to build a full-stack technology barrier.

With respect to the "Brain" and "Cerebellum," the implementation of the fundraising investment projects will facilitate the Company's continuous R&D investment and technological breakthroughs, optimizing and upgrading core model algorithms for embodied manipulation, motion control, and others, improving robots' generalization capabilities, complex instruction comprehension and execution capabilities, and operational precision and flexibility. With respect to the "Hardware Body," the implementation of the fundraising investment projects will facilitate R&D in high power-density motors, high power-density transmission systems, high power-density drives, multi-scale lightweight structural components for legged robots, and centralized thermal control systems for robots, consolidating the Company's hardware body performance advantages and price advantages for high-performance general-purpose robot products.

In addition, the high-performance general-purpose robot industry is currently at a critical stage of commercialization breakthrough and accelerating technology iteration. Insufficient computing power support and the lack of high-quality data have led to inadequate model intelligence, which has become an important bottleneck constraining industry development. Through the implementation of the fundraising investment projects, the Company will first advance "cloud, edge, and end" computing power collaborative deployment to break through computing power bottlenecks and provide support for model training, deep learning, and application expansion. At the same time, the Company will build large-scale real-world datasets, develop cost-effective teleoperation systems, and establish automated annotation mechanisms to form a "data flywheel," supplementing "difficult scenario" data with synthetic data and high-fidelity simulation training to address the pain point of insufficient industry data and lay a data foundation for commercial deployment. Third, the Company will build an intelligent development and training platform integrating three major modules—data engine, model factory, and evaluation center—forming a full-chain R&D system covering "data processing–model training–validation and feedback," significantly shortening development cycles and helping the Company gain a competitive edge in technology innovation, product iteration, and market competition.

Therefore, the implementation of the fundraising investment projects will help the Company continuously accumulate advanced embodied intelligence models and robot hardware body-related technologies, and the projects are necessary.

2. Accelerating the Iteration of Various Types of Robot Products, Enriching the Product Matrix, and Strengthening Market Competitive Advantages

Facing the industry opportunity presented by the accelerating commercialization and explosive demand for high-performance general-purpose robots, the Company aims to enrich its product matrix and strengthen its competitive advantages by continuously driving the iterative upgrade of various types of robot products, further improving the Company's product ecosystem.

Currently, the Company already possesses multiple series of core products: the Go Series, A Series, and B Series quadruped robots can be applied in scientific research, education, and vertical industries such as energy and chemicals and intelligent manufacturing, with global sales ranking first in the industry for multiple consecutive years. The H Series and G Series humanoid robots have seen year-on-year growth in deliveries since their initial debut and have become a new engine of performance growth for the Company. In addition, the Company has also deployed extended and complementary products such as dexterous hands, dexterous robotic arms, and 4D LiDAR to further improve the product chain and deepen coverage of more application scenarios.

By implementing the fundraising investment projects, the Company will make full use of its first-mover advantage, continuously strengthening R&D investment and technology reserves in key areas such as overall architecture design, core component adaptation, functional and performance testing optimization, and process design, accelerating the iteration of various robot products, enriching the product matrix, increasing applicable application scenarios, strengthening market competitive barriers, and enhancing sustained profitability.

Therefore, the use of the fundraising investment projects to accelerate the iteration of various types of robot products and enrich the product matrix is necessary.

3. Expanding Production Scale, Improving Production Efficiency, and Meeting the Increasingly Growing Market Capacity Demand

From the perspective of market size, the global market for humanoid and quadruped robots will see several-fold growth in the future. By expanding capacity, optimizing layout, and improving efficiency, the Company can not only meet the increasingly growing order demand and ensure customer delivery timelines, but also reduce unit production costs through large-scale production, enhance product price competitiveness, further expand market share, and consolidate its industry position.

The Company's existing production system can no longer match the rapidly expanding capacity demand. On one hand, the current production facilities are all leased factories, with limited operating area and equipment configuration capabilities, room for improvement in the degree of intelligent management, and increasingly prominent capacity bottlenecks.

By implementing the fundraising investment projects, the Company will purchase production bases and production testing equipment that meet requirements based on production planning needs, resolving the problems of limited floor space and insufficient production capacity. The new base will scientifically plan functional zones such as production areas, inspection areas, and warehousing areas based on production processes and product characteristics: the production area will introduce automated assembly lines to improve the precision assembly efficiency of core components for humanoid and quadruped robots; the inspection area will be equipped with precision inspection instruments to conduct full-process inspection of product performance, motion accuracy, safety, and other indicators to ensure product quality; the warehousing area will adopt an intelligent warehousing system to achieve efficient storage, retrieval, and sorting of materials and finished products through the Internet of Things and automated equipment, reducing material turnover time.

Furthermore, the Company will leverage the hardware advantages of the new base to achieve data connectivity across production, inventory, procurement, and other segments, enabling real-time monitoring and analysis of production data to ensure precise alignment between production plans and product orders, and improving per-capita output efficiency.

Therefore, the use of the fundraising investment projects to expand production capacity, optimize factory layout, and improve production efficiency is necessary.

(II) Feasibility Analysis of the Fundraising Investment Projects

1. Positive Industrial Policies Have Created a Favorable Development Environment

The implementation of the fundraising investment projects has solid policy feasibility, highly in alignment with national and local strategic planning and support directions for the robot industry.

At the national level, the state continues to provide top-level design and directional guidance for the development of the robot industry. The Proposal of the Central Committee of the Communist Party of China for Formulating the Fifteenth Five-Year Plan for National Economic and Social Development explicitly mentions "proactively laying out future industries, exploring diverse technological pathways, typical application scenarios, viable business models, and market regulatory rules, and promoting quantum science and technology, biomanufacturing, hydrogen energy and nuclear fusion energy, brain-computer interfaces, embodied intelligence, and sixth-generation mobile communications as new economic growth drivers. Innovate regulatory approaches, develop venture capital, and establish investment growth and risk-sharing mechanisms for future industries. Promote the specialized, refined, distinctive, and innovative development of small and medium-sized enterprises, and cultivate unicorn enterprises." The Guiding Opinions on the Innovative Development of Humanoid Robots further refines the objectives, calling for the formation of a scaled industrial development pattern by 2027, while explicitly supporting the development of AI large model-based "Brain" systems and the construction of "Cerebellum" motion control algorithm libraries. The 2025 Government Work Report for the first time incorporated "cultivating the embodied intelligence industry and vigorously developing intelligent robots" into national strategy, highlighting its core position in achieving global technological leadership. In addition, the Action Plan for "Robot +" Applications promotes the deployment of robots in manufacturing, healthcare, elderly care, and other scenarios, providing policy guarantees for the expansion of industrial applications.

At the local level, policies are deeply coordinated with national strategy, with targeted support measures being introduced. Zhejiang Province's Implementation Plan for the Innovative Development of the Humanoid Robot Industry (2024–2027) sets a target of "core industry scale reaching RMB 20 billion," focusing on the integration of complete units and components, technological breakthroughs, and scenario applications. Hangzhou City's Hangzhou Humanoid Robot Industry Development Plan (2024–2029) and Several Policy Measures for Promoting the Innovative Development of the Humanoid Robot Industry include complete robots and algorithms in the list of key breakthroughs, directly providing policy support for the project's algorithm R&D and production and manufacturing.

Therefore, the fundraising investment projects are highly aligned with national and local policy orientations, the policy environment is mature, and the projects are feasible.

2. Broad Market Space Provides Market Assurance for the Projects

Currently, the global general-purpose robot industry has entered a period of explosive growth, with both humanoid robots and quadruped robots demonstrating broad development potential, providing sufficient market assurance for the technical R&D of "Brain, Cerebellum, and Hardware Body" technologies, new product iteration, and absorption of new production capacity involved in the fundraising investment projects.

The humanoid robot track, as a market focal point, is at the beginning stage of mass production explosion, with particularly considerable long-term growth potential. According to industry research institution forecasts, global humanoid robot sales are expected to reach 605,700 units with a market size of USD 15 billion by 2030; global quadruped robot sales are expected to exceed 560,000 units with a market size exceeding RMB 8 billion by 2030. The explosive growth in market size will not only absorb the new production capacity from the fundraising investment projects, but will also provide commercialized application scenarios for the "Brain, Cerebellum, and Hardware Body"-related technology R&D, forming a virtuous cycle of R&D, deployment, and iteration.

Therefore, the abundant and broad market space provides sufficient market assurance for technology R&D, new product iteration, and the absorption of new production capacity, and the projects are feasible.

3. Deep Technological Accumulation and a Professional Talent Team Provide Technical Support for the Projects

The Company has been deeply engaged in the field of high-performance general-purpose robots for many years, possessing deep technological accumulation and a professional talent team, which provide technical support for project implementation.

At the level of technological accumulation, the Company adheres to full-industry-chain independent R&D and independent R&D of core components, building a complete technology system covering hardware, software, and algorithms, while accumulating extensive experience in technology synergy and cost control. The Company currently possesses multiple core technologies with successful commercial applications, including integrated joint integration technology, high-compactness robot body integration technology, robot LiDAR fully self-developed core technology, robot auto-following technology, robot heat dissipation and active cooling-related technology, high-dynamic motion control algorithm technology, and others. As of January 31, 2026, the Company has cumulatively held 262 patents (including 20 domestic invention patents), and has successively won multiple awards including the China Design Intelligence Award and the Robot Award at the China International Industry Fair, and was recognized as a member unit of the Artificial Intelligence Branch of the China Industrial Design Association, with its technical strength having received authoritative recognition, providing a solid foundation for the technology R&D and new product iteration involved in the fundraising investment projects.

At the level of the talent team, the Company has built a scientifically efficient R&D system and professional team. On one hand, the Company has established a comprehensive R&D organizational management system capable of accurately assessing technology trends and formulating R&D plans, ensuring R&D efficiency and the transformation of results. On the other hand, the Company's core R&D team is stable, with major R&D backbones mostly graduating from well-known domestic and overseas universities, covering disciplines such as mechanical engineering, electronic engineering, computer science, and control science and engineering, possessing both solid scientific research capabilities and extensive project experience, providing talent assurance for the implementation of the fundraising investment projects.

Therefore, the deep technological accumulation and professional talent team provide technical support for the projects, and the projects are feasible.

4. The Company's Good Brand Reputation and Broad Customer Recognition Provide Customer Assurance for the Projects

Regarding brand reputation, as a globally renowned high-performance general-purpose robot enterprise, the Company's products have successively appeared on multiple international exhibition and performance platforms including the 2021 CCTV Spring Festival Gala, the 2023 U.S. Super Bowl, the 2023 Hangzhou 19th Asian Games, the 2023 Hangzhou 4th Asian Para Games, the 2025 CCTV Spring Festival Gala, and the 2026 CCTV Spring Festival Gala, and have been repeatedly reported by authoritative media including CCTV News. The Company has been named among Time magazine's 100 Most Influential Companies globally for 2025 and received the 2025 Best Inventions honor, among other recognitions. The Company's product capabilities have received broad recognition from the domestic and international industry and users, demonstrating the technological stability and environmental adaptability of the Company's products, and winning the Company an excellent brand reputation.

Regarding customer recognition, the Company has expanded product applications across multiple industry sectors and gained broad customer recognition. On one hand, the Company has promoted industry-level applications of general-purpose robots in inspection and surveying, fire rescue, public services, and other fields globally, bringing more efficient, safe, and intelligent new solutions to industries such as energy and chemicals, intelligent firefighting, and smart cities. On the other hand, the Company's "high-performance + high price-to-performance" strategy has pioneered the path from technology R&D to large-scale application of general-purpose robots, significantly lowering the usage threshold for consumer-level users, broadly covering the consumer-level market demand for general-purpose robot products, and promoting the application of general-purpose robot products in consumer-level markets such as education, cultural performance, and intelligent services, while winning broad user favor through outstanding product experience and scenario adaptability.

Therefore, good brand reputation and broad customer recognition provide customer assurance for the projects, and the projects are feasible.

(III) Relationship Between the Use of Proceeds and the Issuer's Principal Business and Core Technologies

The Company is a world-renowned, internationally leading high-performance general-purpose robot company, focused on the R&D, production, and sale of high-performance general-purpose humanoid robots, quadruped robots, robot components, and embodied intelligence models. All fundraising investment projects to which the proceeds are intended to be directed are conducted around the Company's principal businesses, and the R&D projects are highly related to the Company's core technologies. The specific relationships are as follows:

No. Fundraising Investment Project Relationship with the Company's Principal Business Relationship with the Company's Core Technologies
1 Intelligent Robot Model Research and Development Project By building R&D centers and improving the R&D office environment, continuously accumulating advanced robot embodied intelligence models and hardware body-related technology R&D, while also accelerating the iteration of various types of robot products, enriching the product matrix, and strengthening market competitive advantages The technology R&D and product development involved in these projects are further extensions based on the Company's existing core technologies
2 Robot Hardware Body Research and Development Project
3 New Intelligent Robot Product Development Project
4 Intelligent Robot Manufacturing Base Construction Project By building a production base, expanding the scale of robot production, improving the level of automation and production efficiency, and meeting the increasingly growing product demand from the market This project involves products that rely on core technologies throughout from development to mass production, and constitutes a specific embodiment of the landing of core technology achievements

III. Specific Details of the Fundraising Investment Projects

The Company's fundraising investment projects for this issuance are the "Intelligent Robot Model Research and Development Project," the "Robot Hardware Body Research and Development Project," the "New Intelligent Robot Product Development Project," and the "Intelligent Robot Manufacturing Base Construction Project," which will support the Company in achieving its development objectives and strategic plans. For specific details, please refer to "Annex 9: Specific Use of Proceeds" in "Section 12: Annexes" of this Prospectus.

IV. Future Development Plans

(I) Development Objectives and Strategic Plans

The Company's development objective is to become a continuously leading enterprise in the global field of high-performance general-purpose robots. With independent R&D as the core, the Company has through years of accumulation established a self-developed and self-produced system covering robot hardware bodies, core intelligent algorithms, embodied intelligence, and core components and sub-assemblies, forming a rich product matrix including core products represented by humanoid robots and quadruped robots, as well as robot components represented by joint modules, dexterous hands, collaborative robotic arms, and perceptual sensors, and has conducted product promotion and industry applications around the above-mentioned products.

In the future, the Company will continue to invest in cutting-edge technology R&D, and strives to, through the listing, enhance its financial strength, optimize corporate governance, and improve talent incentives while accelerating the high-quality development of the embodied intelligence industry chain, and through continuous technological innovation, lead the global robot industry toward large-scale scenario application.

(II) Measures Taken During the Reporting Period to Achieve Strategic Objectives and Their Implementation Results

1. Increasing R&D Investment

Regarding R&D investment, the Company continued to increase R&D investment during the reporting period. During the reporting period, the Company's R&D expenses were RMB 29,984,800, RMB 49,951,800, RMB 70,017,000, and RMB 90,209,400, respectively, with a compound annual growth rate of 52.81% over the most recent three years, showing a rapid growth trend, and resulting in the formation of multiple core technologies and intellectual property rights. As of January 31, 2026, the Company holds 262 patents (including 20 domestic invention patents).

2. Strengthening Technological Accumulation

Through years of R&D innovation and technological accumulation, the Company has gradually formed multiple core technologies with commercial applications, including integrated joint integration technology, high-compactness robot body integration technology, and robot LiDAR fully self-developed core technology, which have been applied to the Company's high-performance general-purpose humanoid robots, quadruped robots, and other products. On this basis, the Company has conducted research and strategic planning in subdivided technology areas such as robots' external sensing capabilities, environmental interaction capabilities, improving robot joint degrees of freedom, environmental adaptability, and the universality of application scenarios, forming certain technology reserves and continuously undergoing technology updates and iterations.

3. Emphasizing Talent Development

The Company places great importance on talent recruitment and development. During the reporting period, the Company established a systematic and scientific talent selection standard and human resources management system, established the value concept of joint progress between the Company and its employees, and formed a set of clear management systems in areas including employee selection and recruitment, internal training, position promotion, and reward and punishment incentive mechanisms. The Company motivates its talent team to continuously strive for excellence and pursue continuous innovation through a market-competitive compensation system and incentive mechanism.

(III) Measures to Be Taken Under Future Plans

In order to better achieve the Company's development objectives, the plans and measures the Company will adopt are as follows:

1. Strengthening Cutting-Edge Technologies to Build Continuously Leading Technology Barriers

The Company continues to increase R&D investment in robot hardware bodies and embodied intelligence models, with the R&D of industry-leading underlying technologies as the core, continuously evolving toward high-performance and high-universality R&D directions. In the future, the Company will, relying on the "Intelligent Robot Model Research and Development Project," "Robot Hardware Body Research and Development Project," and "New Intelligent Robot Product Development Project" to be funded by the proceeds from this issuance, conduct research on subjects including robot embodied intelligence models and hardware bodies, and continuously develop and iterate new robot products, strengthen cutting-edge technology exploration, achieve breakthroughs in robot autonomous reasoning, environmental adaptability, extreme hardware performance, and other aspects, continuously consolidate and expand technology leadership advantages, and enhance product competitiveness.

2. Expanding into International Markets and Accelerating the Construction of a Global Robot Ecosystem

The Company will vigorously advance the open strategy of its development platform to attract global developers and partners. At the same time, it will actively participate in the formulation of international technical standards, promoting its own technical protocols to become industry-wide paradigms. The Company will deeply cultivate international markets, achieving comprehensive globalization from technology to brand.

3. Enhancing Financial Assurance to Support R&D Investment and Capacity Expansion

The field of high-performance general-purpose robots is a technology-intensive industry that is currently in a high-speed development phase, with substantial funding requirements and R&D investment scale. As of the current date, the Company has provided the necessary financial support for business development through multiple rounds of financing. The proceeds from this issuance will provide strong support for the Company's technology R&D and product development, high-performance general-purpose robot product production, as well as daily operations and business expansion, helping the Company achieve its development objectives and strategic plans. The Company will make good use of the proceeds to implement projects and assist in long-term development.

4. Deepening the Governance System and Improving Organizational Synergy Efficiency

The Company will, in accordance with the requirements for listed companies, further deepen organizational mechanism development, strengthen internal management, and achieve standardized internal controls, refined management, and automated information management; improve the corporate governance structure to promote the Company's compliant operation and strengthen the scientific nature and transparency of all decisions; improve the level of coordination among the Company's R&D, sales, finance, procurement, production, and other departments to achieve efficient collaboration and resource sharing; strengthen the information technology development of the Company's management systems to promote institutional innovation and management upgrades, achieving inter-departmental data connectivity, optimizing operations management, and improving management efficiency.

5. Establishing a Talent Hub and Building the Core Human Capital to Support Sustainable Development

The Company will implement more forward-looking and longer-lasting incentive plans to build a "talent hub" that brings together the world's top intellect, ensuring the Company continuously possesses the core human capital to drive industrial transformation. At the same time, through creating an open, inclusive, and innovation-encouraging team atmosphere and providing internationally first-class technical research conditions and career development platforms, the Company will ensure it continuously possesses the core human capital capable of driving technological transformation in the industry and leading the future development of the industry, providing solid assurance for the Company's technological and market leadership position.

Section 8: Corporate Governance and Independence

▶

I. Deficiencies in Corporate Governance During the Reporting Period and Improvements Made

At the beginning of the reporting period, Unitree Co., Ltd. (宇树有限) had not yet been restructured into a joint stock limited company, had not established an independent director system, had not set up a Board Secretary or board-level special committees, and the corporate governance structure required further improvement.

As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, the Company has, in accordance with the requirements of laws and regulations and normative documents such as the Company Law and Securities Law, established a corporate governance structure consisting of the General Meeting of Shareholders, the Board of Directors and its special committees, and senior management. Under the Company's Board of Directors, four special committees have been established: the Audit Committee, the Strategy Committee, the Nomination Committee, and the Remuneration and Evaluation Committee. The Company has established and improved a series of rules and regulations including the Articles of Association, clearly defining the scope of rights and responsibilities and working procedures among the General Meeting of Shareholders, the Board of Directors, and senior management.

Since the establishment of the corporate governance structure and various rules and regulations, the General Meeting of Shareholders, the Board of Directors, and their special committees have operated independently in accordance with the law, faithfully fulfilling their respective duties and obligations. The corporate governance mechanism has been effectively implemented, ensuring the Company's compliant operation, with no material violations of laws and regulations occurring and no corporate governance deficiencies present.

II. Internal Control Status of the Issuer

(I) Management's Self-Assessment Opinion on the Completeness, Reasonableness, and Effectiveness of Internal Controls

The Company's existing internal control system is capable of meeting the Company's management requirements, providing reasonable assurance for the preparation of true and fair financial statements, and providing assurance for the healthy operation of all of the Company's business activities and the implementation of relevant national laws and regulations and internal rules and regulations. The Company believes that, in accordance with the Basic Standards for Enterprise Internal Control and related regulations, the Company's internal controls were effective in all material respects as of September 30, 2025.

(II) Certified Public Accountants' Attestation Opinion on the Issuer's Internal Control System

Rongcheng Certified Public Accountants has issued the Internal Control Audit Report (Rongcheng Audit Zi [2026] 230Z0441), opining that Unitree Technology (宇树科技) maintained effective financial reporting internal controls in all material respects as of September 30, 2025 in accordance with the Basic Standards for Enterprise Internal Control and related regulations.

(III) Internal Control Compliance Status of the Company During the Reporting Period

During the reporting period, the Company had situations of lending funds to related parties. For specific details, please refer to the relevant content in "VII. (II) 6. General and Occasional Related-Party Transactions" of this Section. As of the end of the reporting period, the relevant inter-company fund lending has been fully cleared. This does not constitute a material deficiency in financial internal controls, and the Company has formulated internal control measures for relevant fund management. The related-party fund lending during the reporting period does not constitute a material adverse impact on the effectiveness of the Company's internal control system, does not involve material violations of laws and regulations, and will not have a material impact on the Company's day-to-day operations and management.

Apart from the above matters, as of the end of the reporting period, the Issuer has no other non-compliant financial internal control circumstances.

III. Violations of Laws and Regulations by the Issuer During the Reporting Period

During the reporting period, the Company strictly conducted its business activities in accordance with relevant laws and regulations and the provisions of the Articles of Association, with no material violations of laws and regulations, and has not been subject to any major administrative penalties by national administrative or industry regulatory authorities for violations of laws and regulations.

IV. Occupation of Funds and Guarantees Provided to Related Parties During the Reporting Period

During the reporting period, the Company had no situations in which its funds were occupied by the controlling shareholder, actual controller, or other enterprises controlled by them through loans, debt repayment on behalf of others, advance payments on behalf of others, or other means, and the Company had no situations of providing guarantees for the controlling shareholder, actual controller, or other enterprises controlled by them.

V. Independent Operation Status of the Issuer

(I) Completeness of Assets

The Company possesses the main production systems, auxiliary production systems, and supporting facilities related to production and operations, and legally holds ownership or use rights of office premises, machinery and equipment, trademarks, patents, and non-patented technologies related to production and operations, and has an independent raw material procurement and product sales system.

(II) Independence of Personnel

The Company's General Manager, Chief Financial Officer, Board Secretary, and other senior management members do not hold any positions other than director or supervisor at the controlling shareholder, actual controller, or other enterprises controlled by them, and do not receive salaries from the controlling shareholder, actual controller, or other enterprises controlled by them. The Company's financial personnel do not hold concurrent positions at the controlling shareholder, actual controller, or other enterprises controlled by them.

(III) Financial Independence

The Company has established an independent financial accounting system and is capable of making independent financial decisions. The Company has standardized financial accounting systems and financial management systems for subsidiaries. The Company has opened independent bank accounts, and there are no situations of sharing bank accounts with the controlling shareholder, actual controller, or other enterprises controlled by them.

(IV) Institutional Independence

The Company has established and improved its internal operational management institutions, independently exercising operational management authority, with no situations of institutional overlap with the controlling shareholder, actual controller, or other enterprises controlled by them.

(V) Business Independence

The Company's business is independent from the controlling shareholder, actual controller, and other enterprises controlled by them. There is no horizontal competition with the controlling shareholder, actual controller, or other enterprises controlled by them that constitutes a material adverse impact on the Company, nor are there related-party transactions that seriously affect independence or are manifestly unfair.

(VI) Stability of the Company's Principal Business, Control Rights, Management Team, and Core Technical Personnel

The Company's principal business, control rights, management team, and core technical personnel are stable. Within the most recent two years, there have been no material adverse changes in the principal business, directors, senior management, or core technical personnel. The Company's share ownership is clear, with no material ownership disputes that may lead to changes in control rights, and the actual controller has not changed within the most recent two years.

(VII) Other Matters Having a Material Impact on the Company's Ability to Continue as a Going Concern

As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, the Company has no material ownership disputes regarding its major assets, core technologies, or trademarks; no material debt repayment risks; no contingent matters such as material guarantees, litigations, or arbitrations; and no matters having a material impact on continuing operations such as material changes that have occurred or are about to occur in the operating environment.

In summary, the Company is mutually independent from the controlling shareholder, actual controller, and other enterprises controlled by them in terms of assets, personnel, finance, institutions, and business, and possesses an independent and complete business system with the capability to independently and sustainably operate directly in the market.

VI. Horizontal Competition

(I) No Horizontal Competition Constituting Material Adverse Impact Exists Between the Issuer and the Controlling Shareholder, Actual Controller, and Other Enterprises Controlled by Them

1. Controlling Shareholder and Actual Controller

As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, Wang Xingxing (王兴兴) directly holds 86,714,964 shares of the Issuer, accounting for 23.8216% of the Issuer's total share capital. He controls 10.9414% of the Issuer's shares through Shanghai Yuyi (上海宇翼), for a combined control of 34.7630% of the Issuer's shares. Taking into account the arrangements for special voting rights, prior to this issuance, Wang Xingxing's aggregate control of voting rights is 68.7816%, which can have a significant impact on the vast majority of resolutions at the Company's General Meeting of Shareholders, making him the Company's controlling shareholder and actual controller.

2. Other Enterprises Controlled and Invested in by the Controlling Shareholder

As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, other than the Issuer and its controlled subsidiaries, the enterprises controlled by the controlling shareholder and actual controller Wang Xingxing (王兴兴) are as follows:

No. Enterprise Name Shareholding Situation Principal Business
1 Hangzhou Tianze (杭州天则) Wang Xingxing holds 100% and serves as its director No actual business; serves as the actual controller's holding company
2 Shanghai Yuyi (上海宇翼) Enterprise controlled by Wang Xingxing through Hangzhou Tianze; Hangzhou Tianze is the executive partner of Shanghai Yuyi No actual business; serves as the Company's equity incentive platform
3 Hangzhou Yixin (杭州翌心) Enterprise controlled by Wang Xingxing through Hangzhou Tianze; Hangzhou Tianze is the executive partner of Hangzhou Yixin, and Hangzhou Yixin is a limited partner of Shanghai Yuyi No actual business; serves as the Company's equity incentive platform
4 Hangzhou Yiyi (杭州翌意) Enterprise controlled by Wang Xingxing through Hangzhou Tianze; Hangzhou Tianze is the executive partner of Hangzhou Yixin, and Hangzhou Yixin is a limited partner of Shanghai Yuyi No actual business; serves as the Company's equity incentive platform
5 Shanghai Yixin (上海翌心) Enterprise controlled by Wang Xingxing through Hangzhou Tianze; Hangzhou Tianze is the executive partner of Shanghai Yixin No actual business

The above entities serve solely as shareholding vehicles, have not conducted actual business operations, and have no horizontal competition with the Company.

(II) Commitments to Avoid Horizontal Competition

In order to avoid horizontal competition with the Company, the Company's controlling shareholder and actual controller have issued relevant commitments. For the specific content of the commitments, please refer to "Annex 1: (XIV) Commitments of the Controlling Shareholder and Actual Controller Regarding the Avoidance of Horizontal Competition" in "Section 12: Annexes" of this Prospectus.

VII. Related Parties and Related-Party Transactions

(I) Related Parties and Related-Party Relationships

As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, in accordance with the relevant provisions of the Company Law, Accounting Standards for Business Enterprises No. 36 – Disclosure of Related Parties, the Listing Rules, and other relevant regulations, the specific situation of the Company's related parties and related-party relationships is as follows:

1. Related Natural Persons

(1) Controlling Shareholder and Actual Controller

The Company's controlling shareholder and actual controller are both Wang Xingxing (王兴兴).

(4) Other Related Natural Persons

Other related natural persons of the Company include: 1) close family members of the related natural persons referred to in items (1) through (3) above, including spouses, children aged 18 or above and their spouses, parents and parents-in-law, siblings and their spouses, siblings-in-law, and parents of children's spouses; 2) directors, supervisors, and senior management who have resigned within the twelve months prior to and up to the reporting period, and their close family members; 3) other natural persons who, in accordance with the principle of substance over form, are determined to have a special relationship with the Issuer that may cause the Issuer to favor their interests.

2. Related Legal Persons

(1) Legal persons or other organizations directly holding 5% or more of the Company's shares and their persons acting in concert

No. Name of Related Party Description of Related Relationship

(II) Related Party Transactions During the Reporting Period

1. Summary Table of Related Party Transactions

The summary table of related party transactions of the Company during the reporting period is as follows:

Unit: RMB 10,000

Item January–September 2025 Full Year 2024 Full Year 2023 Full Year 2022
Recurring Related Party Transactions Purchase of goods / receipt of services — — — —
Sale of goods / provision of services 1,683.65 36.47 21.51 0.68
Compensation of key management personnel 859.09 863.89 534.60 484.30

In addition, the Company had incidental related party transactions during the reporting period, primarily consisting of inter-company fund borrowings with related parties. For details, please refer to "VII. (II) 6. General Incidental Related Party Transactions" in this section.

2. Criteria and Basis for Determining Significant Related Party Transactions

Pursuant to the provisions of the Related Party Transaction Management System, transactions between the Company and related parties (excluding transactions in which the Company receives donated cash assets, related parties unconditionally reduce or waive obligations owed by the Company, or related parties provide guarantees for the Company) where the transaction amount (excluding guarantees) accounts for 1% or more of the Company's most recently audited total assets and exceeds RMB 30 million shall be submitted to the shareholders' meeting for deliberation and approval and are classified as significant related party transactions. The Issuer identifies related party transactions meeting the foregoing criteria as significant related party transactions.

3. Significant Recurring Related Party Transactions

During the reporting period, the Company had no significant recurring related party transactions.

4. Significant Incidental Related Party Transactions

During the reporting period, the Company had no significant incidental related party transactions.

5. General Recurring Related Party Transactions

During each period of the reporting period, the amounts of goods sold to Beijing Galaxy General Robot Co., Ltd. (北京银河通用机器人股份有限公司) were RMB 0, RMB 17,700, RMB 364,700, and RMB 15,143,800 respectively, with the main products sold being quadruped robots, humanoid robots, and robot components. Beijing Galaxy General Robot Co., Ltd. is a well-known enterprise in the industry engaged in embodied multimodal large-model general-purpose robots. Its purchases of the Company's products are primarily for two purposes: its own technology research and development, and secondary development for external sales. The transaction prices do not differ significantly from market prices and are fair. In addition, the amounts sold to other related parties are relatively small, and the transaction prices do not differ significantly from market prices and are fair.

(2) Compensation of key management personnel

During each period of the reporting period, the compensation received by key management personnel of the Company was RMB 4,843,000, RMB 5,346,000, RMB 8,638,900, and RMB 8,590,900 respectively.

During the reporting period, the Company's fund lending to related parties is as follows:

Borrower Amount Borrowed Borrowing Date Repayment Date Interest Amount
Chen Li (陈立) 20.00 March 2022 August 2022 0.31
15.00 September 2022 December 2022 0.12

During the reporting period, Chen Li (陈立) borrowed funds from the Company on a short-term basis due to personal funding needs and repaid the borrowings within the same year. The interest on the fund borrowings was determined with reference to the People's Bank of China loan rate for the same period, and the interest rate on such borrowings is fair.

During the reporting period, in order to avoid potential competition in the same industry, actual controller Wang Xingxing (王兴兴) gratuitously transferred to the Issuer a patent related to intelligent fitness equipment (Patent No. 3240329, Patent Name: "A Fitness Resistance Puller") in August 2025. The relevant ownership change registration procedures were completed in October 2025.

(III) Compliance with Procedures for Related Party Transactions During the Reporting Period and Independent Directors' Opinions on Related Party Transactions

The Company has established a comprehensive corporate governance system. The Articles of Association, Rules of Procedure of the Shareholders' Meeting, Rules of Procedure of the Board of Directors, System for Independent Directors, and Related Party Transaction Management System set out provisions regarding the abstention voting system, decision-making authority, and decision-making procedures for related party transactions, in order to ensure the fairness of the Company's related party transactions and to ensure that related party transactions do not harm the interests of the Company and all of its shareholders.

The Ninth Meeting of the First Board of Directors, the Thirteenth Meeting of the First Board of Directors, and the Sixth Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders of 2025 have confirmed the related party transaction matters during the reporting period. The Company's independent directors have convened a dedicated meeting to deliberate on the relevant proposals regarding related party transactions and have issued approving review opinions.

(IV) Measures to Regulate and Reduce Related Party Transactions

1. Formulation and Improvement of Relevant Systems

The Company has incorporated provisions in the Articles of Association, Rules of Procedure of the Shareholders' Meeting, Rules of Procedure of the Board of Directors, System for Independent Directors, and Related Party Transaction Management System regarding the allocation of approval authority for the shareholders' meeting and the board of directors in deliberating on related party transactions, as well as systems for related shareholders and related directors to abstain from voting, thereby clearly establishing the decision-making procedures for related party transactions.

2. Commitment Letters on Regulating Related Party Transactions

In order to regulate related party transactions with the Issuer, the controlling shareholder, actual controller, directors, and senior management of the Issuer have issued relevant commitments. The content of such commitments is set out in "Section 12 Appendices" of this Prospectus, "Appendix 1: (XI) Commitments on Reducing and Regulating Related Party Transactions."

3. Measures to Further Regulate and Reduce Unnecessary Related Party Transactions

The measures that the Company will further implement in the future to regulate and reduce unnecessary related party transactions are as follows:

  1. For procurement matters where external suppliers are available, the Company will introduce external suppliers through public tendering, competitive negotiation, price inquiry, and other means to further reduce the proportion of related party transactions;
  2. For related party transactions that arise from and are unavoidable in the ordinary course of business of both parties, the Company shall comply with the principles of fairness and impartiality, and price such transactions with reference to transaction prices with unrelated third parties, market fair prices, and similar benchmarks;
  3. In the future, the Company will continue to strictly enforce the internal control systems such as the Rules of Procedure of the Shareholders' Meeting, Rules of Procedure of the Board of Directors, System for Independent Directors, and Related Party Transaction Management System to manage related party transactions, strictly implement the decision-making procedures, abstention system, and information disclosure system for related party transactions, strengthen the oversight of related party transactions by independent directors, further improve the Company's corporate governance structure, ensure that related party transactions are fair, impartial, and equitable, and prevent related party transactions from harming the interests of the Company and its shareholders.

Section 9 Investor Protection

▶

I. Distribution Arrangements for Accumulated Retained Profits Prior to Completion of This Offering and Decision-Making Procedures Followed

Pursuant to the Proposal on the Plan for Distribution of Accumulated Retained Profits Prior to the Company's Initial Public Offering of Shares approved by the Sixth Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders of 2025, the accumulated undistributed profits as of the date prior to the Company's initial public offering of shares shall be shared by all existing and new shareholders after the completion of the listing in proportion to their respective shareholdings in the Company.

II. Dividend Distribution Policy and Long-Term Return Plan

(I) Provisions on Profit Distribution in the Articles of Association

Pursuant to the Articles of Association (Draft) passed by the Sixth Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders of 2025, the profit distribution policy following this offering is determined as follows:

The Company shall implement a continuous and stable profit distribution policy. The Company's profit distribution shall place emphasis on providing investors with reasonable and stable investment returns while also giving consideration to the Company's long-term and sustainable development. Provided that the Company's profits and cash flows are sufficient to meet its normal operations and long-term development needs, the Company shall prioritize the distribution of dividends in cash. Where it is expected that the Company will maintain good development prospects but its development requires significant cash resources, the Company may distribute dividends in the form of stock dividends. The specific dividend payout ratio for each year shall be proposed by the Board of Directors based on the Company's annual profitability and future capital utilization plans. Independent directors shall express independent opinions thereon. The Company may conduct interim cash dividend distributions.

(II) Special Research and Assessment by the Board of Directors on Shareholder Return Matters and the Rationale for the Corresponding Plan

1. Special Research and Assessment by the Board of Directors on Shareholder Return Matters

In order to improve and strengthen the Company's scientific, continuous, and stable dividend decision-making and oversight mechanism, to clarify the Company's reasonable investment returns for investors after listing, to increase the transparency and operability of profit distribution decisions, to facilitate investors' oversight of the Company's operations and profit distribution, and to guide investors to establish the concept of long-term and rational investment, in accordance with the requirements of the China Securities Regulatory Commission's Guidelines for the Supervision of Listed Companies No. 3 — Cash Dividends of Listed Companies (2025 Revision) (CSRC Announcement [2025] No. 5) and other relevant documents, the Company's Board of Directors has formulated the Three-Year Shareholder Dividend Return Plan Following Listing.

2. Factors Considered by the Company in Formulating This Plan

With a focus on long-term and sustainable development, and on the basis of a comprehensive analysis of the Company's actual operating and development conditions, the social cost of capital, and the external financing environment, the Company shall fully solicit the requirements and wishes of independent directors and minority shareholders, and shall take into account factors such as the Company's current and future scale of profitability, cash flow position, stage of development, capital requirements for project investment, and the bank credit and debt financing environment, in order to establish a continuous, stable, and scientific return plan and mechanism for investors, thereby making institutional arrangements for profit distribution and ensuring the continuity and stability of profit distribution policies.

3. Principles for Formulating This Plan

The Company shall actively implement a continuous and stable dividend distribution policy. The Company's dividend distribution shall emphasize providing investors with reasonable investment returns while also giving consideration to the Company's sustainable development. The Board of Directors shall comply with relevant laws, regulations, and the provisions of the Articles of Association. In formulating profit distribution plans, particularly cash dividend plans, the Board shall solicit opinions from all parties, and in particular shall fully solicit the opinions of independent directors and minority shareholders.

(III) Profit Distribution Plan of the Issuer for the Three Years Following Listing, Basis and Feasibility of Such Plan, and Arrangements for the Use of Undistributed Profits

1. Method of Distribution

Provided that the Company's profits and cash flows are sufficient to meet its normal operations and long-term development needs, the Company shall prioritize the distribution of dividends in cash. Where it is expected that the Company will maintain good development prospects but its development requires significant cash resources, the Company may distribute dividends in the form of stock dividends.

2. Conditions for Implementing Cash Distribution

The distributable profits realized by the Company in that year (i.e., the after-tax profits remaining after the Company has made up losses and appropriated reserve funds) are positive, and the cash flow is abundant such that implementing cash dividends will not affect the Company's subsequent continuing operations; the Company's accumulated distributable profits are positive; the auditing firm has issued a standard unqualified opinion audit report on the Company's financial statements for that year; and the Company has no significant investment plans or significant cash expenditures (excluding fundraising project expenditures).

3. Conditions for Implementing Stock Dividends

When the Company's operations are in good condition and the Board of Directors considers that issuing stock dividends is conducive to the overall interests of all shareholders of the Company, the Company may, on the premise of ensuring adequate cash dividend distribution, propose a plan for distributing stock dividends. Where the Company uses stock dividends to distribute profits, it shall fully consider whether the total share capital after the distribution of stock dividends is commensurate with the Company's current scale of operations, growth rate of profits, dilution of net assets per share, and other factors, so as to ensure that the profit distribution plan is consistent with the overall and long-term interests of all shareholders. The Company's dividend distribution shall not exceed the scope of accumulated distributable profits.

4. Proportion of Cash Distribution

The Company shall maintain the continuity and stability of its profit distribution policy. When the conditions for cash dividends are met, the Company shall in principle distribute cash dividends of no less than 10% of the distributable profits realized in that year. Distributable profits not distributed in the current year may be carried forward for distribution in subsequent years.

The Company's Board of Directors shall comprehensively consider factors such as the characteristics of the industry in which it operates, its stage of development, its own business model, its level of profitability, and whether there are significant cash expenditure arrangements, and shall, in accordance with the procedures set forth in the Articles of Association, distinguish between the following circumstances and propose differentiated cash dividend policies:

  1. Where the Company's stage of development is the mature stage and there are no significant cash expenditure arrangements, the proportion of cash dividends in the current profit distribution shall be no less than 80%;
  2. Where the Company's stage of development is the mature stage and there are significant cash expenditure arrangements, the proportion of cash dividends in the current profit distribution shall be no less than 40%;
  3. Where the Company's stage of development is the growth stage and there are significant cash expenditure arrangements, the proportion of cash dividends in the current profit distribution shall be no less than 20%.

Where the Company's stage of development is not easily distinguishable but there are significant investment plans or significant cash expenditure arrangements, the provisions of the preceding item may be applied.

5. Distribution Interval

On the premise that the conditions for profit distribution are met and the Company's normal operations and long-term development are ensured, the Company shall in principle conduct one profit distribution per year after approval by the annual general meeting of shareholders. The Company's Board of Directors may propose that the Company conduct an interim cash dividend distribution based on the Company's profitability and capital requirements, and shall submit such proposal to the shareholders' meeting for deliberation and approval.

6. Decision-Making Mechanism and Procedures for Profit Distribution

The Board of Directors shall formulate annual profit distribution plans and interim profit distribution plans. If an independent director considers that a specific cash dividend plan may harm the interests of the listed company or minority shareholders, such independent director has the right to express an independent opinion. Independent directors may solicit opinions from minority shareholders, propose dividend proposals, and submit such proposals directly to the Board of Directors for deliberation.

After the Board of Directors has deliberated and approved the profit distribution plan, such plan shall be submitted to the shareholders' meeting for deliberation and approval.

Where the Board of Directors fails to propose a cash profit distribution plan, or where the cash profit distribution plan proposed by the Board of Directors does not meet the requirements stipulated in the Articles of Association, the Company shall disclose in detail in its periodic reports the reasons therefor and the intended use of funds not distributed as dividends, and independent directors shall express independent opinions thereon.

The shareholders' meeting shall deliberate on the profit distribution plan. The Company shall provide means such as online voting to facilitate shareholders' participation in the shareholders' meeting vote. Before shareholders deliberate on specific cash dividend plans, the Company shall proactively communicate and interact with shareholders, particularly minority shareholders, through multiple channels (including but not limited to shareholder hotlines, facsimile, email, and interactive platforms), fully solicit the opinions and demands of minority shareholders, and respond promptly to questions of concern to minority shareholders.

After the shareholders' meeting has passed a resolution on the profit distribution plan, the Company's Board of Directors shall complete the profit distribution within 2 months of the approval by the shareholders' meeting.

(IV) Long-Term Return Plan of the Company

The Company shall review the shareholder dividend return plan at least once every three years and, in compliance with the Articles of Association, shall, based on the opinions of independent directors and minority shareholders, make appropriate and necessary amendments to the profit distribution policy currently in effect in light of specific operating data by the Board of Directors, taking full account of the Company's scale of profitability, cash flow position, stage of development, and capital requirements, and shall determine the shareholder dividend return plan for that period.

In the event of force majeure events such as natural disasters, changes in the Company's external operating environment that have had or are expected to have a material impact on the Company's production and operations, or significant changes in the Company's own operating conditions, the Company may make appropriate and necessary adjustments to the dividend plan and schedule based on the opinions of shareholders (particularly public investors) and independent directors. Adjustments to the dividend plan and schedule shall be made with the protection of shareholders' interests as the starting point and shall not contravene the relevant provisions of the Articles of Association.

III. Differences in Dividend Distribution Policies Before and After the Offering

In accordance with the relevant provisions of the CSRC's Notice on Further Implementing Matters Relating to Cash Dividends of Listed Companies, Guidelines for the Supervision of Listed Companies No. 3 — Cash Dividends of Listed Companies, and Guidelines on Regulatory Rules Applicable to Issuance — No. 10, the Company has formulated, clarified, and refined the principles, forms, and intervals of profit distribution after the offering, the specific conditions and proportions for cash dividends, the specific conditions for distributing stock dividends, the decision-making procedures and supervisory mechanism for profit distribution plans, and the adjustment of profit distribution policies. The profit distribution policy and future dividend plan after the offering place greater emphasis on providing investors with reasonable investment returns while giving consideration to the Company's sustainable development, thereby further strengthening the protection of and returns to all shareholders of the Company, particularly minority investors.

IV. Special Voting Rights Shares, Contractual Control Structures or Similar Special Arrangements

The Company has established relevant investor protection measures in connection with the special voting rights arrangement. For details, please refer to "Section 4 Basic Information of the Issuer" of this Prospectus, under "VIII. (III) Investor Protection Measures."

In addition, Wang Xingxing (王兴兴), the Company's shareholder holding special voting rights shares, has made a commitment: "I undertake to exercise my rights in accordance with relevant laws, regulations, and the Company's Articles of Association, not to abuse special voting rights, and not to harm the lawful interests of investors. If I harm the lawful interests of investors, I will promptly make corrections and bear the liability for compensation to investors in accordance with the law."

Other than the special voting rights arrangement, as of the date of signing of this Prospectus, the Issuer does not have any contractual control structures or similar special arrangements.

V. Investor Protection Measures in Cases of Unrecovered Losses or Accumulated Deficit

As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, the Company has no situation of unrecovered losses or accumulated deficit.

4 Shenzhen Huaxin Liye Technology Co., Ltd. (深圳市华鑫立业科技有限公司) Agency agreement June 2025 In performance
5 Beijing Chaoyuan Times Technology Co., Ltd. (北京朝元时代科技有限公司) Agency agreement January 2025 In performance
6 Chengdu Jintaier Technology Development Co., Ltd. (成都金泰尔科技发展有限公司) Agency agreement April 2025 In performance
7 Overseas Customer A (Asia) Agency agreement April 2024 / January 2025 Performance completed / In performance
8 Overseas Customer B (Asia) Agency agreement June 2024 / January 2025 Performance completed / In performance
9 Zhongke Kaichuang (Guangzhou) Intelligent Technology Development Co., Ltd. (中科开创（广州）智能科技发展有限公司) Framework agreement June 2025 In performance
10 Beijing JD Century Trade Co., Ltd. (北京京东世纪贸易有限公司) Framework agreement November 2024 In performance
11 Overseas Customer C (Europe) Agency agreement December 2024 In performance
12 Overseas Customer N (Europe) USD 7,527,600 July 2025 In performance
13 Xingchuangyi (Kunshan) Entertainment Co., Ltd. (星创艺（昆山）文娱有限公司) Framework agreement July 2025 In performance
14 China Mobile (Hangzhou) Information Technology Co., Ltd. (中移（杭州）信息技术有限公司) Not exceeding RMB 39,703,500 September 2025 In performance

All of the above contracts are material sales contracts related to the Company's principal business signed with major customers, and have a certain impact on the Company's operating conditions and financial data. Failure to perform such contracts in a timely manner may affect the Company's operating results and its business relationships with customers.

(III) Loan Contracts

During the reporting period, the material loan contracts of the Company and its controlled subsidiaries with amounts of RMB 10,000,000 or more that have been performed and are currently being performed are as follows:

No. Contract Number Borrower Lender Amount (RMB 10,000) Loan Term Performance Status
1 IR2304260000069 Unitree Limited (宇树有限) China Merchants Bank Hangzhou Qiantang Sub-branch (招商银行杭州钱塘支行) 1,000 2023.4.26–2023.7.26 Performance completed
2 IR2310240000070 Unitree Limited (宇树有限) China Merchants Bank Hangzhou Qiantang Sub-branch (招商银行杭州

Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (宇树科技股份有限公司)                                                           Prospectus

the Company to compensate attorney's fees incurred in the litigation and bear the costs of the counterclaim; in December 2025, the Hangzhou Intermediate Court accepted the Company's aforementioned counterclaim and consolidated it with Luweimei's original action for joint trial.

In February 2026, the Hangzhou Intermediate Court rendered a Civil Judgment holding that the accused infringing products of Unitree Technology (宇树科技) lacked multiple technical features of the patent claims at issue and did not fall within the scope of protection of the patent rights at issue, and thus did not constitute infringement; the court also held that Luweimei Company had violated the purpose and spirit of the intellectual property legal protection system, constituted an abuse of rights, and amounted to malicious litigation against Unitree Technology (宇树科技). The court judgment dismissed all of Luweimei Company's litigation claims and ordered Luweimei Company to compensate Unitree Technology (宇树科技) for reasonable expenses. As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, Luweimei Company has filed an appeal and the second-instance judgment has not yet been rendered.

3. In January 2026, Luweimei Company filed suit against Unitree Technology (宇树科技) and Beijing Jingdong Century Trading Co., Ltd. (北京京东世纪贸易有限公司) for infringement

In January 2026, Luweimei Company filed a lawsuit with the Hangzhou Intermediate Court on grounds of infringement of invention patent rights (case number: (2026) Zhe 01 Zhi Min Chu No. 13), requesting a court order requiring the Company and Beijing Jingdong Century Trading Co., Ltd. (北京京东世纪贸易有限公司) to cease manufacturing, selling, and offering for sale the Unitree robot dog "Go2 pro" series products that infringe its Invention Patent No. ZL201610396363.0, to compensate for economic losses of RMB 2,000, to bear punitive damages, and to pay related litigation costs.

Based on technical assessment and review of evidence, the Company believes that Luweimei Company's litigation claims lack factual and legal basis and that the Company's products do not constitute infringement of Luweimei Company's invention patent rights. As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, the first-instance judgment in this case has not yet been rendered.

Apart from the above litigation, the Company has no litigation or arbitration that may have a material impact on the Company's financial condition, operating results, reputation, business activities, or future prospects.

(II) Criminal litigation, material litigation or arbitration matters involving the controlling shareholder or actual controller of the Company, subsidiaries, and directors, supervisors, senior management and other core personnel of the Issuer as parties that may have an impact on the Issuer

As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, the controlling shareholder, actual controller, and the directors, senior management and core technical personnel of the Company are not involved as parties in any material litigation or arbitration matters that may have an impact on the Issuer.
