Section 12 Appendices

▶

I. Documents Available for Inspection

Investors may inspect all formal legal documents relating to this public offering. Such documents are also disclosed on the designated website, as follows:

  1. Issuance sponsorship letter;
  2. Listing sponsorship letter;
  3. Legal opinion;
  4. Financial statements and audit report;
  5. Articles of Association (draft);
  6. Arrangements for implementing investor relations management regulations, dividend distribution decision-making procedures, and the establishment of shareholder voting mechanisms;
  7. Undertakings related to investor protection;
  8. Undertaking matters made by the Issuer and other responsible parties in relation to the Issuer's current offering and listing;
  9. Internal control audit report;
  10. Schedule of non-recurring profit or loss items certified by certified public accountants;
  11. Explanation of the establishment, improvement, and operation of the shareholders' meeting, board of directors, independent directors, and board secretary systems;
  12. Explanation of the establishment of the audit committee and other special committees;
  13. Important undertakings of core technical personnel, restrictive measures for failure to fulfill undertakings, and the fulfillment status of undertakings for which conditions have been triggered;
  14. Other important documents related to this offering.

1-1-283

Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (宇树科技股份有限公司)                                                           Prospectus

Appendix I: Undertakings Related to This Offering

(I) Undertakings Regarding Lock-up Arrangements for Shares Held by Pre-IPO Shareholders, Voluntary Share Lock-up, and Extension of Lock-up Period

1. Undertakings of the Controlling Shareholder and Actual Controller, Wang Xingxing (王兴兴)

"1. Within 36 months from the date on which the Issuer's shares are listed, I will not transfer or entrust others to manage the shares of the Issuer directly or indirectly held by me prior to this offering and listing, nor will I propose that the Issuer repurchase such shares.

2. Within 4 years from the expiration of the lock-up period for my pre-IPO shares of the Issuer, during the period in which I serve as a core technical personnel of the Company, the number of pre-IPO shares transferred by me in any single year shall not exceed 25% of the total number of the Company's pre-IPO shares held by me at the time of listing, and the reduction ratio may be accumulated and used cumulatively.

3. After the expiration of the above-mentioned lock-up period, during the period in which I serve as a director/senior management member of the Issuer, I will report to the Issuer the shares of the Issuer held by me and any changes thereto; the number of shares transferred in any single year shall not exceed 25% of the total number of shares of the Issuer held by me; within 6 months after my resignation, I shall not transfer the shares of the Issuer held by me. If I resign before the expiration of my term of office, I undertake that during the term of office determined at the time of my appointment and within 6 months after the expiration of such term, I will continue to comply with the above-mentioned restrictive provisions on share transfers by directors/senior management members.

4. Within 2 years after the expiration of the lock-up period, the price at which I reduce my holdings shall not be lower than the offering price (if the Issuer carries out ex-dividend or ex-rights actions such as cash dividend distribution, bonus share issuance, or capitalization of capital reserve between the Issuer's listing and the time of my reduction in holdings, the offering price shall be adjusted accordingly).

5. If, within 6 months after the Issuer's listing, the closing price of the Issuer's shares on 20 consecutive trading days is lower than the offering price, or if the closing price at the end of the 6-month period after listing (if such date is not a trading day, then the first trading day after such date) is lower than the offering price (if the Issuer carries out ex-dividend or ex-rights actions such as cash dividend distribution, bonus share issuance, or capitalization of capital reserve within 6 months after listing, the offering price shall be adjusted accordingly), the lock-up period for shares directly and indirectly held by me in the Issuer shall automatically be extended by 6 months on the basis of the original lock-up period.

6. The above undertakings shall remain continuously valid, and I shall not be released from fulfilling the above undertakings due to changes in position, resignation, or other reasons.

7. If I sell the Issuer's shares in violation of the above undertakings, I agree to bear the legal liability arising from such violation of the above undertakings in accordance with the law.

1-1-284

Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (宇树科技股份有限公司) — Prospectus

8. If laws, regulations, departmental rules, or the CSRC or stock exchange have other provisions regarding the lock-up period applicable to the Issuer's shares held by me, I agree to comply with such provisions."

2. Commitments by Director and Core Technical Personnel Yang Zhiyu (杨知雨) and Zhang Yangguang (张阳光)

"1. Within 12 months from the date on which the Issuer's shares are listed, I will not transfer or entrust others to manage the Issuer's shares that I directly or indirectly hold prior to this issuance and listing, and will not propose that the Issuer repurchase such shares.

2. Within 4 years from the expiration of the lock-up period applicable to my pre-IPO shares, during the period in which I serve as a core technical personnel of the Company, the number of pre-IPO shares I transfer in any given year shall not exceed 25% of the total number of pre-IPO shares I held at the time of listing; the reduction ratio may be accumulated and applied cumulatively.

3. After the expiration of the above lock-up period, during my tenure as a director of the Issuer, I will report to the Issuer the Issuer's shares held by me and any changes thereto; the number of shares I transfer in any given year shall not exceed 25% of the total number of Issuer's shares held by me; I will not transfer the Issuer's shares held by me within 6 months after leaving office. If I resign before the expiration of my term of office, I undertake to continue to comply with the above restrictions on directors' share transfers during the term of office determined at the time of my appointment and within 6 months after the expiration of such term.

4. For any reduction in holdings within 2 years after the expiration of the lock-up period, the reduction price shall not be lower than the issue price (if the Issuer carries out dividend distribution, bonus share issuance, capitalization of capital reserves, or other ex-dividend or ex-rights actions during the period from listing to the time of my reduction, the issue price shall be adjusted accordingly).

5. If, within 6 months after the Issuer's listing, the closing price of the Issuer's shares on any 20 consecutive trading days is below the issue price, or the closing price at the end of the 6-month period after listing (if such day is not a trading day, then the first trading day thereafter) is below the issue price (if the Issuer carries out dividend distribution, bonus share issuance, capitalization of capital reserves, or other ex-dividend or ex-rights actions within 6 months after listing, the issue price shall be adjusted accordingly), the lock-up period applicable to the Issuer's shares directly and indirectly held by me shall be automatically extended by 6 months on the basis of the original lock-up period.

6. The above commitments remain continuously effective, and I shall not abandon performance of the above commitments due to changes in position, resignation, or other reasons.

7. If I fail to fulfill the above commitments and sell the Issuer's shares, I agree to bear the legal liability arising from the breach of the above commitments in accordance with the law.

8. If laws, regulations, departmental rules, or the CSRC or stock exchange have other provisions regarding the lock-up period applicable to the Issuer's shares held by me, I agree to comply with such provisions."

3. Commitments by Director Chen Li (陈立)

"1. Within 12 months from the date on which the Issuer's shares are listed, I will not transfer or entrust others to manage the Issuer's shares that I directly or indirectly hold prior to this issuance and listing, and will not propose that the Issuer repurchase such shares.

2. After the expiration of the above lock-up period, during my tenure as a director of the Issuer, I will report to the Issuer the Issuer's shares held by me and any changes thereto; the number of shares I transfer in any given year shall not exceed 25% of the total number of Issuer's shares held by me; I will not transfer the Issuer's shares held by me within 6 months after leaving office. If I resign before the expiration of my term of office, I undertake to continue to comply with the above restrictions on directors' share transfers during the term of office determined at the time of my appointment and within 6 months after the expiration of such term.

3. For any reduction in holdings within 2 years after the expiration of the lock-up period, the reduction price shall not be lower than the issue price (if the Issuer carries out dividend distribution, bonus share issuance, capitalization of capital reserves, or other ex-dividend or ex-rights actions during the period from listing to the time of my reduction, the issue price shall be adjusted accordingly).

4. If, within 6 months after the Issuer's listing, the closing price of the Issuer's shares on any 20 consecutive trading days is below the issue price, or the closing price at the end of the 6-month period after listing (if such day is not a trading day, then the first trading day thereafter) is below the issue price (if the Issuer carries out dividend distribution, bonus share issuance, capitalization of capital reserves, or other ex-dividend or ex-rights actions within 6 months after listing, the issue price shall be adjusted accordingly), the lock-up period applicable to the Issuer's shares directly and indirectly held by me shall be automatically extended by 6 months on the basis of the original lock-up period.

5. The above commitments remain continuously effective, and I shall not abandon performance of the above commitments due to changes in position, resignation, or other reasons.

6. If I fail to fulfill the above commitments and sell the Issuer's shares, I agree to bear the legal liability arising from the breach of the above commitments in accordance with the law.

7. If laws, regulations, departmental rules, or the CSRC or stock exchange have other provisions regarding the lock-up period applicable to the Issuer's shares held by me, I agree to comply with such provisions."

4. Commitments by Other Shareholders

Shareholder Name Commitment Content
Shanghai Yuyi (上海宇翼)

1. Within 36 months from the date on which the Issuer's shares are listed, this enterprise will not transfer or entrust others to manage the Issuer's shares held by this enterprise prior to this issuance, and will not propose that the Issuer repurchase such shares.

2. For any reduction in holdings within 2 years after the expiration of the lock-up period, the reduction price shall not be lower than the issue price (if the Issuer carries out dividend distribution, bonus share issuance, capitalization of capital reserves, or other ex-dividend or ex-rights actions during the period from listing to the time of this enterprise's reduction, the issue price shall be adjusted accordingly).

3. If, within 6 months after the Issuer's listing, the closing price of the Issuer's shares on any 20 consecutive trading days is below the issue price, or the closing price at the end of the 6-month period after listing (if such day is not a trading day, then the first trading day thereafter) is below the issue price (if the Issuer carries out dividend distribution, bonus share issuance, capitalization of capital reserves, or other ex-dividend or ex-rights actions within 6 months after listing, the issue price shall be adjusted accordingly), the lock-up period applicable to the Issuer's shares directly and indirectly held by this enterprise shall be automatically extended by 6 months on the basis of the original lock-up period.

4. If this enterprise fails to fulfill the above commitments and sells the Issuer's shares, this enterprise agrees to bear the legal liability arising from the breach of the above commitments in accordance with the law.

5. If laws, regulations, departmental rules, or the CSRC or stock exchange have other provisions regarding the lock-up period applicable to the Issuer's shares held by this enterprise, this enterprise agrees to comply with such provisions.
Ningbo Hongshan (宁波红杉)

1. Within 12 months from the date on which the Issuer's shares are listed in this offering, this enterprise will not transfer or entrust others to manage the Issuer's shares held by this enterprise prior to this issuance and listing, and will not propose that the Issuer repurchase such shares.

2. If the Issuer formally submits the registration application documents for this issuance and listing to the stock exchange on or before December 18, 2025 (inclusive) (hereinafter referred to as the "IPO Filing"), then the 22,426,000 newly issued shares obtained by this enterprise on June 18, 2025 through capitalization of the Issuer's capital reserves shall, within 36 months from the date of acquisition of such shares (for the avoidance of doubt, the date of acquisition refers to the date on which the relevant share change completes the business registration change with the market supervision and administration authority, i.e., June 18, 2025), not be transferred or entrusted to others for management by this enterprise, and this enterprise shall not propose that the Issuer repurchase such shares. If the Issuer makes the IPO Filing after December 18, 2025 (exclusive), the aforementioned shares held by this enterprise shall only be locked up for 12 months from the date of listing of the Issuer's shares in this offering pursuant to Item 1 of this commitment.

3. If this enterprise fails to fulfill the above commitments and sells the Issuer's shares, this enterprise agrees to bear the legal liability arising from the breach of the above commitments in accordance with the law.

4. If laws, regulations, departmental rules, or the CSRC or stock exchange have other provisions regarding the lock-up period applicable to the Issuer's shares held by this enterprise, this enterprise agrees to comply with such provisions.
Astrend IV

1. Within 12 months from the date on which the Issuer's shares are listed in this offering, this enterprise will not transfer or entrust others to manage the Issuer's shares held by this enterprise prior to this issuance and listing, and will not propose that the Issuer repurchase such shares.

2. As of the date on which the Issuer files its application for this issuance and listing with the Shanghai Stock Exchange, if the 15,978,125 newly issued shares of the Issuer obtained by this enterprise through capital increase and expansion and capitalization of capital reserves (hereinafter referred to as the "Newly Issued Shares") have been held for less than 6 months, then within 36 months from the date of acquisition of such Newly Issued Shares (for the avoidance of doubt, the date of acquisition refers to the date on which the relevant share change completes the business registration change with the market supervision and administration authority), this enterprise shall not transfer or entrust others to manage such Newly Issued Shares, and shall not propose that the Issuer repurchase such Newly Issued Shares.

3. If this enterprise fails to fulfill the above commitments and sells the Issuer's shares, this enterprise agrees to bear the legal liability arising from the breach of the above commitments in accordance with the law.

4. If laws, regulations, departmental rules, or the CSRC or stock exchange have other provisions regarding the lock-up period applicable to the Issuer's shares held by this enterprise, this enterprise agrees to comply with such provisions.
Yuanma Capital (源码资本)

1. Within 12 months from the date on which the Issuer's shares are listed, this enterprise will not transfer or entrust others to manage the Issuer's shares held by this enterprise prior to this issuance and listing, and will not propose that the Issuer repurchase such shares.

2. Shares acquired by this enterprise from the controlling shareholder or actual controller within 6 months prior to the Issuer's submission of this issuance and listing application shall be subject to the same lock-up as the controlling shareholder and actual controller; within 36 months from the date on which the Issuer's shares are listed, this enterprise shall not transfer or entrust others to manage such shares, and shall not propose that the Issuer repurchase such shares.

3. Newly issued shares of the Issuer (including shares from capital increase and expansion and capitalization of capital reserves) obtained by this enterprise within 6 months prior to the Issuer's submission of this issuance and listing application shall, within 36 months from the date of acquisition of such shares (for the avoidance of doubt, the date of acquisition refers to the date on which the relevant share change completes the business registration change with the market supervision and administration authority; for shares acquired by transfer after the Issuer's conversion into a joint stock company, since no business registration change is required, the date of acquisition is the date on which such change is recorded in the Issuer's register of shareholders), not be transferred or entrusted to others for management by this enterprise, and this enterprise shall not propose that the Issuer repurchase such shares.

4. If this enterprise fails to fulfill the above commitments and sells the Issuer's shares, this enterprise agrees to bear the legal liability arising from the breach of the above commitments in accordance with the law.

5. If laws, regulations, departmental rules, or the CSRC or stock exchange have other provisions regarding the lock-up period applicable to the Issuer's shares held by this enterprise, this enterprise agrees to comply with such provisions.
Xiamen Yaheng (厦门雅恒)

1. Within 12 months from the date on which the Issuer's shares are listed in this offering, this enterprise will not transfer or entrust others to manage the Issuer's shares held by this enterprise prior to this issuance and listing, and will not propose that the Issuer repurchase such shares.

2. If the Issuer makes the IPO Filing on or before December 12, 2025 (inclusive), then the 11,551 newly issued shares obtained by this enterprise on June 12, 2025 through capital increase and expansion of the Issuer shall, within 36 months from the date of acquisition of such shares (for the avoidance of doubt, the date of acquisition refers to the date on which the relevant share change completes the business registration change with the market supervision and administration authority, i.e., June 12, 2025), not be transferred or entrusted to others for management by this enterprise, and this enterprise shall not propose that the Issuer repurchase such shares. If the Issuer makes the IPO Filing after December 12, 2025 (exclusive), the aforementioned shares held by this enterprise shall only be locked up for 12 months from the date of listing of the Issuer's shares in this offering pursuant to Item 1 of this commitment.

3. If the Issuer makes the IPO Filing on or before December 18, 2025 (inclusive), then the 3,268,000 newly issued shares obtained by this enterprise on June 18, 2025 through capitalization of the Issuer's capital reserves shall, within 36 months from the date of acquisition of such shares (for the avoidance of doubt, the date of acquisition refers to the date on which the relevant share change completes the business registration change with the market supervision and administration authority, i.e., June 18, 2025), not be transferred or entrusted to others for management by this enterprise, and this enterprise shall not propose that the Issuer repurchase such shares. If the Issuer makes the IPO Filing after December 18, 2025 (exclusive), the aforementioned shares held by this enterprise shall only be locked up for 12 months from the date of listing of the Issuer's shares in this offering pursuant to Item 1 of this commitment.

4. If this enterprise fails to fulfill the above commitments and sells the Issuer's shares, this enterprise agrees to bear the legal liability arising from the breach of the above commitments in accordance with the law.

5. If laws, regulations, departmental rules, or the CSRC or stock exchange have other provisions regarding the lock-up period applicable to the Issuer's shares held by this enterprise, this enterprise agrees to comply with such provisions.
Guanghe Erqi (光合贰期), Hangzhou Chuxin (杭州初心), Tencent Technology (腾讯科技), Wuxi Jinqiu (无锡锦秋), Hangzhou Haoyue (杭州灏月), Tianjin Suanli (天津算力), Xiangfeng Xiamen (祥峰厦门), Shanghai Yunyang (上海云玚), Hechuang Investment (合创投资), Guangyue Investment (光越投资), China Mobile Héchuang (中移和创)

1. Within 12 months from the date on which the Issuer's shares are listed, this enterprise will not transfer or entrust others to manage the Issuer's shares held by this enterprise prior to this issuance and listing, and will not propose that the Issuer repurchase such shares.

2. This enterprise became a new shareholder of the Issuer within 12 months prior to the Issuer's submission of this issuance and listing application; the shares held shall, within 36 months from the date of acquisition (for the avoidance of doubt, the date of acquisition refers to the date on which the relevant share change completes the business registration change with the market supervision and administration authority; for shares acquired by transfer after the Issuer's conversion into a joint stock company, since no business registration change is required, the date of acquisition is the date on which such change is recorded in the Issuer's register of shareholders), not be transferred or entrusted to others for management by this enterprise, and this enterprise shall not propose that the Issuer repurchase such shares.

3. Newly issued shares of the Issuer (including shares from capital increase and expansion and capitalization of capital reserves) obtained by this enterprise within 6 months prior to the Issuer's submission of this issuance and listing application shall, within 36 months from the date of acquisition of such shares, not be transferred or entrusted to others for management by this enterprise, and this enterprise shall not propose that the Issuer repurchase such shares.

4. If this enterprise fails to fulfill the above commitments and sells the Issuer's shares, this enterprise agrees to bear the legal liability arising from the breach of the above commitments in accordance with the law.

5. If laws, regulations, departmental rules, or the CSRC or stock exchange have other provisions regarding the lock-up period applicable to the Issuer's shares held by this enterprise, this enterprise agrees to comply with such provisions.
Hanhai Information (汉海信息), Jingwei Yihao (经纬壹号), Jinshi Chengzhang (金石成长), Robot Fund (机器人基金), Jiaxing Huamao (嘉兴骅茂), Junwan Hongyi (君万弘毅), Zhongwang Investment (中网投资), Vertex (嘉兴睿利), Haikesikang (海克斯康), Xinjiang Shenchuang Tou (新疆深创投), Jingwei Sanhao (经纬叁号), Zhejiang Rongteng (浙江容腾), Galaxy Z, Chengdu Longzhu (成都龙珠), Zhongguancun Kexuecheng (中关村科学城), Rongteng Erhao (容腾二号), Jisi Investment (极思投资), Dexun Investment (德迅投资), Guangzhou Chuxin (广州初心), Hupo Anyun (琥珀安云), Jiangsu Jiequan (江苏疌泉), Chuangxin Capital (创新资本), Shanghai Kechuang (上海科创), Zhongzheng Investment (中证投资), Shenchuang Tou Group (深创投集团), Junshi Chuangtou (钧石创投), Weifang Chuxin (潍坊初心), Shanghai Mida (上海米达)

1. Within 12 months from the date on which the Issuer's shares are listed, this enterprise will not transfer or entrust others to manage the Issuer's shares held by this enterprise prior to this issuance and listing, and will not propose that the Issuer repurchase such shares.

2. Newly issued shares of the Issuer (including shares from capital increase and expansion and capitalization of capital reserves) obtained by this enterprise within 6 months prior to the Issuer's submission of this issuance and listing application shall, within 36 months from the date of acquisition of such shares (for the avoidance of doubt, the date of acquisition refers to the date on which the relevant share change completes the business registration change with the market supervision and administration authority; for shares acquired by transfer after the Issuer's conversion into a joint stock company, since no business registration change is required, the date of acquisition is the date on which such change is recorded in the Issuer's register of shareholders), not be transferred or entrusted to others for management by this enterprise, and this enterprise shall not propose that the Issuer repurchase such shares.

3. If this enterprise fails to fulfill the above commitments and sells the Issuer's shares, this enterprise agrees to bear the legal liability arising from the breach of the above commitments in accordance with the law.

4. If laws, regulations, departmental rules, or the CSRC or stock exchange have other provisions regarding the lock-up period applicable to the Issuer's shares held by this enterprise, this enterprise agrees to comply with such provisions.

(II) Commitments Regarding Shareholding Intentions and Reduction Intentions After the IPO

1. Commitments by Controlling Shareholder and Actual Controller Wang Xingxing (王兴兴)

"1. With respect to the Company's shares held by me prior to this issuance and listing, I will strictly comply with the commitments already made regarding share lock-up and will not sell the Company's shares held prior to this issuance and listing during the lock-up period. I remain continuously optimistic about the Company's business prospects, fully support the Company's development, and intend to hold the Company's shares for the long term.

2. Within 2 years after the expiration of the lock-up period, subject to the following conditions, I may reduce my holdings: (1) the above lock-up period has expired and there are no circumstances requiring extension of the lock-up period; if the lock-up period is extended, the same shall be postponed accordingly; (2) if circumstances arise requiring compensation to investors, such compensation obligations have been fulfilled in accordance with the law.

3. After the expiration of the lock-up period, if I intend to reduce my holdings in the Company's shares, I will, in compliance with the relevant laws, regulations, and normative documents and the public commitments already made by me, select methods permitted by laws and regulations such as centralized competitive bidding, block trading, and negotiated transfer to reduce my holdings, and the reduction price shall be determined based on the prevailing secondary market price. For reductions within 2 years after the expiration of the lock-up period, the reduction price shall not be lower than the issue price at the time of this issuance and listing (if there are ex-rights or ex-dividend events, the issue price shall be adjusted accordingly).

4. I undertake that the reduction of the Issuer's shares shall strictly comply with the relevant laws and regulations of the CSRC and the Shanghai Stock Exchange, and a public announcement shall be made 3 trading days in advance; for the first reduction through centralized competitive bidding or block trading on the stock exchange, a public announcement shall be made 15 trading days prior to the reduction. I will fulfill my information disclosure obligations in a timely and accurate manner in accordance with the rules applicable at the time as required by the securities regulatory authorities and the stock exchange.

5. During the period of holding the Issuer's shares, if the laws, regulations, normative documents, policies, and requirements of securities regulatory authorities regarding share reduction change, I will apply the amended laws, regulations, normative documents, policies, and requirements of securities regulatory authorities as required by the relevant laws and regulations."

2. Commitments by Shanghai Yuyi (上海宇翼)

"1. With respect to the Company's shares held by this enterprise prior to this issuance and listing, this enterprise will strictly comply with the commitments already made regarding share lock-up and will not sell the Company's shares held prior to this issuance and listing during the lock-up period. This enterprise remains continuously optimistic about the Company's business prospects, fully supports the Company's development, and intends to hold the Company's shares for the long term.

After the expiration of the lock-up period, if this enterprise intends to reduce its holdings in the Company's shares, it will, in compliance with the relevant laws, regulations, and normative documents and the public commitments already made by this enterprise, select methods permitted by laws and regulations such as centralized competitive bidding, block trading, and negotiated transfer to reduce its holdings, and the reduction price shall be determined based on the prevailing secondary market price. For reductions within 2 years after the expiration of the lock-up period, the reduction price shall not be lower than the issue price at the time of this issuance and listing (if there are ex-rights or ex-dividend events, the issue price shall be adjusted accordingly).

This enterprise will fulfill its information disclosure obligations in a timely and accurate manner in accordance with the rules applicable at the time as required by the securities regulatory authorities and the stock exchange.

5. During the period of holding the Issuer's shares, if the laws, regulations, normative documents, policies, and requirements of securities regulatory authorities regarding share reduction change, this enterprise will apply the amended laws, regulations, normative documents, policies, and requirements of securities regulatory authorities as required by the relevant laws and regulations."

4. Commitments by Shareholders Jingwei Yihao (经纬壹号) and Jingwei Sanhao (经纬叁号)

"1. With respect to the Company's shares held by this enterprise prior to this issuance and listing, this enterprise will strictly comply with the commitments already made regarding share lock-up and will not sell the Company's shares held prior to this issuance and listing during the lock-up period.

2. Within 2 years after the expiration of the lock-up period, subject to not violating the relevant laws, regulations, and normative documents and the other public commitments made by this enterprise, there is a possibility that this enterprise may appropriately reduce its holdings in the Issuer's shares.

3. After the expiration of the lock-up period, if this enterprise intends to reduce its holdings in the Company's shares, it will, in compliance with the relevant laws, regulations, and normative documents and the public commitments already made by this enterprise, select methods permitted by laws and regulations such as centralized competitive bidding, block trading, and negotiated transfer to reduce its holdings, and the reduction price shall be determined based on the prevailing secondary market price. For reductions within 2 years after the expiration of the lock-up period, the reduction price shall not be lower than the most recently audited net assets per share of the Issuer at the time of reduction (if after the latest audit base date the Company's net assets or total number of shares change due to capitalization of capital reserves, distribution of shares or cash dividends, new share issuance, rights issue, or share consolidation, the net assets per share shall be adjusted accordingly).

4. This enterprise undertakes that the reduction of the Issuer's shares shall strictly comply with the relevant laws and regulations of the CSRC and the Shanghai Stock Exchange. When this enterprise and its affiliates collectively hold more than 5% of the Company's shares, this enterprise will notify the Issuer in writing in advance of its reduction intention and proposed reduction quantity, and the Issuer shall make a public announcement of the relevant reduction plan 3 trading days prior to the reduction; for the first reduction through centralized competitive bidding or block trading on the stock exchange, a public announcement shall be made 15 trading days prior to the reduction. This enterprise will fulfill its information disclosure obligations in a timely and accurate manner in accordance with the rules applicable at the time as required by the securities regulatory authorities and the stock exchange.

5. During the period of holding the Issuer's shares, if the laws, regulations, normative documents, policies, and requirements of securities regulatory authorities regarding share reduction change, this enterprise will apply the amended laws, regulations, normative documents, policies, and requirements of securities regulatory authorities as required by the relevant laws and regulations."

"1. With respect to the Company's shares held by this enterprise prior to this issuance and listing, this enterprise will strictly comply with the commitments already made regarding share lock-up and will not sell the Company's shares held prior to this issuance and listing during the lock-up period.

Within 2 years after the expiration of the lock-up period, subject to not violating the relevant laws, regulations, and normative documents and the other public commitments made by this enterprise, there is a possibility that this enterprise may appropriately reduce its holdings in the Issuer's shares.

3. After the expiration of the lock-up period, if this enterprise intends to reduce its holdings in the Company's shares, it will, in compliance with the relevant laws, regulations, and normative documents and the public commitments already made by this enterprise, select methods permitted by laws and regulations such as centralized competitive bidding, block trading, and negotiated transfer to reduce its holdings, and the reduction price shall be determined based on the prevailing secondary market price. For reductions within 2 years after the expiration of the lock-up period, the reduction price shall not be lower than the most recently audited net assets per share of the Issuer at the time of reduction (if after the latest audit base date the Company's net assets or total number of shares change due to capitalization of capital reserves, distribution of shares or cash dividends, new share issuance, rights issue, or share consolidation, the net assets per share shall be adjusted accordingly).

4. This enterprise undertakes that the reduction of the Issuer's shares shall strictly comply with the relevant laws and regulations of the CSRC and the Shanghai Stock Exchange. When this enterprise and its persons acting in concert collectively hold more than 5% of the Company's shares, this enterprise will notify the Issuer in writing in advance of its reduction intention and proposed reduction quantity, and the Issuer shall make a public announcement of the relevant reduction plan 3 trading days prior to the reduction; for the first reduction through centralized competitive bidding or block trading on the stock exchange, a public announcement shall be made 15 trading days prior to the reduction. This enterprise will fulfill its information disclosure obligations in a timely and accurate manner in accordance with the rules applicable at the time as required by the securities regulatory authorities and the stock exchange.

5. 6. Commitments by Shareholder Ningbo Hongshan (宁波红杉)

"1. With respect to the Company's shares held by this enterprise prior to this issuance and listing, this enterprise will strictly comply with the commitments already made regarding share lock-up and will not sell the Company's shares held prior to this issuance and listing during the lock-up period.

2. After the expiration of the lock-up period, subject to not violating the relevant laws, regulations, and normative documents, there is a possibility that this enterprise may reduce its holdings in the Issuer's shares.

3. After the expiration of the lock-up period, if this enterprise intends to reduce its holdings in the Company's shares, it will, in compliance with the relevant laws, regulations, and normative documents and the public commitments already made by this enterprise, select methods permitted by laws and regulations such as centralized competitive bidding, block trading, and negotiated transfer to reduce its holdings, and the reduction price shall be determined based on the prevailing secondary market price. The reduction price shall not be lower than the lower of: (i) the per-share cost price corresponding to this enterprise's investment amount at the time of reduction (if the Issuer carries out dividend distribution, bonus share issuance, capitalization of capital reserves, or other ex-dividend or ex-rights actions during the period from the Issuer's listing to the time of this enterprise's reduction, the aforementioned price shall be adjusted for ex-rights and ex-dividends accordingly); and (ii) the net assets per share of the Company for the most recently audited fiscal year at the time of reduction (if the Issuer carries out dividend distribution, bonus share issuance, capitalization of capital reserves, or other ex-dividend or ex-rights actions during the period from the most recently audited fiscal year to the time of this enterprise's reduction, the net assets per share price shall be adjusted for ex-rights and ex-dividends accordingly).

4. This enterprise undertakes that the reduction of the Issuer's shares shall strictly comply with the relevant laws and regulations of the CSRC and the Shanghai Stock Exchange. When this enterprise and its persons acting in concert collectively hold more than 5% of the Company's shares, this enterprise will fulfill its information disclosure obligations in a timely and accurate manner in accordance with the rules applicable at the time as required by the securities regulatory authorities and the stock exchange.

5. During the period of holding the Issuer's shares, if the laws, regulations, normative documents, policies, and requirements of securities regulatory authorities regarding share reduction change, this enterprise will apply the amended laws, regulations, normative documents, policies, and requirements of securities regulatory authorities as required by the relevant laws and regulations."

"1. 2. After the expiration of the lock-up period, subject to not violating the relevant laws, regulations, and normative documents, there is a possibility that this enterprise may reduce its holdings in the Issuer's shares.

4. This enterprise undertakes that the reduction of the Issuer's shares shall strictly comply with the relevant laws and regulations of the CSRC and the Shanghai Stock Exchange. When this enterprise and its persons acting in concert collectively hold more than 5% of the Company's shares, this enterprise will fulfill its information disclosure obligations in a timely and accurate manner in accordance with the rules applicable at the time as required by the securities regulatory authorities and the stock exchange.

(III) Commitments Regarding Share Price Stabilization After the IPO

"I. Specific Conditions Triggering Share Price Stabilization Measures

(1) Trigger conditions: Within 36 months after the Issuer (i.e., the Company, same below) completes its IPO and lists on the STAR Market, if not caused by force majeure, the closing price of the Issuer's shares on any 20 consecutive trading days is below the audited net assets per share for the Issuer's preceding fiscal year (hereinafter referred to as the "Trigger Conditions"; if ex-rights or ex-dividend events render the above closing prices and the Company's audited net assets per share for the preceding fiscal year incomparable, the above closing prices shall be adjusted accordingly), and the Issuer and relevant parties simultaneously satisfy the requirements of laws, administrative regulations, departmental rules, normative documents, and securities regulatory authorities regarding share repurchase, share purchase increase, and other changes in equity, the Issuer, its controlling shareholder, actual controller, directors (excluding independent directors), and senior management shall implement specific share price stabilization measures in accordance with laws and regulations, the Company's articles of association, and the share price stabilization plan.

(2) Cessation conditions: Before or during the implementation of specific share price stabilization measures, if the closing price of the Issuer's shares on any 20 consecutive trading days is above the Issuer's audited net assets per share for the preceding fiscal year, the implementation of share price stabilization measures shall cease.

II. Specific Measures for Stabilizing Share Price

When the aforementioned Trigger Conditions are met, the Issuer, as the primary party responsible for stabilizing its share price, shall promptly take some or all of the following measures to stabilize the Issuer's share price:

(1) Share Repurchase by the Issuer

① When the aforementioned Trigger Conditions are met, the Issuer shall first repurchase a portion of its shares from public shareholders in accordance with the provisions of the Rules on Share Repurchase by Listed Companies, while ensuring that the results of the repurchase will not cause the Issuer's share distribution to fail to meet the listing conditions. In accordance with laws, regulations, and the Company's articles of association, the Issuer shall convene a board of directors meeting to deliberate on the share price stabilization plan within 10 trading days from the date on which the Trigger Conditions are met and submit the plan to the shareholders' meeting for approval. A resolution by the Issuer's shareholders' meeting on the relevant proposal for share repurchase shall require approval by more than two-thirds of the voting rights held by shareholders present at the meeting. The specific implementation plan shall be announced after the Trigger Conditions are met and after the Issuer convenes the board of directors and shareholders' meeting in accordance with the law to pass a resolution on share repurchase.

② The funds used by the Issuer for share repurchase must be lawful and compliant; the price at which shares are repurchased shall not exceed the audited net assets per share as of the end of the preceding fiscal year; the method of share repurchase shall be centralized competitive bidding, tender offer, or other methods recognized by the securities regulatory authorities. If the Issuer's share price no longer satisfies the Trigger Conditions prior to the implementation of the share repurchase plan, the Issuer may refrain from implementing such plan.

③ If the Issuer's share price meets the Trigger Conditions multiple times within a given fiscal year (excluding situations during the implementation of share price stabilization measures and situations where the closing price of the shares on 20 consecutive trading days counted from the date of announcement of the completion of that particular share price stabilization measure is below the Issuer's net assets per share as of the end of the preceding fiscal year), the Issuer shall continue to implement the above share price stabilization plan subject to the following principles:

  • A. The amount of funds used for a single share repurchase shall not exceed 20% of the net profit attributable to shareholders of the parent company as of the end of the preceding fiscal year;
  • B. The total amount of repurchase funds used for share price stabilization in a single fiscal year shall not exceed 50% of the net profit attributable to shareholders of the parent company as of the end of the preceding fiscal year.

If the above thresholds are exceeded, the relevant share price stabilization measures shall no longer be implemented in the current year; however, if share price stabilization measures need to be initiated again in the following year, the Issuer shall continue to implement the share price stabilization plan in accordance with the above principles.

④ The total cumulative amount of funds used by the Issuer for share repurchase shall not exceed the total proceeds raised by the Issuer through its IPO net of issuance expenses.

(2) Share Purchase Increase by the Controlling Shareholder and Actual Controller

① When the aforementioned Trigger Conditions are met, if the Issuer is unable to implement share repurchase, or the shareholders' meeting resolves not to repurchase shares, or the relevant proposal for share repurchase fails to obtain the approval of the shareholders' meeting, and provided that the timing and conditions for the controlling shareholder and actual controller to increase their holdings of shares comply with the relevant laws, regulations, normative documents, and the regulatory rules of the CSRC and the stock exchange, and that the share purchase increase will not cause the Issuer's share distribution to fail to meet the listing conditions, the controlling shareholder and actual controller shall stabilize the share price by increasing their holdings of the Issuer's shares.

The controlling shareholder and actual controller shall propose a plan for increasing their holdings of the Issuer's shares (including the quantity, price range, and timing of the share purchase increase) within 10 trading days after the conditions for initiating share price stabilization measures are met, fulfill the required approval procedures in accordance with the law, notify the Issuer within 3 trading days after obtaining approval, and the Issuer shall disclose the plan for the controlling shareholder and actual controller to increase their holdings of the Issuer's shares in accordance with relevant regulations; 3 trading days after the Issuer discloses the plan for the controlling shareholder and actual controller to increase their holdings of the Issuer's shares, the controlling shareholder and actual controller shall begin to implement the plan for increasing their holdings of the Issuer's shares in accordance with the plan.

② The price at which the controlling shareholder and actual controller increase their holdings of the Issuer's shares shall not exceed the Issuer's audited net assets per share as of the end of the preceding fiscal year; if the Issuer's share price no longer satisfies the Trigger Conditions prior to the implementation of the share purchase increase plan, the controlling shareholder and actual controller may refrain from implementing such plan.

③ If the Issuer's share price meets the Trigger Conditions multiple times within a given fiscal year (excluding situations during the implementation of share price stabilization measures and situations where the closing price of the shares on 20 consecutive trading days counted from the date of announcement of the completion of that particular share price stabilization measure is still below the Issuer's net assets per share as of the end of the preceding fiscal year), the controlling shareholder and actual controller shall continue to implement the above share price stabilization plan subject to the following principles:

  • A. The amount of funds used for a single share purchase increase for share price stabilization shall not be less than 20% of the cumulative cash dividends received by the controlling shareholder and actual controller from the Issuer since the Issuer's listing;
  • B. The total amount of funds used by the controlling shareholder and actual controller for share purchase increase for share price stabilization in a single fiscal year shall not exceed 50% of the cumulative cash dividends received by the controlling shareholder and actual controller from the Issuer since the Issuer's listing.

If the above thresholds are exceeded, the relevant share price stabilization measures shall no longer be implemented in the current year; however, if share price stabilization measures need to be initiated again in the following year, the controlling shareholder and actual controller shall continue to implement the share price stabilization plan in accordance with the above principles; when share price stabilization measures are triggered in the following year, funds previously used for share purchase increase for share price stabilization in prior years shall no longer be counted toward cumulative cash dividends.

④ The total cumulative amount of funds used by the controlling shareholder and actual controller for share purchase increase for share price stabilization shall not exceed the total cumulative cash dividends received by the controlling shareholder and actual controller from the Issuer since the Issuer's listing.

⑤ If the Issuer has initiated share price stabilization measures after the conditions requiring such measures are triggered, the controlling shareholder and actual controller may choose to initiate share price stabilization measures simultaneously with the Issuer, or to initiate the above measures after the Issuer's share price stabilization measures have been fully implemented (as of the date of the Issuer's announcement of completion), if the Issuer's share price is still below the audited net assets per share as of the end of the preceding fiscal year. If the Issuer's share price no longer meets the conditions for initiating share price stabilization measures after the Issuer implements such measures, the controlling shareholder and actual controller may refrain from implementing the above share price stabilization measures.

(3) Share Purchase Increase by Directors (Excluding Unpaid Non-Independent Directors and Independent Directors) and Senior Management

① When the aforementioned Trigger Conditions are met, if the Issuer and its controlling shareholder and actual controller have all taken and fully implemented share price stabilization measures but the Issuer's share price is still below its net assets per share as of the end of the preceding fiscal year, the Issuer's directors (excluding unpaid non-independent directors and independent directors) and senior management shall initiate a plan to purchase the Issuer's shares. The Issuer's directors (excluding unpaid non-independent directors and independent directors) and senior management shall, provided that the timing and conditions for increasing their holdings of shares comply with the relevant laws, regulations, normative documents, and the regulatory rules of the CSRC and the stock exchange, and that the share purchase increase will not cause the Issuer's share distribution to fail to meet the listing conditions, purchase the Issuer's shares through the secondary market by means of competitive bidding in order to stabilize the Issuer's share price. The Issuer shall disclose their plan to purchase the Issuer's shares in accordance with relevant regulations. Three trading days after the Issuer discloses their plan to purchase the Issuer's shares, they shall begin to implement the plan to purchase the Issuer's shares in accordance with the plan.

② The price at which the Issuer's directors (excluding unpaid non-independent directors and independent directors) and senior management purchase the Issuer's shares through the secondary market by means of competitive bidding shall not exceed the Issuer's audited net assets per share as of the end of the preceding fiscal year; if the Issuer's share price no longer satisfies the Trigger Conditions within 3 trading days after the Issuer discloses their purchase plan, the Issuer's directors (excluding unpaid non-independent directors and independent directors) and senior management may refrain from implementing such plan.

③ If the Issuer's share price meets the Trigger Conditions multiple times within a given fiscal year (excluding situations during the implementation of share price stabilization measures and situations where the closing price of the shares on 20 consecutive trading days counted from the date of announcement of the completion of that particular share price stabilization measure is still below the Issuer's net assets per share as of the end of the preceding fiscal year), the Issuer's directors (excluding unpaid non-independent directors and independent directors) and senior management shall continue to implement the above share price stabilization plan subject to the following principles:

  • A. The amount of funds used for a single purchase of shares for share price stabilization shall not be less than 20% of the cumulative after-tax remuneration received by the relevant individual from the Issuer in the preceding fiscal year during their tenure as director or senior management;
  • B. The total amount of funds used by such individual for purchasing shares for share price stabilization in a single fiscal year shall not exceed 50% of the cumulative after-tax remuneration received by the relevant individual from the Issuer in the preceding fiscal year during their tenure as director or senior management.

④ If the above thresholds are exceeded, the relevant share price stabilization measures shall no longer be implemented in the current year; however, if share price stabilization measures need to be initiated again in the following year, the Issuer's directors (excluding unpaid non-independent directors and independent directors) and senior management shall continue to implement the share price stabilization plan in accordance with the above principles.

III. Restraining Measures

If the controlling shareholder, actual controller, directors (excluding unpaid non-independent directors and independent directors), and senior management fail to fulfill the above commitments, the controlling shareholder, actual controller, directors (excluding unpaid non-independent directors and independent directors), and senior management shall issue a public apology to investors; the controlling shareholder, actual controller, directors who are shareholders (excluding unpaid non-independent directors and independent directors), and senior management who fail to fulfill the above commitments shall not participate in the Issuer's cash dividend distribution for the current year, and the cash dividends to which they are entitled shall belong to the Issuer; at the same time, 50% of the annual remuneration owed to all directors (excluding unpaid non-independent directors and independent directors) and senior management from the Issuer for the current year shall belong to the Issuer. Directors (excluding unpaid non-independent directors and independent directors) and senior management newly appointed within three years after the Issuer's listing must first sign this commitment, and this commitment shall be equally binding on directors (excluding unpaid non-independent directors and independent directors) and senior management newly appointed within three years after the Issuer's listing."

(IV) Commitments Regarding Share Repurchase

1. Commitments by the Issuer and Controlling Shareholder and Actual Controller Wang Xingxing (王兴兴)

"1. If the securities regulatory authorities or other competent authorities determine that there are any false records, misleading statements, or material omissions in the Prospectus and other information disclosure documents for this issuance and listing of the Company, and such circumstances constitute a material and substantive impact on determining whether the Company meets the statutory conditions for issuance, and registration has been obtained through fraudulent means and the shares have already been issued and listed, the Company undertakes to repurchase in accordance with the law all of the new shares issued in this offering.

2. If the above circumstances occur during the stage where the new shares issued in this public offering have been fully issued but not yet listed for trading, the Company shall, within 5 working days from the date of occurrence of the above circumstances, refund the proceeds raised from this public offering of A shares to investors who have paid share subscription funds, at the issue price plus interest on bank deposits for the same period.

3. If the above circumstances occur after the new shares issued in this public offering have been listed for trading, the Company's board of directors shall, within 10 working days after the CSRC or other competent authorities make a final determination or administrative penalty decision on the above facts in accordance with the law, formulate a share repurchase plan and submit it to the shareholders' meeting for deliberation and approval, and repurchase in accordance with the law all of the new shares issued in this public offering, at the issue price plus current bank demand deposit interest for the period from the date of listing of the new shares to the date of repurchase, or at a price not lower than the arithmetic average of the daily weighted average prices of the Company's shares for the 30 trading days prior to the date on which the CSRC files a case for investigation of false records, misleading statements, or material omissions in the Company's Prospectus and other information disclosure documents (if the Company has any ex-rights or ex-dividend events such as dividend distribution, bonus share issuance, capitalization of capital reserves, rights issue, or share consolidation, the aforementioned price shall be adjusted accordingly), or at such other price as recognized by the CSRC, through the stock exchange's trading system to repurchase all of the new shares issued in this public offering.

4. When the Company is involved in share repurchase in the future, the Company shall simultaneously comply with the relevant provisions of the CSRC and the Shanghai Stock Exchange and other securities regulatory authorities. The actual controller and controlling shareholder shall supervise and guarantee that the Issuer fulfills the above commitments."

(V) Commitments Regarding Share Buyback in the Event of Fraudulent Issuance and Listing

1. Commitments by the Issuer

"1. This Company's issuance and listing does not involve any fraudulent issuance.

2. If this Company does not meet the conditions for issuance and listing, and has obtained registration through deception and has already issued and listed shares,

market, the Company shall initiate a share repurchase procedure within 5 working days after the illegal facts of such fraudulent issuance are finally confirmed by the China Securities Regulatory Commission or other competent authorities, and shall repurchase all new shares issued in this offering in accordance with the law.

3. The above commitments are the genuine expression of the Company's intent. If the Company violates the above commitments, the Company shall bear corresponding legal liability in accordance with the law."

2. Commitments of the Controlling Shareholder and Actual Controller Wang Xingxing (王兴兴)

"1. There are no circumstances of fraudulent issuance in connection with the Issuer's current offering and listing.

2. If the Issuer does not meet the conditions for issuance and listing, and has obtained registration for issuance by fraudulent means and has already completed the issuance and listing, I shall initiate a share repurchase procedure within 5 working days after the illegal facts of such fraudulent issuance are finally confirmed by the China Securities Regulatory Commission or other competent authorities, and shall repurchase all new shares issued by the Issuer in this offering in accordance with the law.

3. The above commitments are the genuine expression of my intent. If I violate the above commitments, I shall bear corresponding legal liability in accordance with the law."

(VI) Commitments Regarding Measures to Compensate for Dilution of Immediate Returns

1. Commitments of the Issuer

"Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (宇树科技股份有限公司) (hereinafter referred to as 'the Company') intends to apply for an initial public offering of shares and listing on the STAR Market (hereinafter referred to as 'this Offering and Listing'). In order to reduce the impact of the dilution of immediate returns caused by this Offering, the Company commits to enhancing the Company's sustainable development capabilities and improving the Company's business scale and operational performance by further improving the Company's management level, strengthening the management and utilization efficiency of the raised funds, accelerating the investment progress of the fundraising projects, and strictly implementing the cash dividend policy. The specific measures to be taken are as follows:

1. Further improve the Company's management level and enhance the efficiency of fund utilization

The Company will improve and strengthen budget management, control various expense expenditures of the Company, enhance the efficiency of fund utilization, comprehensively and effectively control the Company's operational and management risks, and improve operational efficiency and profitability. In addition, the Company will improve the compensation and incentive mechanisms, introduce outstanding market talent, and maximize employee motivation to tap into the creativity and potential drive of the Company's employees. Through the above measures, the Company will comprehensively enhance operational efficiency, reduce costs, and improve the Company's operational performance.

2. Strengthen raised funds management and ensure the proper and standardized use of raised funds

The Company has formulated the Raised Funds Management System in accordance with the provisions of the Company Law, the Securities Law, the Regulatory Rules for Raised Funds of Listed Companies, and other laws, regulations, normative documents, and the Articles of Association. With respect to the use and management of the funds raised in this Offering, the Company has passed relevant resolutions for the establishment of dedicated accounts. After the raised funds are in place, they will be deposited in the dedicated accounts designated by the Company. The Company will strictly manage the use of raised funds in accordance with relevant regulations and the requirements of the Raised Funds Management System, and will actively cooperate with supervisory banks and the sponsor in inspecting and supervising the use of raised funds to ensure the proper and standardized use of raised funds and effectively prevent risks associated with their use.

3. Accelerate the investment progress of fundraising projects and achieve expected returns as soon as possible

The implementation of the investment projects funded by the raised funds meets the Company's needs for expanding production capacity, optimizing the industrial structure, and adjusting the product structure, improves the Company's research and development capabilities, and further promotes brand building, continuously consolidating and increasing the Company's market share, which will play a positive role in improving the Company's overall performance. After the raised funds from this Offering are in place, the Company will accelerate the construction of each fundraising project, actively allocate resources, and strive to shorten the project construction period while ensuring construction quality, with the aim of completing each project as early as possible and achieving the expected returns.

4. Strictly implement the cash dividend policy and strengthen the investor return mechanism

The Company has amended its Articles of Association in accordance with relevant laws and regulations and established a sound and effective shareholder return mechanism. After the completion of this Offering, the Company will, in accordance with the provisions of laws and regulations and the Articles of Association, actively promote profit distribution to shareholders when the conditions for profit distribution are met, and effectively safeguard and increase returns to shareholders."

2. Commitments of the Controlling Shareholder and Actual Controller Wang Xingxing (王兴兴)

"1. I will not overstep my authority to interfere with the Issuer's operational and management activities, and will not encroach upon the Issuer's interests;

2. I will faithfully fulfill the obligations of a controlling shareholder and actual controller, diligently and loyally perform my duties, and safeguard the legitimate rights and interests of the Company and all shareholders;

3. I will not transfer benefits to other entities or individuals gratuitously or on unfair terms, nor will I damage the Company's interests by other means;

4. I will not use the Company's assets to engage in investment or consumption activities unrelated to the performance of my duties;

5. I will faithfully implement the measures formulated by the Issuer regarding compensation for diluted immediate returns and this letter of commitment;

6. After the date of issuance of this commitment, if the CSRC or the Shanghai Stock Exchange issues other requirements regarding measures to compensate for returns and related commitments, and the above commitments fail to meet the relevant requirements of the regulatory authorities, I commit to issue supplementary commitments in accordance with the relevant regulations at that time."

3. Commitments of Directors and Senior Management

"1. I commit not to transfer benefits to other entities or individuals gratuitously or on unfair terms, nor to damage the Issuer's interests by other means;

2. I commit to exercise restraint over my own job-related consumption behavior;

3. I commit not to use the Issuer's assets to engage in investment or consumption activities unrelated to the performance of my duties;

4. Within the scope of my duties and authority, I will make every effort to ensure that the compensation system formulated by the Company's Board of Directors or Remuneration and Assessment Committee is linked to the Company's implementation of measures to compensate for returns;

5. If the Company intends to implement equity incentive plans in the future, within the scope of my duties and authority, I will make every effort to ensure that the exercise conditions of the proposed equity incentive plans are linked to the Company's implementation of measures to compensate for returns;

6. After the date of issuance of this commitment, if the CSRC or the Shanghai Stock Exchange issues other requirements regarding measures to compensate for returns and related commitments, and the above commitments fail to meet the relevant requirements of the regulatory authorities, I commit to issue supplementary commitments in accordance with the relevant regulations at that time."

(VII) Commitments of the Issuer Regarding Profit Distribution

"1. Distribution Methods

On the premise that the Company is profitable and the cash flow meets the Company's normal operations and long-term development needs, the Company will give priority to cash dividends; where the Company anticipates good future development prospects and the Company's development has significant cash requirements, the Company may distribute dividends in the form of stock dividends.

2. Conditions for Implementing Cash Distribution

The distributable profit realized by the Company in that year (i.e., the after-tax profit remaining after the Company makes up for losses and draws reserve funds) is positive, and cash flow is sufficient, and the implementation of cash dividends will not affect the Company's subsequent continued operations; the Company's cumulative distributable profit is positive; the auditing firm has issued a standard unqualified audit opinion on the Company's financial report for that year; and the Company has no major investment plans or major cash expenditures (except for fundraising projects).

3. Conditions for Implementing Stock Dividends

When the Company's operations are in good condition and the Board of Directors believes that the distribution of stock dividends is in the overall interest of all shareholders of the Company, the Company may propose a stock dividend distribution plan on the premise of ensuring adequate cash dividend distribution. When the Company uses stock dividends to distribute profits, it shall fully consider whether the total share capital after the distribution of stock dividends is compatible with the Company's current operating scale, profit growth rate, and the dilution of net assets per share, in order to ensure that the profit distribution plan is in the overall and long-term interests of all shareholders. The Company's dividend distribution shall not exceed the scope of cumulative distributable profits.

4. Proportion of Cash Distribution

(1) The Company shall maintain the continuity and stability of its profit distribution policy. When the conditions for cash dividends are met, in principle, the Company's annual cash dividends shall not be less than 10% of the distributable profits realized in that year. Distributable profits not distributed in the current year may be carried forward for distribution in subsequent years.

(2) The Company's Board of Directors shall comprehensively consider factors such as the characteristics of the industry, the stage of development, the Company's own business model, profitability level, and whether there are arrangements for major capital expenditures, and shall distinguish among the following circumstances and propose differentiated cash dividend policies in accordance with the procedures stipulated in the Articles of Association:

  1. If the Company is in a mature stage of development with no arrangements for major capital expenditures, the proportion of cash dividends in this profit distribution shall be at least 80%;
  2. If the Company is in a mature stage of development with arrangements for major capital expenditures, the proportion of cash dividends in this profit distribution shall be at least 40%;
  3. If the Company is in a growth stage of development with arrangements for major capital expenditures, the proportion of cash dividends in this profit distribution shall be at least 20%.

If the Company's stage of development is not easily distinguishable but there are major investment plans or arrangements for major capital expenditures, the provisions of the preceding paragraph may apply.

5. Distribution Interval

On the premise of meeting the conditions for profit distribution and ensuring the Company's normal operations and long-term development, the Company shall, in principle, conduct one profit distribution per year after it is approved by the annual general meeting of shareholders. The Company's Board of Directors may, based on the Company's profitability and capital requirements, propose that the Company conduct interim cash dividends, and submit such proposal to the general meeting of shareholders for review and approval.

6. Decision-making Mechanism and Procedures for Profit Distribution

(1) The Board of Directors shall formulate annual profit distribution plans and interim profit distribution plans, and independent directors shall separately issue clear opinions on the profit distribution plans. Independent directors may solicit opinions from small and medium-sized shareholders, propose dividend proposals, and submit them directly to the Board of Directors for deliberation.

(2) After the Board of Directors reviews and approves the profit distribution plan, it shall be submitted to the general meeting of shareholders for review and approval.

(3) If the Company's Board of Directors has not made a cash profit distribution plan, or the cash profit distribution plan made by the Board of Directors does not comply with the provisions of the Articles of Association, the reasons shall be disclosed in detail in the periodic reports, together with the intended use of funds not used for dividends retained by the Company, and independent directors shall issue independent opinions thereon;

(4) The general meeting of shareholders shall review the profit distribution plan. The Company shall provide online voting and other methods to facilitate shareholder participation in the voting at the general meeting of shareholders. Before shareholders deliberate on the specific cash dividend plan, the Company shall proactively communicate and exchange views with shareholders, especially small and medium-sized shareholders, through multiple channels (including but not limited to shareholder hotlines, fax, email, interactive platforms, etc.), fully listen to the opinions and demands of small and medium-sized shareholders, and promptly respond to issues of concern to small and medium-sized shareholders.

(5) After the general meeting of shareholders makes a resolution on the profit distribution plan, the Company's Board of Directors must complete the profit distribution matters within 2 months after the general meeting of shareholders approves the plan."

(VIII) Commitments Regarding Compensation of Investor Losses in Accordance with the Law

1. Commitments of the Issuer and its Controlling Shareholder, Actual Controller, All Directors, and Senior Management

"(I) Commitments of the Issuer

1. If the Prospectus and other information disclosure materials contain false records, misleading statements, or material omissions, causing investors to suffer losses in the issuance and trading of securities, the Issuer will compensate investors for losses incurred thereby in accordance with the law.

2. If the Issuer violates the above commitments, the Issuer will issue a public apology to shareholders and public investors in the designated media for information disclosure, and will compensate investors for actual losses as determined by the CSRC and relevant judicial authorities in accordance with the law.

(II) Commitments of the Issuer's Controlling Shareholder and Actual Controller

1. If the Prospectus and other information disclosure materials contain false records, misleading statements, or material omissions due to reasons attributable to me, causing investors to suffer losses in the issuance and trading of securities, I will compensate investors for losses incurred thereby in accordance with the law.

2. If I violate the above commitments, I will issue a public apology to shareholders and public investors at the Issuer's general meeting of shareholders and in the designated media for information disclosure, and from the date of such violation, I will cease to receive dividends from the Issuer (if any), and the shares of the Issuer directly or indirectly held by me shall not be transferred, until I have taken and completed the corresponding compensation measures in accordance with the above commitments.

(III) Joint Commitments of All Directors and Senior Management of the Issuer

1. If the Prospectus and other information disclosure materials contain false records, misleading statements, or material omissions due to reasons attributable to me, causing investors to suffer losses in the issuance and trading of securities, I will compensate investors for losses incurred thereby in accordance with the law.

2. If I violate the above commitments, I will issue a public apology to shareholders and public investors at the Issuer's general meeting of shareholders and in the designated media for information disclosure, and from the date of such violation, I will cease to receive salary and dividends from the Issuer (if any), and the shares of the Issuer directly or indirectly held by me shall not be transferred, until I have taken and completed the corresponding compensation measures in accordance with the above commitments."

(IX) Commitments Regarding Performance Decline

1. Commitments of the Controlling Shareholder and Actual Controller Wang Xingxing (王兴兴)

"1. If the Issuer's net profit in the year of listing (based on net profit attributable to the parent company after deducting non-recurring gains and losses, the same below) declines by more than 50% compared to the year prior to listing, the lock-up period for the shares I hold at that time shall be extended by 12 months;

2. If the Issuer's net profit in the second year after listing declines by more than 50% compared to the year prior to listing, the lock-up period for the shares I hold at that time shall be further extended by 12 months on the basis of the preceding item;

3. If the Issuer's net profit in the third year after listing declines by more than 50% compared to the year prior to listing, the lock-up period for the shares I hold at that time shall be further extended by 12 months on the basis of the preceding two items.

The aforesaid 'shares held at that time' refer to the shares acquired by me prior to this Offering and Listing that are still held by me at the time of the annual report disclosure of the Issuer in the year of listing, the second year after listing, and the third year after listing, respectively."

2. Commitments of Shanghai Yuyi (上海宇翼)

"1. If the Issuer's net profit in the year of listing (based on net profit attributable to the parent company after deducting non-recurring gains and losses, the same below) declines by more than 50% compared to the year prior to listing, the lock-up period for the shares held by our enterprise at that time shall be extended by 12 months;

2. If the Issuer's net profit in the second year after listing declines by more than 50% compared to the year prior to listing, the lock-up period for the shares held by our enterprise at that time shall be further extended by 12 months on the basis of the preceding item;

3. If the Issuer's net profit in the third year after listing declines by more than 50% compared to the year prior to listing, the lock-up period for the shares held by our enterprise at that time shall be further extended by 12 months on the basis of the preceding two items.

The aforesaid 'shares held at that time' refer to the shares acquired by our enterprise prior to this Offering and Listing that are still held by our enterprise at the time of the annual report disclosure of the Issuer in the year of listing, the second year after listing, and the third year after listing, respectively."

(X) Commitments of the Issuer Regarding No Cash Dividends During the Review Period

"1. The accumulated undistributed profits of the Company prior to this Offering and Listing shall be shared by both new and existing shareholders after the completion of this Offering and Listing in proportion to their respective shareholdings;

2. From the time the Company applies for an initial public offering of shares and listing on the STAR Market until the completion of the listing (i.e., during the review period), the Company commits not to distribute cash dividends;

3. The above commitments are the genuine expression of the Company's intent. The Company voluntarily accepts the supervision of regulatory authorities, self-regulatory organizations, and the general public.
