If the Company's violation of the above commitments causes losses to investors, the Company will bear corresponding legal liability to investors in accordance with the law."

(XI) Commitments Regarding Reducing and Regulating Related-Party Transactions

1. Commitments of the Controlling Shareholder and Actual Controller Wang Xingxing (王兴兴)

"1. There are no related-party transactions that seriously affect independence or are grossly unfair between me and other enterprises controlled by me on the one hand, and the Company and its controlled subsidiaries on the other hand; I and other enterprises controlled by me will endeavor to minimize and avoid related-party transactions with the Company and its controlled subsidiaries; for related-party transactions that are genuinely necessary and unavoidable, I guarantee that they will be conducted on the basis of fairness, reasonableness, and the principle of equal consideration, that related transaction agreements will be signed in accordance with the law, and that transaction procedures and information disclosure obligations will be fulfilled in accordance with relevant laws, regulations, and normative documents, so as to ensure that related-party transactions are not used to damage the legitimate rights and interests of the Company and other shareholders.

2. During the period when I serve as the Company's controlling shareholder and actual controller, I and other enterprises controlled by me will strictly comply with the Company Law of the People's Republic of China, the Securities Law of the People's Republic of China, and the relevant regulations of the China Securities Regulatory Commission and the Shanghai Stock Exchange regarding the regulation of capital transactions between listed companies and their related parties.

3. I will comply with the provisions of the Company's Articles of Association and Related-Party Transaction Management System, and will not use my position as actual controller to affect the Company's independence, and guarantee not to use related-party transactions to illegally transfer the Company's funds or profits, seek any other improper benefits, or cause the Company to bear any improper obligations.

4. I will strictly fulfill the above commitments. If I violate the above commitments by conducting related-party transactions with the Company and its controlled subsidiaries and thereby cause losses to the Company and its controlled subsidiaries or other shareholders, I am willing to bear liability for such losses.

This letter of commitment shall remain continuously effective during the period when I serve as the Company's controlling shareholder and actual controller."

2. Commitments of Other Directors and Senior Management

"1. There are no related-party transactions that seriously affect independence or are grossly unfair between me and other enterprises controlled by me on the one hand, and the Company and its controlled subsidiaries on the other hand; I and other enterprises controlled by me will endeavor to minimize and avoid related-party transactions with the Company and its controlled subsidiaries; for related-party transactions that are genuinely necessary and unavoidable, I guarantee that they will be conducted on the basis of fairness, reasonableness, and the principle of equal consideration, that related transaction agreements will be signed in accordance with the law, and that transaction procedures and information disclosure obligations will be fulfilled in accordance with relevant laws, regulations, and normative documents, so as to ensure that related-party transactions are not used to damage the legitimate rights and interests of the Company and other shareholders.

2. During the period when I serve as the Company's director and/or senior management, I and other enterprises controlled by me will strictly comply with the Company Law of the People's Republic of China, the Securities Law of the People's Republic of China, and the relevant regulations of the China Securities Regulatory Commission and the Shanghai Stock Exchange regarding the regulation of capital transactions between listed companies and their related parties.

3. I will comply with the provisions of the Company's Articles of Association and Related-Party Transaction Management System, and will not use my position as a director or senior management to affect the Company's independence, and guarantee not to use related-party transactions to illegally transfer the Company's funds or profits, seek any other improper benefits, or cause the Company to bear any improper obligations.

4. I will strictly fulfill the above commitments. If I violate the above commitments by conducting related-party transactions with the Company and its controlled subsidiaries and thereby cause losses to the Company and its controlled subsidiaries or other shareholders, I am willing to bear liability for such losses.

This letter of commitment shall remain continuously effective during the period when I serve as the Company's director and/or senior management."

3. Commitments of Major Shareholders Shanghai Yuyi (上海宇翼), Hanhai Information (汉海信息), Galaxy Z, Chengdu Longzhu (成都龙珠), Jingwei Yi Hao (经纬壹号), and Jingwei San Hao (经纬叁号), Each Holding or Collectively Holding with Concert Parties More Than 5% of the Issuer's Shares

"1. Our enterprise will, to the greatest extent possible, regulate related-party transactions between our enterprise or other enterprises controlled by our enterprise on the one hand, and the Company and its controlled subsidiaries on the other hand.

2. For related-party transactions that are unavoidable or that occur for reasonable cause, our enterprise or other enterprises controlled by our enterprise will conduct such transactions in accordance with relevant laws, regulations, and normative documents and the provisions of the Articles of Association, adhering to the general commercial principles of equality, voluntariness, equivalence, and compensation; will sign related-party transaction agreements with the Company and its controlled subsidiaries; and will ensure that the prices of related-party transactions are fair and, in principle, do not deviate from the prices or fee standards of independent third parties in the market, so as to safeguard the interests of the Company and other shareholders.

3. Our enterprise guarantees not to use its position and influence in the Company to damage the legitimate rights and interests of the Company and other shareholders through related-party transactions. Our enterprise or other enterprises controlled by our enterprise guarantee not to use our enterprise's position and influence in the Company to illegally occupy or transfer the Company's funds, assets, and other resources, or to require the Company to provide guarantees in violation of regulations.

4. This commitment shall take effect on the date when our enterprise affixes its seal and signature, and shall remain valid during the period when the Issuer exists and our enterprise is identified as a related party of the Company in accordance with relevant regulations of the CSRC or securities exchanges."

4. Commitments of Issuer Shareholder Ningbo Hongshan (宁波红杉)

"1. Our enterprise will, to the greatest extent possible, regulate related-party transactions between our enterprise or other enterprises controlled by our enterprise on the one hand, and the Company and its controlled subsidiaries on the other hand.

2. For related-party transactions that are unavoidable or that occur for reasonable cause, our enterprise or other enterprises controlled by our enterprise will promote such transactions to be conducted in accordance with relevant laws, regulations, and normative documents and the provisions of the Articles of Association, adhering to the general commercial principles of equality, voluntariness, equivalence, and compensation; will promote the signing of related-party transaction agreements with the Company and its controlled subsidiaries; and will promote the fairness of related-party transaction prices.

3. Our enterprise guarantees not to use its position and influence in the Company to damage the legitimate rights and interests of the Company and other shareholders through related-party transactions. Our enterprise or other enterprises controlled by our enterprise guarantee not to use our enterprise's position and influence in the Company to illegally occupy or transfer the Company's funds and assets, or to require the Company to provide guarantees in violation of regulations."

5. Commitments of Issuer Shareholder Xiamen Yaheng (厦门雅恒)

"1. Our enterprise will, to the greatest extent possible, regulate related-party transactions between our enterprise or other enterprises controlled by our enterprise on the one hand, and the Company and its controlled subsidiaries on the other hand.

2. For related-party transactions that are unavoidable or that occur for reasonable cause, our enterprise or other enterprises controlled by our enterprise will promote such transactions to be conducted in accordance with relevant laws, regulations, and normative documents and the provisions of the Articles of Association, adhering to the general commercial principles of equality, voluntariness, equivalence, and compensation; will promote the signing of related-party transaction agreements with the Company and its controlled subsidiaries; and will promote the fairness of related-party transaction prices.

3. Our enterprise guarantees not to use its position and influence in the Company to damage the legitimate rights and interests of the Company and other shareholders through related-party transactions. Our enterprise or other enterprises controlled by our enterprise guarantee not to use our enterprise's position and influence in the Company to illegally occupy or transfer the Company's funds and assets, or to require the Company to provide guarantees in violation of regulations."

(XII) Commitments Regarding the Prevention of Fund Occupation and Unauthorized Guarantees

1. Commitments of the Controlling Shareholder and Actual Controller Wang Xingxing (王兴兴)

"1. As of the date of issuance of this commitment, neither I nor my related parties have occupied the funds of the Issuer and its controlled subsidiaries, nor has the Issuer or its controlled subsidiaries provided any guarantees for me or my related parties.

2. I guarantee to exercise shareholder rights in accordance with the law, not to abuse shareholder rights to damage the interests of the Issuer or other shareholders of the Issuer, and neither I nor my related parties will occupy the funds of the Issuer and its controlled subsidiaries in any manner or require the Issuer or its controlled subsidiaries to provide guarantees in violation of laws and regulations.

3. If any gains are obtained due to failure to fulfill the above commitment matters, such gains shall belong to the Issuer; if any losses are caused to the Issuer due to failure to fulfill the above commitment matters, I shall bear liability for compensation to the Issuer.

4. The above commitments shall remain continuously effective during the period when I serve as the Issuer's controlling shareholder and actual controller."

2. Commitments of Other Directors and Senior Management

"1. As of the date of issuance of this commitment, neither I nor my related parties have occupied the funds of the Issuer and its controlled subsidiaries, nor has the Issuer or its controlled subsidiaries provided any guarantees for me or my related parties.

2. I guarantee to exercise director and/or senior management rights in accordance with the law, not to abuse director and/or senior management rights to damage the interests of the Issuer or other shareholders of the Issuer, and neither I nor my related parties will occupy the funds of the Issuer and its controlled subsidiaries in any manner or require the Issuer or its controlled subsidiaries to provide guarantees in violation of laws and regulations.

3. If any gains are obtained due to failure to fulfill the above commitment matters, such gains shall belong to the Issuer; if any losses are caused to the Issuer due to failure to fulfill the above commitment matters, I shall bear liability for compensation to the Issuer.

4. The above commitments shall remain continuously effective during the period when I serve as the Issuer's director and/or senior management."

(XIII) Commitments Regarding Restrictive Measures for Failure to Fulfill Commitment Matters

1. Commitments of the Issuer

"1. Our enterprise will promptly and fully disclose the specific reasons for any failure, inability, or inability to fulfill commitments on time.

2. If the violated commitment is capable of continued performance, our enterprise shall continue to fulfill such commitment. If it cannot be continued, our enterprise will make corrections within the period required by the relevant regulatory authorities or promptly make valid and effective supplementary commitments or alternative commitments.

3. If our enterprise fails to fulfill relevant commitment matters due to reasons other than force majeure and thereby causes losses to investors, our enterprise will bear compensation liability to the Issuer or investors in accordance with the law, in a manner recognized by regulatory authorities or in accordance with judicial rulings."

2. Commitments of the Controlling Shareholder and Actual Controller Wang Xingxing (王兴兴)

"1. I will promptly and fully disclose the specific reasons for any failure, inability, or inability to fulfill commitments on time.

2. If the violated commitment is capable of continued performance, I shall continue to fulfill such commitment. If it cannot be continued, I will make corrections within the period required by the relevant regulatory authorities or promptly make valid and effective supplementary commitments or alternative commitments.

3. If failure to fulfill relevant commitment matters due to reasons other than force majeure causes losses to the Issuer or investors, I will bear compensation liability to the Issuer or investors in accordance with the law, in a manner recognized by regulatory authorities or in accordance with judicial rulings."

3. Commitments of Other Directors, Senior Management, and Core Technical Personnel

"1. I will promptly and fully disclose the specific reasons for any failure, inability, or inability to fulfill commitments on time.

2. If the violated commitment is capable of continued performance, I shall continue to fulfill such commitment. If it cannot be continued, I will make corrections within the period required by the relevant regulatory authorities or promptly make valid and effective supplementary commitments or alternative commitments.

3. If failure to fulfill relevant commitment matters due to reasons other than force majeure causes losses to the Issuer or investors, I will bear compensation liability to the Issuer or investors in accordance with the law, in a manner recognized by regulatory authorities or in accordance with judicial rulings."

4. Commitments of Ningbo Hongshan (宁波红杉) and Xiamen Yaheng (厦门雅恒)

"1. Our enterprise will strictly fulfill the commitment matters signed by our enterprise and disclosed in the prospectus of the Issuer's initial public offering of shares and listing on the STAR Market.

2. If our enterprise fails to fulfill the aforementioned commitment matters, it shall explain to the Issuer in accordance with the law the specific reasons for the failure, inability, or inability to fulfill the commitments on time. If the violated commitment is capable of continued performance, our enterprise shall continue to fulfill such commitment. If it cannot be continued, our enterprise will, in accordance with the law and based on the actual circumstances, promptly make valid and effective supplementary commitments or alternative commitments."

5. Commitments of China Mobile Hechuang (中移和创)

"1. If the violated commitment is capable of continued performance, our enterprise shall continue to fulfill such commitment. If it cannot be continued, our enterprise will make corrections or issue supplementary commitments or alternative commitments in accordance with the law pursuant to written requirements of the regulatory authorities.

2. If failure by our enterprise to fulfill relevant commitment matters results in significant legal consequences (except those caused by force majeure), our enterprise will bear compensation liability in accordance with the law pursuant to written requirements of the regulatory authorities or in accordance with effective judicial rulings."

6. Commitments of Other Shareholders

"1. Our enterprise will promptly and fully disclose the specific reasons for any failure, inability, or inability to fulfill commitments on time.

2. If the violated commitment is capable of continued performance, our enterprise shall continue to fulfill such commitment. If it cannot be continued, our enterprise will make corrections within the period required by the relevant regulatory authorities or promptly make valid and effective supplementary commitments or alternative commitments.

3. If our enterprise fails to fulfill relevant commitment matters due to reasons other than force majeure and thereby causes losses to the Issuer or investors, our enterprise will bear compensation liability to the Issuer or investors in accordance with the law, in a manner recognized by regulatory authorities or in accordance with judicial rulings."

(XIV) Commitments of the Controlling Shareholder and Actual Controller Regarding the Avoidance of Competition in the Same Industry

"1. As of the date of issuance of this letter of commitment, neither I nor any other enterprises controlled by me (other than the Issuer and its controlled subsidiaries, hereinafter referred to as 'Controlled Enterprises') are currently engaged in, in any form, the same or similar businesses that constitute substantive competition with the principal businesses of the Issuer and its controlled subsidiaries.

2. From the date of issuance of this letter of commitment, I and my Controlled Enterprises undertake to take lawful and effective measures and will not engage in any business or activities that constitute substantive competition with the principal businesses of the Issuer and its controlled subsidiaries.

3. This letter of commitment shall take effect from the date of issuance and shall terminate upon the occurrence of any of the following: (1) the proportion of shares directly or indirectly held by me in the Issuer falls below 5% (exclusive); (2) the Issuer's shares cease to be listed on the Shanghai Stock Exchange (except where the Issuer's shares are temporarily suspended from trading for any reason); (3) when the state no longer requires any particular commitment content, the corresponding portion shall automatically terminate."

(XV) Commitments of the Issuer Regarding Shareholder Information Disclosure

"1. The Company has truthfully, accurately, and completely disclosed shareholder information in the prospectus;

2. As of the date of signing this letter of commitment, none of the direct or indirect shareholders of the Company are entities prohibited from holding shares under laws and regulations, and each shareholder, as a share-holding entity, complies with the provisions of Chinese laws and regulations. As of the date of signing this letter of commitment, the Company has no circumstances of nominee shareholding, entrusted shareholding, trust shareholding, or any circumstances of equity disputes or potential disputes;

3. As of the date of signing this letter of commitment, except for the equity relationships already disclosed in the Prospectus, the intermediaries of this Offering (CITIC Securities Co., Ltd. (中信证券股份有限公司), Beijing Deheng Law Offices (北京德恒律师事务所), Rongcheng Certified Public Accountants (Special General Partnership) (容诚会计师事务所（特殊普通合伙）)) and their principals, senior management, and handling personnel do not directly or indirectly hold shares in the Company;

4. As of the date of signing this letter of commitment, no shareholder of the Company has engaged in improper transfer of benefits using the Company's equity;

5. As of the date of signing this letter of commitment, if the Company violates the above commitments, the Company will bear all legal consequences arising therefrom."

(XVI) Letters of Commitment Regarding Waiver of Voting Rights and Share Reduction Arrangements

1. Commitments of Zhongzheng Investment (中证投资)

1. Waiver of Voting Rights

During the period when our enterprise holds shares in the Issuer, our enterprise waives the voting rights corresponding to 1,229,382 shares of the Issuer held by our enterprise (representing 0.3377% of the Issuer's current total share capital), and will not entrust any other party to exercise the voting rights of such shares. If ex-rights or ex-dividend adjustments are made due to the Issuer's distribution of cash dividends, bonus shares, capitalization of capital reserves, issuance of new shares, or other reasons, the above number of shares and shareholding ratio shall be adjusted accordingly.

2. Intentions Regarding Shareholding and Share Reduction

(1) Our enterprise will strictly comply with the provisions of relevant laws, regulations, and normative documents and the lock-up period requirements set out in the commitment documents issued by our enterprise, and will not conduct any share reduction in violation of relevant regulations and share lock-up commitments during the lock-up period.

In order to avoid any potential uncertainty in the application of reduction obligations, during the period when our enterprise holds shares in the Issuer, our enterprise voluntarily combines the shares of the Issuer held by our enterprise with the shares of the Issuer held by Jinshi Chengzhang (金石成长) for calculation, and on this basis, applies the relevant provisions of the Shanghai Stock Exchange Listed Companies Self-Regulatory Guidelines No. 15 — Share Reduction by Shareholders, Directors, and Senior Management (hereinafter referred to as the "Share Reduction Guidelines") and other relevant provisions, including but not limited to: applying the corresponding reduction quotas based on the combined shareholding ratio, complying with the corresponding reduction procedures, and fulfilling the statutory information disclosure obligations, etc.

(2) Conditions for Share Reduction

After the expiration of the lock-up period, and subject to not violating the provisions of relevant laws, regulations, and normative documents and other public commitments made by our enterprise, our enterprise may reduce its shareholding in the Issuer.

(3) Methods of Share Reduction

After the expiration of the lock-up period, our enterprise intends to reduce its holdings of shares issued by the Issuer prior to the initial public offering through methods including but not limited to centralized competitive trading on the secondary market, block trading, and agreement transfer.

3. During the period when our enterprise holds shares in the Issuer, if the laws, regulations, normative documents, policies, and requirements of securities regulatory authorities and securities exchanges regarding share lock-up periods and share reduction change, our enterprise is willing to automatically comply with the changed laws, regulations, normative documents, policies, and requirements of securities regulatory authorities and securities exchanges.

4. The above commitments are the genuine expression of our enterprise's intent. Our enterprise voluntarily accepts the supervision of regulatory authorities, self-regulatory organizations, and the general public. If our enterprise violates the above commitments, it will bear corresponding responsibility.

2. Commitments of Shanghai Yunyang (上海云玚)

1. Waiver of Voting Rights

During the period when our enterprise holds shares in the Issuer, our enterprise waives the voting rights corresponding to 817,236 shares of the Issuer held by our enterprise (representing 0.2245% of the Issuer's current total share capital), and will not entrust any other party to exercise the voting rights of such shares. If ex-rights adjustments are made due to the Issuer's distribution of stock dividends, bonus shares, capitalization of capital reserves, issuance of new shares, or other reasons, the above number of shares and shareholding ratio shall be adjusted accordingly.

2. Intentions Regarding Shareholding and Share Reduction

(1) Our enterprise will strictly comply with the provisions of relevant laws, regulations, and normative documents and the lock-up period requirements set out in the commitment documents issued by our enterprise, and will not conduct any share reduction in violation of relevant regulations and share lock-up commitments during the lock-up period.

During the period when our enterprise holds shares in the Issuer, our enterprise voluntarily combines the shares of the Issuer held by our enterprise with the shares of the Issuer held by Hangzhou Haoyue (杭州灏月) for calculation, and on this basis, applies the relevant provisions of the Shanghai Stock Exchange Listed Companies Self-Regulatory Guidelines No. 15 — Share Reduction by Shareholders, Directors, and Senior Management (hereinafter referred to as the "Share Reduction Guidelines") and other laws, regulations, and normative documents related to share reduction by listed companies on the STAR Market of the Shanghai Stock Exchange, including but not limited to: applying the corresponding reduction quotas based on the combined shareholding ratio, complying with the corresponding reduction procedures, and fulfilling the statutory information disclosure obligations, etc. (if applicable). For the avoidance of doubt, the above combined calculation arrangement applies only to the shares of the Issuer held by our enterprise, and does not involve any other entities and projects invested in, held, or managed by our enterprise (including Ant Group (蚂蚁集团) and its subsidiaries).

(2) Conditions for Share Reduction

After the expiration of the lock-up period, and subject to not violating the provisions of relevant laws, regulations, and normative documents and other public commitments made by our enterprise (if any), our enterprise may reduce its shareholding in the Issuer.

(3) Methods of Share Reduction

After the expiration of the lock-up period, our enterprise intends to reduce its holdings of shares issued by the Issuer prior to the initial public offering through methods including but not limited to centralized competitive trading on the secondary market, block trading, and agreement transfer.

3. During the period when our enterprise holds shares in the Issuer, if the laws, regulations, normative documents, policies, and requirements of securities regulatory authorities and securities exchanges regarding share lock-up periods and share reduction change,

If the laws, regulations, normative documents, policies, and requirements of securities regulatory authorities and the Shanghai Stock Exchange change, this enterprise agrees to automatically apply the amended laws, regulations, normative documents, policies, and requirements of the securities regulatory authorities and the Shanghai Stock Exchange.

4. The above commitments are a true expression of this enterprise's intentions, and this enterprise voluntarily accepts supervision by regulatory authorities, self-regulatory organizations, and the general public. If this enterprise violates the above commitments, it shall bear corresponding legal liability in accordance with the law.

Annex II: Arrangements for Implementing Relevant Provisions on Investor Relations Management, Dividend Distribution Decision-Making Procedures, and the Establishment of Shareholder Voting Mechanisms

(I) Arrangements for Implementing Relevant Provisions on Investor Relations Management

1. Information Disclosure System and Procedures

In accordance with the Company Law, the Securities Law, the Administrative Measures for Information Disclosure of Listed Companies, the Shanghai Stock Exchange Science and Technology Innovation Board Stock Listing Rules, and other laws and regulations, the Company has formulated the Information Disclosure Affairs Management System. The Information Disclosure Affairs Management System sets forth clear provisions regarding the principles and general requirements for information disclosure, the content of information disclosure, and the procedures and confidentiality measures for information disclosure.

2. Investor Communication Channels

In order to further standardize and strengthen communication between the Company after listing and investors and potential investors, promote investors' understanding and awareness of the Company, reinforce positive interactive relationships between the Company and investors, enhance the Company's image, improve corporate governance structure, foster a corporate culture of good investor returns, and effectively protect investors' interests, the Company has formulated the Investor Relations Management System. The Company's Board Secretary is responsible for organizing and coordinating investor relations management work, and the Board of Directors Office serves as the standing body for investor relations management.

3. Future Plans for Investor Relations Activities

After listing, the Company will strictly comply with the requirements of the Company Law, the Securities Law, the Shanghai Stock Exchange Science and Technology Innovation Board Stock Listing Rules, and other relevant laws and regulations as well as the Articles of Association (Draft), diligently fulfill its information disclosure obligations, ensure that information disclosed is true, accurate, and complete, and continuously improve the Company's level of standardized operations and transparency.

The Company's Board Secretary is responsible for information disclosure affairs and investor relations work, including liaising with the China Securities Regulatory Commission and its dispatched offices, securities exchanges, relevant securities business institutions, and media organizations. Through information disclosure and communication, the Company will strengthen communication with investors and potential investors, deepen investors' understanding and recognition of the Company, and elevate the level of corporate governance.

(II) Dividend Distribution Decision-Making Procedures

For the Company's dividend distribution decision-making procedures, please refer to "Section 9 Investor Protection," sub-section "II. (III) 6. Decision-Making Mechanism and Procedures for Profit Distribution" of this Prospectus.

(III) Establishment of Shareholder Voting Mechanisms

The Company has refined shareholder voting mechanisms through the Articles of Association (Draft), the Rules of Procedure for Shareholders' Meetings, the Implementation Rules for the Cumulative Voting System, and other systems, including the establishment of cumulative voting for the election of the Company's directors, separate vote counting for small and medium investors, and arrangements for holding shareholders' meetings via online voting for statutory matters and for soliciting voting rights.

1. Cumulative Voting System

When electing two or more directors at a single shareholders' meeting, the Company shall adopt the cumulative voting system to ensure that small and medium shareholders of the Company have the opportunity to elect director candidates who represent their interests and opinions into the Board of Directors. The Company shall clearly indicate in the meeting notice for the shareholders' meeting whether the cumulative voting system will be used in such director election.

Principles for the election of directors: (1) The composition of directors elected at a shareholders' meeting shall comply with the provisions of the Articles of Association. Whether director candidates are elected shall be determined by the number of votes received, but the number of votes received by each elected director must exceed one-half of the total number of voting shares held by shareholders attending the shareholders' meeting. (2) If the number of director candidates elected at the shareholders' meeting exceeds the number to be elected, candidates shall be ranked by number of votes received, and those with more votes shall be elected. If the number of elected directors is less than the number to be elected, but the number of directors already elected reaches or exceeds two-thirds of the total number of board members specified in the Articles of Association, the vacancies shall be filled at the next shareholders' meeting. If the number of elected directors is less than the number to be elected and is also less than two-thirds of the total number of board members specified in the Articles of Association, a shareholders' meeting to elect the vacant directors shall be convened within two months after the conclusion of the current shareholders' meeting.

2. Separate Vote Counting System for Small and Medium Investors

When a shareholders' meeting deliberates on significant matters affecting the interests of small and medium investors, votes of small and medium investors shall be counted separately, and the results of the separate vote count shall be publicly disclosed in a timely manner.

3. Online Voting Mechanism

Shareholders' meetings shall in principle be held in person at a designated venue and may also, in accordance with laws, administrative regulations, departmental rules, or the Articles of Association (Draft), provide online voting as a means of convenience for shareholders.

The same voting right may only be exercised through one method — in-person, online, or other voting methods. In the event of duplicate voting of the same voting right, the first vote cast shall prevail.

The in-person session of a shareholders' meeting shall not close earlier than the online or other voting methods. The meeting chairperson shall announce the voting status and results of each proposal and shall announce whether each proposal is approved based on the voting results.

Prior to the official announcement of voting results, all parties involved in the in-person, online, and other voting methods at the shareholders' meeting — including the Company, vote counters, scrutineers, shareholders, and online service providers — are obligated to maintain confidentiality regarding the voting status.

4. Solicitation of Voting Rights

The Company's Board of Directors, independent directors, shareholders holding more than one percent of shares with voting rights, or investor protection institutions established in accordance with laws, administrative regulations, or the requirements of the China Securities Regulatory Commission may publicly solicit shareholders' voting rights. The solicitation of shareholders' voting rights shall fully disclose to the solicited persons information such as specific voting intentions. Solicitation of shareholders' voting rights through compensated or disguised compensated means is prohibited. Except under statutory conditions, the Company may not impose minimum shareholding ratio requirements on the solicitation of voting rights.

Annex III: Description of the Establishment, Improvement, and Operation of the Shareholders' Meeting, Board of Directors, Independent Directors, and Board Secretary Systems

In accordance with the requirements of relevant laws, regulations, and normative documents such as the Company Law, the Securities Law, and the Guidelines on Corporate Governance of Listed Companies, as well as the Articles of Association, the Company has established and improved the shareholders' meeting, Board of Directors, independent directors, Board Secretary, and Board of Directors special committee systems including the Audit Committee. The Company convenes shareholders' meetings and Board of Directors meetings in accordance with relevant laws, regulations, normative documents, and the Articles of Association. Relevant shareholders and directors attend each shareholders' meeting and Board of Directors meeting in accordance with the Articles of Association. The convening of shareholders' meetings and Board of Directors meetings and the content of their resolutions are lawful and valid, with no instances of exercising authority in violation of the Company Law, the Articles of Association, or other provisions.

(I) Establishment, Improvement, and Operation of the Shareholders' Meeting System

In accordance with the Company Law and relevant provisions, the Company has formulated the Articles of Association and the Rules of Procedure for Shareholders' Meetings. The Articles of Association and the Rules of Procedure for Shareholders' Meetings set forth clear provisions regarding the convening, proposals and notices, format, conditions for convening, and voting methods of the Company's shareholders' meetings. The shareholders' meeting complies with the provisions of the Articles of Association, the Rules of Procedure for Shareholders' Meetings, and relevant laws and regulations in terms of meeting procedures, voting methods, and resolution content, with no instances of exercising authority in violation of the Company Law or other provisions.

(II) Establishment, Improvement, and Operation of the Board of Directors System

The Company has established a Board of Directors that is accountable to the shareholders' meeting. In accordance with relevant laws, regulations, and the Articles of Association, the Company has formulated the Rules of Procedure for the Board of Directors and established a relatively complete Board of Directors system. The Board of Directors consists of 9 directors, with 1 chairperson of the Board of Directors. The chairperson is elected by the Board of Directors by a majority of all directors. The Board of Directors complies with the provisions of the Articles of Association, the Rules of Procedure for the Board of Directors, and relevant laws and regulations in terms of convening methods, meeting procedures, voting methods, and resolution content, with no instances of exercising authority in violation of the Company Law or other provisions.

(III) Establishment, Improvement, and Operation of the Independent Directors System

In order to improve the structure of the Company's Board of Directors and strengthen the decision-making functions of the Board, the Company has formulated the Independent Directors Working System in accordance with the relevant provisions of the Company Law and the Articles of Association, setting forth detailed provisions regarding the qualifications, selection procedures, term of office, powers and duties, and matters on which independent directors are required to express independent opinions.

As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, among the Company's 9 board members, there are 3 independent directors, including 2 accounting professionals, accounting for no less than one-third of the total number of board members. Since their appointment, the Company's independent directors have strictly fulfilled their duties in accordance with the requirements of the Articles of Association and the Independent Directors Working System and related documents, attended each Board of Directors meeting on time, provided professional and constructive opinions on the Company's major decisions, conscientiously supervised management's work, and played an active role in promoting the Company's standardized operations in accordance with its corporate governance structure. As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, the independent directors have not raised any objections to any board resolutions or related decision-making matters.

(IV) Establishment, Improvement, and Operation of the Board Secretary System

In accordance with relevant laws, regulations, and the Articles of Association, the Company has formulated the Board Secretary Working System and appointed one Board Secretary, who serves as a senior management member of the Company and is accountable to the Board of Directors. Since assuming office, the Board Secretary has strictly prepared Board of Directors and shareholders' meeting meetings in accordance with the relevant provisions of the Articles of Association and the Board Secretary Working System, diligently fulfilled various duties, ensured the lawful convening of the Company's Board of Directors and shareholders' meetings, and played a positive role in the Company's operations.

Annex IV: Description of the Establishment of the Audit Committee and Other Special Committees

The Company's Board of Directors has established four special committees: Audit, Nomination, Remuneration and Assessment, and Strategy. All members of the special committees are composed of directors. Among them, independent directors form the majority of and serve as the convenors of the Audit, Nomination, and Remuneration and Assessment Committees. The independent director serving as convenor of the Audit Committee is an accounting professional. The composition of each special committee is as follows:

Committee Name Convenor Other Members
Audit Committee Li Zongyan (李宗彦) Ni Chenkai (倪晨凯), Zhang Yangguang (张阳光)
Nomination Committee Ni Chenkai (倪晨凯) Song Huasheng (宋华盛), Wang Xingxing (王兴兴)
Remuneration and Assessment Committee Song Huasheng (宋华盛) Li Zongyan (李宗彦), Wang Xingxing (王兴兴)
Strategy Committee Wang Xingxing (王兴兴) Zhang Yangguang (张阳光), Yang Zhiyu (杨知雨)

The Company has formulated the Audit Committee Working System, the Nomination Committee Working System, the Remuneration and Assessment Committee Working System, the Strategy Committee Working System, and other related systems, setting forth provisions regarding the composition, responsibilities and authorities, and rules of procedure of the Audit, Nomination, Remuneration and Assessment, and Strategy Committees. Since the establishment of each special committee of the Board of Directors, each member has effectively fulfilled their duties and ensured the Company's standardized operations.

No. Investment Component Investment Amount Proportion of Investment
1 Equipment Procurement and Installation 5,145.00 11.55%
2 R&D Personnel Investment 22,795.00 51.18%
3 R&D Implementation Expenses 16,600.00 37.27%
Total Project Investment 44,540.00 100.00%

(1) Site Investment

This project does not involve site investment and will share the R&D center building with the "Robot Body R&D Project."

(2) Equipment Procurement and Installation

The equipment procurement and installation cost for this project is RMB 51,450,000 (5,145.00 万元), primarily comprising design, trial-manufacture, and testing equipment required for the development of new intelligent robot products, such as industry-grade verification platforms, mechanical performance test benches, gradient test benches, and airtightness test benches.

(3) R&D Personnel Investment

The R&D personnel investment for this project is RMB 227,950,000 (22,795.00 万元), primarily covering 24 categories of personnel during the project construction period, including industrial design engineers, robot testing engineers, and navigation application engineers. Employment costs are referenced against the average levels of these positions during the reporting period.

(4) R&D Implementation Expenses

The R&D implementation expenses for this project are RMB 166,000,000 (16,600.00 万元), primarily comprising expert consultant fees, R&D material costs, technical service fees, patent service fees, and other implementation expenses.

3. Project Construction Plan

The construction period for this project is estimated to be 36 months. The construction plan is as follows:

Construction Content T+1 T+2 T+3
Q1Q2Q3Q4 Q1Q2Q3Q4 Q1Q2Q3Q4
Preliminary Preparatory Work
Equipment Inquiry, Procurement, Installation and Commissioning
R&D Personnel Recruitment and Training
Relevant Technology Research and Development

4. Project Site Selection and Land Use

The proposed construction site for this project is located in the Hangzhou High-Tech Industry Development Zone (Binjiang). The Company has obtained the Letter on Ensuring Development Space for Hangzhou Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (杭州宇树科技股份有限公司) issued by the Economy and Information Technology Bureau of Hangzhou High-Tech Development Zone (Binjiang), ensuring that the necessary space will be provided for the Company's development so as to avoid any material adverse impact on the substantive implementation and overall progress of the project.

5. Project Filing Procedures Relating to the Use of Raised Funds

This project has been filed with the Development and Reform Bureau of Hangzhou High-Tech Development Zone (Binjiang), and the Notice of Filing for Enterprise Investment Projects in Hangzhou High-Tech Zone (Binjiang) (Reference No.: 滨发改金融【2025】010) has been obtained.

6. Environmental Protection Status of the Project

Pursuant to the Classified Management Catalogue for Environmental Impact Assessment of Construction Projects (2021 Edition) issued by the Ministry of Ecology and Environment of the People's Republic of China, this project does not fall within the scope requiring the preparation of an environmental impact report, an environmental impact form, or the filing of an environmental impact registration form for construction projects.

(IV) Intelligent Robot Manufacturing Base Construction Project

1. Project Overview

Through this project, the Company proposes to construct a proprietary production base and other related supporting infrastructure, procure advanced production and inspection equipment, improve the production environment, optimize the production layout, enhance production management efficiency, comprehensively expand the production and supply capacity of its principal products including humanoid and quadruped robots, reinforce its first-mover and scale advantages, and lay an important capacity foundation for the Company's future business expansion.

The implementing entity of this project is Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (宇树科技股份有限公司), with a construction period of 2 years. The total project investment is RMB 624,113,900 (62,411.39 万元), comprising site investment, equipment procurement and installation, basic contingency reserves, and working capital seed funding and other necessary inputs. The project will benefit the Company in seizing the development opportunities in the general-purpose robotics industry, strengthening its global market competitiveness, and further consolidating the Company's industry position.

2. Project Investment Details

The estimated total investment for this project is RMB 624,113,900 (62,411.39 万元), with proposed use of raised funds of RMB 624,113,900 (62,411.39 万元). The specific breakdown is as follows:

Unit: RMB 10,000

No. Investment Component Investment Amount Proportion of Investment
1 Site Investment 30,922.37 49.55%
2 Equipment Procurement and Installation 20,898.00 33.48%
3 Basic Contingency Reserve 2,591.02 4.15%
4 Working Capital Seed Funding 8,000.00 12.82%
Total Project Investment 62,411.39 100%

(1) Site Investment

The site investment for this project specifically comprises expenditures for acquiring the intelligent robot manufacturing base. The acquisition cost of the manufacturing base is calculated based on cost estimates from the design firm. Pursuant to the plan, the site investment amount for this project is RMB 309,223,700 (30,922.37 万元), with a total construction area of 78,690.00 square meters.

(2) Equipment Procurement and Installation

The equipment procurement and installation cost for this project is RMB 208,980,000 (20,898.00 万元), primarily comprising production equipment, inspection equipment, and intelligent warehousing equipment required for the construction of the intelligent robot production line.

(3) Basic Contingency Reserve

The basic contingency reserve for this project is RMB 25,910,200 (2,591.02 万元), calculated at 5% of the total site investment and equipment procurement and installation costs.

(4) Working Capital Seed Funding

Working capital seed funding refers to the working capital necessary to ensure the normal operations of the project in its initial stage and the smooth production of products. The planned working capital seed funding for this project is RMB 80,000,000 (8,000.00 万元).

3. Project Construction Plan

The construction period for this project is estimated to be 24 months. The construction schedule is as follows:

Construction Content T+1 T+2
Q1Q2Q3Q4 Q1Q2Q3Q4
Preliminary Engineering Work
Site Acquisition and Renovation Works
Equipment Inquiry, Procurement, Installation and Commissioning
Production Line Trial Run
Completion Acceptance

4. Project Site Selection and Land Use

The proposed construction site for this project is located in the Hangzhou High-Tech Industry Development Zone (Binjiang). The Company has obtained the Letter on Ensuring Development Space for Hangzhou Unitree Technology Co., Ltd. (杭州宇树科技股份有限公司) issued by the Economy and Information Technology Bureau of Hangzhou High-Tech Development Zone (Binjiang), ensuring that the necessary space will be provided for the Company's development so as to avoid any material adverse impact on the substantive implementation and overall progress of the project.

5. Project Filing Procedures Relating to the Use of Raised Funds

This project has been filed with the Development and Reform Bureau of Hangzhou High-Tech Development Zone (Binjiang), and the Notice of Filing for Enterprise Investment Projects in Hangzhou High-Tech Zone (Binjiang) (Reference No.: 滨发改金融【2025】012) has been obtained.

6. Environmental Protection Status of the Project

This project has been filed with the Hangzhou Municipal Bureau of Ecology and Environment, and the Acceptance Letter for the Commitment-Based Filing of Environmental Impact Assessment Documents for Construction Projects under the "Planning Environmental Assessment + Environmental Standards" List-Based Management Reform Pilot (杭环滨备【2025】No. 27) has been obtained.
